radiology_facts.report_text:
CTA CHEST

DATE:  ___.

COMPARISON:  ___.

CLINICAL INDICATION:  ___ female with faintness and ischemic lesion in
cecum.  Question PE.

TECHNIQUE:  Unenhanced axial images were obtained of the chest using a
low-dose protocol.  Subsequently, images were obtained after the uneventful
administration of 100 mL Omnipaque intravenous contrast.  Coronal and sagittal
and oblique reformatted images were constructed.
Total Exam DLP: 344.9 mGy-cm.

FINDINGS:  

There is no pulmonary embolus.  The aorta and great vessels are within normal
limits.  There is no axillary, mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy.  The
heart is normal in size.  There is no pericardial or pleural effusion.  Tiny
calcified granuloma is present at the right base (2:46).  A few additional 1-2
mm pulmonary nodules are not definitely calcified in the right upper lobe
(2:21), lingula (2:27), right lower lobe (2:34).  The airways are patent.  A
calcified lymph node is present in the right paratracheal region.

There are no destructive osseous lesions.  

IMPRESSION:

1.  No pulmonary embolus.
2.  There are four, less than 4 mm pulmonary nodules, at least one of which is
calcified with additional finding of calcified lymph node in the right
paratracheal region.  These may relate to sequelae of prior granulomatous
disease.  In a low-risk patient no followup is needed.  In a high-risk
patient, a followup CT at 12 months may be obtained and if unchanged, no
further followup would be necessary.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  Rectal cancer, status post resection and chemotherapy, followup.

COMPARISON:  ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Axial MDCT images were obtained from the lung apices to the pubic
symphysis after the intravenous administration of Optiray.  Coronal and
sagittal reformatted images are provided.

CONTRAST:  Oral and intravenous nonionic contrast.

CT OF THE CHEST WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST:  There are no pathologically
enlarged mediastinal, hilar, or axillary lymph nodes.  A left-sided pacemaker
is again seen, along with coronary artery calcifications.  Prominence of the
central pulmonary arteries suggestive of pulmonary arterial hypertension, is
an unchanged finding.  The central airways are patent.  No definite pulmonary
nodules or masses are identified.  Subpleural nodularity at the right lung
apex (3:12) is unchanged.  There are no pleural effusions and no pneumothorax.
There is minimal dependent atelectasis in the lower lobes bilaterally.

CT OF THE ABDOMEN WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST:  In segment III of the liver, a
6-mm enhancing nodule is unchanged and could represent a hemangioma, although
is incompletely characterized.  In segment VI, a 1-cm oval hypodensity is
unchanged (3:58).  Tiny 4-mm hypodensity in segment II (3:59) is unchanged. No
new hepatic lesions are identified.  The portal vein is patent and there is no
biliary dilation.  A 2.5-cm gallstone is again seen without evidence of
gallbladder distention.  There is fatty infiltration of the pancreas.  The
spleen and adrenal glands appear unremarkable.  Tiny bilateral hypodense renal
lesions, too small to characterize, are unchanged.  An area of scarring in the
interpolar region of the left kidney (3:69), is unchanged.  In the upper pole
of the left kidney, a 9.5-mm hypodense lesion with a tiny dot of dependent
density (3:57) has increased in size by approximately 1 mm since ___, and cannot be completely characterize due to its small size.  There are
no pathologically enlarged mesenteric or retroperitoneal lymph nodes.  The
large and small bowel loops are normal in caliber.  There is a parastomal
hernia containing small bowel just adjacent to the left lower quadrant
colostomy without evidence of bowel obstruction.  The rectus diastasis and
fat-containing anterior abdominal wall hernia is unchanged.

CT OF THE PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST:  The patient is status post
abdominoperineal resection.  Slight stranding in the presacral space with
minimal nodularity (3:100) is an unchanged finding and could represent
postoperative change.  There is stable enlargement of the prostate gland which
measures 6.4 x 5.7 cm.  The bladder and distal ureters appear unremarkable.
There is a small fat-containing left inguinal hernia.

BONE WINDOWS:  There is marked degenerative change of the cervicothoracic
junction.  A mid thoracic vertebral body hemangioma is again noted.  A T12
vertebral body hemangioma and L3 hemangioma are again noted, and wedge
configuration of the T12 vertebra is an unchanged finding.  No lesions
worrisome for osseous metastatic disease are identified.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Status post abdominoperineal resection of the rectum without evidence of
metastatic disease.  The degree of presacral stranding, which could represent
post-treatment change, is stable, although continued attention to this region
on followup imaging is recommended.

2.  Left upper pole renal lesion minimally increased in size since ___ is too
small to characterize, but could represent a cyst with debris.  Continued
attention to this region on followup imaging is recommended.

3.  Unchanged parastomal hernia without bowel obstruction.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  Near syncope and elevated D-dimer.  Assess for PE.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT-acquired axial images were obtained through the lungs in a
low-dose non-contrast fashion.  Subsequent attempt to perform CT angiogram was
unsuccessful due to insufficient IV access.  Attempt was made to recall the
patient; however, the patient was discharged against medical advice.

COMPARISONS:  None available.

FINDINGS:  There is no axillary, supraclavicular or hilar adenopathy.  A 10-mm
subcarinal node is noted.  The esophagus is normal.  Heart and pericardium are
normal, without pericardial effusion.  

The trachea and central airways are patent to the segmental level.  Extensive
paraseptal and centrilobular emphysema is noted.  A 4-mm right lower lobe
nodule is seen (2:40).  There is no pleural effusion or pneumothorax. 
Assessment for PE is not possible in the absence of intravenous contrast.  

Though this study is not tailored for subdiaphragmatic evaluation, limited
view of the upper abdomen demonstrates multiple hepatic hypodensities, several
of which are too small to be fully characterized, the largest three of which
may be simple cysts.  Slight nodular contour to the liver suggests cirrhosis.
Gallbladder is surgically absent.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  There is no lytic or sclerotic bone lesion concerning for
osseous malignant process.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Limited study, both due to the absence of intravenous contrast as well as
a low-dose technique as described above.
2.  Moderate-to-severe emphysema with 4-mm right lower lobe nodule, for which
followup evaluation in one year's time is recommended.
3.  Hepatic hypodensities, which are too small to be characterized, some of
which are simple cysts.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CHEST CTA

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with metastatic colon cancer to lungs/liver, w/
sinus tachycardia and tachypnea.  Concern for pneumonia versus pulmonary
embolism.

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of 100 cc of Omnipaque intravenous
contrast.
Reformatted coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal
intensity projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  DLP: 699 mGy-cm

COMPARISON:  CT chest from ___

FINDINGS: 

The aorta is major branches opacify normally without evidence of dissection,
intramural hematoma, or penetrating atherosclerotic ulcer.  Motion artifact
limits evaluation of some the segmental pulmonary arteries, however there is
no central, lobar, or segmental pulmonary embolism.

There is diffuse supraclavicular, axillary, mediastinal, hilar,
paraesophageal, and upper retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy in kidney with known
metastatic disease.

Since the prior study from ___, bilateral nonhemorrhagic pleural
effusions have enlarged, moderate to large on the right and small to moderate
on the left.

The pulmonary parenchyma demonstrates innumerable metastases, which appear to
have increased in number and size since the prior CT from ___.

The esophagus is partially fluid filled and demonstrates multiple hyperdense
nodules within the distal ___ of the esophagus, which may represent
intraluminal metastases versus enteric contents. Limited images of the upper
abdomen demonstrate diffuse intrahepatic metastases, nodularity of the adrenal
glands, ascites, and significant abdominal lymphadenopathy.

Healed left lower posterior rib fractures are noted. No concerning osseous
lesions are seen.

IMPRESSION: 

1. Partially limited evaluation of the subsegmental pulmonary arteries,
however no evidence of central, lobar, or segmental pulmonary embolism.
2. Since ___, increase in size and number of innumerable pulmonary
metastases, as well as enlargement of bilateral pleural effusions, large on
the right and moderate on the left.
3. Partially imaged upper abdomen demonstrates diffuse intrahepatic metastasis
and considerable upper abdominal lymphadenopathy.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  2

INDICATION:  ___ with Hx of Aflutter, recent diagnosis of poorly
differentiated neuroendocrine tumor and airway compromise thought to be
secondary to SVC syndrome transferred from BRICU for airway monitoring and
management now s/p XRT but with new fevers and hypotension.  // Eval for tumor
shrinking, e/o infection, necrosis of tumor.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector CT images of the abdomen and pelvis were acquired
without intravenous contrast. Non-contrast scan has several limitations in
detecting vascular and parenchymal organ abnormalities, including tumor
detection.
Oral contrast was not administered.
Coronal and sagittal reformations were performed and reviewed on PACS.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 11.7 s, 75.9 cm; CTDIvol = 15.6 mGy (Body) DLP =
1,173.3 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 1,173 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND
PELVIS W/O CONTRAST); Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 11.7 s, 75.9 cm; CTDIvol = 15.6 mGy (Body) DLP =
1,173.3 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 1,173 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  Multiple prior CT chest ___ and CT abdomen pelvis ___

FINDINGS: 

CHEST:
NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE: The imaged thyroid is normal. Infraclavicular
lymphadenopathy.

MEDIASTINUM: Extensive mediastinal conglomerate of lymphadenopathy involving
the paratracheal, subcarinal, and prevascular stations for example are grossly
stable in size compared to CT chest from ___.

HILA: Bilateral hilar lymphadenopathy, right greater than left.

HEART and PERICARDIUM:  Limited evaluation the heart and pericardium due to
lack of contrast.  The aorta and main pulmonary artery are normal in size. No
incidental central pulmonary arterial filling defect identified. Heart size is
normal with severe coronary artery calcifications. There is no pericardial
effusion.

PLEURA: Trace right and moderate left pleural effusions.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS:  Mass effect on the left mainstem and lower lobe bronchi. Near
complete compressive atelectasis of the superior left upper and posterior left
lower lobes. Patchy, bilateral infiltrative, consolidative, and nodular
airspace opacities.

CHEST CAGE/BONES:  No focal lytic or sclerotic lesion concerning for
malignancy. Mild multilevel degenerative changes of the thoracic spine are
unchanged.  Median sternotomy wires are present.


ABDOMEN:

HEPATOBILIARY: The liver demonstrates homogeneous attenuation throughout. 
There is no evidence of focal lesions within the limitations of an unenhanced
scan.  There is no evidence of intrahepatic or extrahepatic biliary
dilatation.  The gallbladder contains gallstones without wall thickening or
evidence of inflammation.

PANCREAS: The pancreas has normal attenuation throughout, without evidence of
focal lesions within the limitations of an unenhanced scan.  There is no
pancreatic ductal dilatation.  There is no peripancreatic stranding.

SPLEEN: The spleen shows normal size and attenuation throughout, without
evidence of focal lesions.

ADRENALS: Right adrenal gland contains a 2 cm heterogeneously hypodense mass
with an average Hounsfield unit of 25.

URINARY: The kidneys are of normal and symmetric size.  There is no evidence
of focal renal lesions within the limitations of an unenhanced scan.  There is
no hydronephrosis.  Nonobstructing punctate nephrolithiasis bilaterally. 
There is no perinephric abnormality.

GASTROINTESTINAL:  Enteric tube core courses into the proximal stomach.  The
stomach is unremarkable.  Small bowel loops demonstrate normal caliber and
wall thickness throughout.  The colon and rectum are within normal limits. 
The appendix is not visualized.  A rectal tube is in appropriate position.

PELVIS: The urinary bladder and distal ureters are unremarkable.  A Foley is
in place within the bladder.  Mild ascites including pericapsular round the
liver, in the bilateral pericolic gutters, and in the dependent portion the
pelvis.

REPRODUCTIVE ORGANS: Penile implant.

LYMPH NODES: Extensive retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy and prominent
subcentimeter mesenteric lymph nodes.  For example, a periaortic node measures
1.9 cm in short axis (12:89).  Additional lymphadenopathy is visualized in the
gastrohepatic, paracaval, retro-aortic, celiac, superior mesenteric, and
inferior mesenteric stations.  Additionally, there is stable ___
lymphadenopathy.

VASCULAR: There is no abdominal aortic aneurysm.  Moderate atherosclerotic
disease is noted.  Upper extremity PICC line is noted.

BONES: There is no evidence of worrisome osseous lesions or acute fracture.

SOFT TISSUES: Mild anasarca.

IMPRESSION:


1. Right greater than left infiltrative and nodular consolidative opacities in
the lungs may represent infection versus less likely metastatic spread of
disease.
2. SVC syndrome cannot be evaluated due to the lack of IV contrast.
3. Grossly stable in size chest and abdominopelvic lymphadenopathy.
4. Cholelithiasis without cholecystitis.
5. Right adrenal mass can be further evaluated with a nonemergent CT adrenal
mass protocol.
 This preliminary report was reviewed with Dr. ___
radiologist.

sections.indication:
___ with Hx of Aflutter, recent diagnosis of poorly
differentiated neuroendocrine tumor and airway compromise thought to be
secondary to SVC syndrome transferred from BRICU for airway monitoring and
management now s/p XRT but with new fevers and hypotension.  // Eval for tumor

sections.technique:
Multidetector CT images of the abdomen and pelvis were acquired
without intravenous contrast. Non-contrast scan has several limitations in
detecting vascular and parenchymal organ abnormalities, including tumor
detection.
Oral contrast was not administered.
Coronal and sagittal reformations were performed and reviewed on PACS.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 11.7 s, 75.9 cm; CTDIvol = 15.6 mGy (Body) DLP =
1,173.3 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 1,173 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND

1,173.3 mGy-cm.

sections.comparison: Multiple prior CT chest ___ and CT abdomen pelvis ___
sections.findings:
CHEST:
NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE: The imaged thyroid is normal. Infraclavicular
lymphadenopathy.

MEDIASTINUM: Extensive mediastinal conglomerate of lymphadenopathy involving
the paratracheal, subcarinal, and prevascular stations for example are grossly
stable in size compared to CT chest from ___.

HILA: Bilateral hilar lymphadenopathy, right greater than left.

HEART and PERICARDIUM:  Limited evaluation the heart and pericardium due to
lack of contrast.  The aorta and main pulmonary artery are normal in size. No
incidental central pulmonary arterial filling defect identified. Heart size is
normal with severe coronary artery calcifications. There is no pericardial
effusion.

PLEURA: Trace right and moderate left pleural effusions.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS:  Mass effect on the left mainstem and lower lobe bronchi. Near
complete compressive atelectasis of the superior left upper and posterior left
lower lobes. Patchy, bilateral infiltrative, consolidative, and nodular
airspace opacities.

CHEST CAGE/BONES:  No focal lytic or sclerotic lesion concerning for
malignancy. Mild multilevel degenerative changes of the thoracic spine are
unchanged.  Median sternotomy wires are present.


ABDOMEN:

HEPATOBILIARY: The liver demonstrates homogeneous attenuation throughout. 
There is no evidence of focal lesions within the limitations of an unenhanced
scan.  There is no evidence of intrahepatic or extrahepatic biliary
dilatation.  The gallbladder contains gallstones without wall thickening or
evidence of inflammation.

PANCREAS: The pancreas has normal attenuation throughout, without evidence of
focal lesions within the limitations of an unenhanced scan.  There is no
pancreatic ductal dilatation.  There is no peripancreatic stranding.

SPLEEN: The spleen shows normal size and attenuation throughout, without
evidence of focal lesions.

ADRENALS: Right adrenal gland contains a 2 cm heterogeneously hypodense mass
with an average Hounsfield unit of 25.

URINARY: The kidneys are of normal and symmetric size.  There is no evidence
of focal renal lesions within the limitations of an unenhanced scan.  There is
no hydronephrosis.  Nonobstructing punctate nephrolithiasis bilaterally. 
There is no perinephric abnormality.

GASTROINTESTINAL:  Enteric tube core courses into the proximal stomach.  The
stomach is unremarkable.  Small bowel loops demonstrate normal caliber and
wall thickness throughout.  The colon and rectum are within normal limits. 
The appendix is not visualized.  A rectal tube is in appropriate position.

PELVIS: The urinary bladder and distal ureters are unremarkable.  A Foley is
in place within the bladder.  Mild ascites including pericapsular round the
liver, in the bilateral pericolic gutters, and in the dependent portion the
pelvis.

REPRODUCTIVE ORGANS: Penile implant.

LYMPH NODES: Extensive retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy and prominent
subcentimeter mesenteric lymph nodes.  For example, a periaortic node measures
1.9 cm in short axis (12:89).  Additional lymphadenopathy is visualized in the
gastrohepatic, paracaval, retro-aortic, celiac, superior mesenteric, and
inferior mesenteric stations.  Additionally, there is stable ___
lymphadenopathy.

VASCULAR: There is no abdominal aortic aneurysm.  Moderate atherosclerotic
disease is noted.  Upper extremity PICC line is noted.

BONES: There is no evidence of worrisome osseous lesions or acute fracture.

SOFT TISSUES: Mild anasarca.
sections.impression:
1. Right greater than left infiltrative and nodular consolidative opacities in
the lungs may represent infection versus less likely metastatic spread of
disease.
2. SVC syndrome cannot be evaluated due to the lack of IV contrast.
3. Grossly stable in size chest and abdominopelvic lymphadenopathy.
4. Cholelithiasis without cholecystitis.
5. Right adrenal mass can be further evaluated with a nonemergent CT adrenal
mass protocol.
 This preliminary report was reviewed with Dr. ___
radiologist.

EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with localized pancreatic cancer  // r/o
metastases

TECHNIQUE:  MULTI DETECTOR HELICAL SCANNING OF THE CHEST WAS COORDINATED WITH
INTRAVENOUS INFUSION OF NONIONIC CONTRAST AGENT, RECONSTRUCTED AS CONTIGUOUS 5
AND 1.25 MM THICK AXIAL, 5 MM THICK CORONAL AND PARASAGITTAL, AND 8 MM MIP
AXIAL IMAGES. SUBSEQUENT SCANNING OF THE ABDOMEN AND PELVIS AND THE TOTAL
DOSAGE OF SCANNING THE ENTIRE TORSO WILL BE REPORTED SEPARATELY.

DOSAGE:  TOTAL DLP reported separatelymGy-cm

COMPARISON:  ___

FINDINGS: 

Heterogeneously enlarged thyroid gland is similar to prior CT and not fully
characterized by this modality.

There are no enlarged intrathoracic lymph nodes.  Heart size is normal, and no
pericardial effusion is present.  Previously present small bilateral pleural
effusions have resolved.

No new suspicious lytic or blastic skeletal lesions are evident.

Within the lungs, linear atelectasis is noted at both lung bases.  No new or
growing pulmonary nodules are detected.

IMPRESSION: 

1.  No CT evidence of pulmonary metastases.

2.  Please see separately dictated CT of the abdomen and pelvis for complete
description of subdiaphragmatic findings.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ woman with metastatic pancreatic cancer.  Restaging
study.

TECHNIQUE:   Multi-detector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic, iodinated contrast agent, reconstructed as
contiguous 5 mm and 1.0 or 1.25 mm thick axial, 2.5 or 5 mm thick coronal and
parasagittal, and 8 mm MIP axial images. Sequential scanning of the abdomen
and pelvis  will be reported separately. Images of the chest were reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 3.1 s, 24.6 cm; CTDIvol = 8.0 mGy (Body) DLP = 195.9
mGy-cm.
   2) Spiral Acquisition 7.8 s, 61.6 cm; CTDIvol = 10.9 mGy (Body) DLP = 673.9
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 870 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CTA ABD AND
PELVIS)

COMPARISON:  ___

FINDINGS: 

FINDINGS:

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: Multinodular appearance of the
enlarged thyroid (goiter) appears similar compared to prior imaging.  No
infiltration of surrounding structures.  No encasement of the trachea. No
supraclavicular adenopathy. No axillary adenopathy.  No gross breast lesions. 
Left-sided prepectoral Port-A-Cath in situ with the tip at the cavoatrial
junction.

UPPER ABDOMEN: Will be reported separately.

MEDIASTINUM: No mediastinal adenopathy.

HILA: No hilar adenopathy.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: Trace pericardial fluid.  Mild cardiomegaly.  No aortic
valve calcification.  No coronary artery calcification.  No aneurysmal
dilatation of the ascending aorta.  Mild atherosclerotic changes of the aortic
arch
PLEURA: No pleural effusion.
LUNG:

1. PARENCHYMA:  Millimetric pulmonary nodule in the left lower lobe is
unchanged.  No new or enlarging pulmonary nodules or masses.  Mild linear
atelectasis in the right lung base.  No confluent airspace consolidation.
2. AIRWAYS:  The airways are patent to the subsegmental level.  No
bronchiectasis.
3. VESSELS:  Mild dilatation of the pulmonary artery measuring 32 mm in
diameter and pulmonary hypertension should be excluded.
CHEST CAGE: No suspicious bony lesions.

IMPRESSION: 

No new or concerning pulmonary nodules or mediastinal adenopathy. No findings
to suggest intrathoracic metastatic disease.

Mild dilatation of the pulmonary artery and pulmonary hypertension should be
excluded.

For abdominal findings please refer to CT abdomen report.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ woman with metastatic pancreatic cancer,
chemotherapy, now presenting for restaging.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic iodinated contrast agent reconstructed as
contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick axial, 2.5-mm thick coronal and parasagittal,
and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images.

DOSE:  Total DLP (Body) = 540 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CTA ABD AND
PELVIS)

COMPARISON:  Chest CT dated ___.

FINDINGS: 

The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber and mildly calcified.  Mild dilation
of the main pulmonary artery to 32 mm is unchanged and can be seen as sequelae
of chronic pulmonary hypertension.  No pericardial effusion.  A left
Port-A-Cath tip ends in the distal SVC.

No axillary, supraclavicular, mediastinal, or hilar lymphadenopathy.  A 7 mm
lymph node at the thoracic inlet and is 8 mm subcarinal node are unchanged. 
22 x 11 mm hypodense ovoid structure anterior to the esophagus below the
subcarinal station is stable from most recent prior but new from ___,
potentially fluid in a pericardial recess (series 8, image 50; series 12,
image 64).

No new or growing pulmonary nodules.  No focal opacities concerning for focal
pneumonia. The airways are patent to at least the subsegmental level.  No
pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

The thyroid is enlarged and heterogeneous with multiple nodules, some
calcified, incompletely evaluated on this exam is similar the prior exam.

No osseous lesions concerning for malignancy in the chest cage.  Multilevel
degenerative changes are mild.

Please refer to the dedicated CT abdomen and pelvis report from the same day
for a description of sub-diaphragm findings.

IMPRESSION: 

1.  No new or growing pulmonary nodules.
2.  Subcarinal lymph node appears slightly larger.  Close attention on
follow-up imaging.
3.  22 x 11 mm hypodense ovoid structure below the subcarinal station is
unchanged from prior but new from ___, could be fluid in a pericardial
recess.  Close attention on follow-up imaging.
4.  Persistent mild dilation of the main pulmonary artery, suggesting sequelae
of chronic pulmonary hypertension.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CTA CHEST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with history of pneumonia treated 1 week ago
who presents with chest pain and elevated d-dimer// r/o PE

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.
Reformatted coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal
intensity projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Total DLP (Body) = 417 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  None

FINDINGS: 

The aorta and its major branch vessels are patent, with no evidence of
stenosis, occlusion, dissection, or aneurysmal formation.  There is no
evidence of penetrating atherosclerotic ulcer or aortic arch atheroma present.

The pulmonary arteries are well opacified to the subsegmental level, with no
evidence of filling defect within the main, right, left, lobar, segmental or
subsegmental pulmonary arteries.  The main and right pulmonary arteries are
normal in caliber, and there is no evidence of right heart strain.

There is a 1 cm left hilar lymph node which is likely reactive.   No
supraclavicular, axillary, or mediastinal lymphadenopathy.

There is no evidence of pericardial effusion.  There is no pleural effusion.

Within the middle lobe and the adjacent right lower lobe is a cluster of solid
nodular opacities and faint ground-glass opacities which likely represent
changes related to pneumonia, given reported history of recent pneumonia
treatment 1 week ago.  The airways are patent to the subsegmental level.

Limited images of the upper abdomen are unremarkable.

Small hemangioma within T9 vertebral body.  No lytic or blastic osseous lesion
suspicious for malignancy is identified.


IMPRESSION:


1. No evidence of pulmonary embolism or aortic abnormality.
2. Cluster of solid nodular opacities within the middle lobe and right lower
lobe likely represent changes related to recent pneumonia.  Please obtain
follow-up chest CT in 3 months to confirm resolution of these parenchymal
changes.

RECOMMENDATION(S):  Follow-up chest CT in 3 months to confirm resolution.

NOTIFICATION:  The findings were discussed with ___, M.D. by ___,
M.D. on the telephone on ___ at 5:00 pm, 1 minutes after discovery of
the findings.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:   ___ year old man with NASH cirrhosis, complicated by RP bleed s/p
cardiac cath for transplant work up now with renal failure, leukocytosis. //
Evaluate for any evidence of intraabdominal abscess, any evidence of PNA?or
complicated pleural effusion?

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic iodinated contrast agent reconstructed as
contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick axial, 2.5-mm thick coronal and parasagittal,
and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images.

DOSE:  DLP: Please refer to the separate dose report in the abdomen and pelvis
CT report.

COMPARISON:  ___

FINDINGS: 

MEDIASTINUM:  Heterogeneous thyroid with subcentimeter nodule. No
pathologically enlarged supraclavicular, axillary, hilar or 21mediastinal
lymph nodes.

HEART AND GREAT VESSELS:  The aorta is not aneurysmal.  The main pulmonary
artery measures 3.2 cm and is enlarged.  No large central filling defects in
the pulmonary arteries. The heart size is mildly enlarged and there is no
pericardial effusion. Minimal atherosclerotic calcifications of the thoracic
aorta and severe of the coronary arteries.  Right dialysis catheter terminates
in the right atrium.

PLEURA:  There is no pneumothorax.  Small bilateral pleural effusions have
decreased since the prior examination.

LUNGS AND TRACHEOBRONCHIAL TREE:  The patient is intubated with the
endotracheal tube in good position.  Moderate patient motion and expiration
cause motion artifact.  Left lower lobe collapse, lingular and right basilar
atelectasis has not substantially changed in volume, however there is
increasing endobronchial plugging in the lower lobes bilaterally.  No acute
interstitial or airspace opacity.  Mild air trapping, suggestive of small
airways disease.

BONES AND CHEST WALL: There are no destructive focal osseous or chest wall
lesions concerning for malignancy within the imaged thoracic skeleton.

UPPER ABDOMEN:  Please refer to the separate CT report of the abdomen and
pelvis.


IMPRESSION: 

No evidence of active intrathoracic infection or malignancy.

Small bilateral effusions with persistent left left lower lobe collapse and
right basal atelectasis with increasing endobronchial secretions in the lower
lobes.

MEDIASTINUM:  Heterogeneous thyroid with subcentimeter nodule. No
pathologically enlarged supraclavicular, axillary, hilar or 21mediastinal
lymph nodes.

HEART AND GREAT VESSELS:  The aorta is not aneurysmal.  The main pulmonary
artery measures 3.2 cm and is enlarged.  No large central filling defects in
the pulmonary arteries. The heart size is mildly enlarged and there is no
pericardial effusion. Minimal atherosclerotic calcifications of the thoracic
aorta and severe of the coronary arteries.  Right dialysis catheter terminates
in the right atrium.

PLEURA:  There is no pneumothorax.  Small bilateral pleural effusions have
decreased since the prior examination.

LUNGS AND TRACHEOBRONCHIAL TREE:  The patient is intubated with the
endotracheal tube in good position.  Moderate patient motion and expiration
cause motion artifact.  Left lower lobe collapse, lingular and right basilar
atelectasis has not substantially changed in volume, however there is
increasing endobronchial plugging in the lower lobes bilaterally.  No acute
interstitial or airspace opacity.  Mild air trapping, suggestive of small
airways disease.

BONES AND CHEST WALL: There are no destructive focal osseous or chest wall
lesions concerning for malignancy within the imaged thoracic skeleton.

UPPER ABDOMEN:  Please refer to the separate CT report of the abdomen and
pelvis.
sections.impression:
No evidence of active intrathoracic infection or malignancy.

Small bilateral effusions with persistent left left lower lobe collapse and
right basal atelectasis with increasing endobronchial secretions in the lower
lobes.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CTA TORSO

INDICATION:  ___ year old man POD#2 EVAR now with severe back pain and
inability to lift legs secondary to pain in groin// ?correct position of
graft?groin access issue??RP hematoma

TECHNIQUE:  Abdomen and pelvis CTA: Non-contrast and multiphasic post-contrast
images were acquired through the abdomen and pelvis.
Oral contrast was not administered.
MIP reconstructions were performed on independent workstation and reviewed on
PACS.

DOSE:
 Total DLP (Body) = 2,042 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CTA abdomen pelvis from outside institution ___.

FINDINGS: 

VASCULAR:

POST EVAR EVALUATION

ANEURYSM Infrarenal
Diameter (largest Cross-sectional): 5.9 cm
Diameter (Center-line): 5.7 x 5.5 cm
Volume:

1. Aneurysm: 131 ml
2. Renal to Aortic bifurcation: 162 ml
3. Renal to Iliac bifurcation: 211 ml
ENDOLEAK: no

EVAR MIGRATION
Length proximal graft to lowest renal artery: 12.1 cm
Length distal graft to iliac bifurcation:  Right: 2.1 cm.  Left: 3.1 cm.

Patient status post aorto bi-iliac endovascular aneurysmal repair for an
infrarenal aneurysm with patent stents extending from the suprarenal abdominal
aorta to the common iliac arteries bilaterally.  Stents appear in appropriate
position.  The largest diameter is just proximal to the aortic bifurcation is
similar in size measuring approximately 5.6 x 5.5 cm.  More detailed
measurements as above.  There is no aneurysmal dilation of the major branches
of the aorta.  There is severe calcium burden in the abdominal aorta and great
abdominal arteries.

CHEST: There is mild biapical centrilobular emphysema.  In addition, there are
few nodular opacities in the superior segment of the right lower lobe
measuring up to 8 mm, which are either new compared to prior or were outside
the field of view on prior imaging.  Minimal atelectasis is noted in the lung
bases. There is no pleural or pericardial effusion. Patient is status post
median sternotomy with CABG postsurgical changes.  The aorta and its major
vessels demonstrate no abnormalities.  Airways are patent.  The esophagus is
slightly patulous.  No evidence of intrathoracic lymphadenopathy.  The
visualized lower neck is unremarkable.

ABDOMEN:

HEPATOBILIARY: There is a 1.7 cm hepatic hypodensity in segment II which is
too small to characterize but likely represents a simple cyst or biliary
hamartoma in the absence of underlying malignancy (3:109).  Otherwise, the
liver demonstrates homogenous attenuation throughout. There is no evidence of
focal lesions. There is no evidence of intrahepatic or extrahepatic biliary
dilatation.  The gallbladder is contains stones, without evidence of
gallbladder wall thickening or pericholecystic fluid.

PANCREAS: The pancreas has normal attenuation throughout, without evidence of
focal lesions or pancreatic ductal dilatation. There is no peripancreatic
stranding.

SPLEEN: The spleen shows normal size and attenuation throughout, without
evidence of focal lesions.

ADRENALS: The right and left adrenal glands are normal in size and shape.

URINARY: There are stable numerous bilateral cortical hypodensities, some with
peripheral wall calcifications, consistent with renal cysts and unchanged
compared to prior.  Otherwise, the kidneys are of normal and symmetric size
with normal nephrogram. There is no evidence of stones, focal renal lesions,
or hydronephrosis. There are no urothelial lesions in the kidneys or ureters.
There is no perinephric abnormality.

GASTROINTESTINAL: Small bowel loops demonstrate normal caliber, wall thickness
and enhancement throughout.  There is diverticulosis without diverticulitis. 
Appendix contains air, has normal caliber without evidence of fat stranding. 
There is no evidence of mesenteric lymphadenopathy.

RETROPERITONEUM: There is no evidence of retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy.

PELVIS: The urinary bladder and distal ureters are unremarkable.  There is no
evidence of pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.  There is no free fluid in the
pelvis.

REPRODUCTIVE ORGANS: The prostate and seminal vesicles are normal.

BONES: There is no evidence of worrisome osseous lesions or acute fracture. 
Stable multilevel degenerative changes of the visualized thoracolumbar spine
are noted.  Posterior lumbosacral spinal fusion hardware is visualized without
gross evidence of hardware complications.

SOFT TISSUES: There are small bilateral fat containing inguinal hernias.  The
abdominal and pelvic wall is within normal limits.

IMPRESSION:


1. Status post EVAR with patent stent in appropriate position and similar
aneurysmal sac ___ as detailed above.  No evidence of endoleak.
2. No evidence of retroperitoneal hematoma.
3. No evidence support groin access complications.
4. Possibly new superior right lower lobe pulmonary nodules measuring up to 8
mm which may be of infectious/inflammatory etiology, however follow-up is
recommended as detailed below in the recommendation section of this report.
5. Cholelithiasis without acute cholecystitis.
6. Diverticulosis without diverticulitis.

RECOMMENDATION(S):

1.  For incidentally detected multiple solid pulmonary nodules measuring 6 to
8mm, a CT follow-up in 3 to 6 months is recommended in a low-risk patient,
with an optional CT follow-up in 18 to 24 months. In a high-risk patient, both
a CT follow-up in 3 to 6 months and in 18 to 24 months is recommended.

See the ___ ___ Society Guidelines for the Management of Pulmonary
Nodules Incidentally Detected on CT" for comments and reference:
___

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  Pedestrian truck by car, now with chest pain and chest wall
tenderness.

COMPARISON:  No prior studies available due to patient's critical status.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT-acquired axial images were obtained through the chest
following the uneventful administration of 75 cc of Omnipaque intravenous
contrast.  Coronal and sagittal reformatted images were generated and
reviewed.

FINDINGS:  The imaged portion of the thyroid gland is unremarkable.  No
pathologically enlarged axillary, mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes are seen. 
There is no mediastinal hemorrhage or pneumomediastinum.  The thoracic aorta
is normal in caliber without evidence of acute aortic syndrome. 
Mild-to-moderate calcification of the aortic arch and descending thoracic
aorta is noted.  The pulmonary arterial trunk is normal in caliber.  The heart
is normal in size without pericardial effusion.  Mild-to-moderate global
coronary artery calcifications are present.  The esophagus is unremarkable.

The central tracheobronchial tree is patent to the subsegmental levels
bilaterally.  Posterior dependent positional changes are noted throughout the
lungs.  No focal consolidation, pleural effusion or pneumothorax is present. 
Paraseptal emphysema is noted in the bilateral upper lobes.  No suspicious
pulmonary nodules or masses are identified.

Although this study is not tailored for the evaluation of subdiaphragmatic
contents, the imaged right upper abdomen demonstrates nodular contours in the
liver suggestive of underlying cirrhosis.  An accessory spleen is incidentally
noted in the left upper quadrant.  No acute abnormality is detected.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  No acute fracture or hematoma is identified in the
thorax.  There is no osseous destructive lesion concerning for malignancy.

IMPRESSION:
1.  No acute fracture or evidence of solid organ injury in the thorax.
2.  Paraseptal emphysema in the upper lobes.
3.  Mild-to-moderate global coronary artery calcifications.
4.  Nodular-appearing liver suggestive of underlying cirrhosis.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ woman with history of medullary and papillary thyroid
cancer, cough, shortness of breath, concerning for post-obstructive pneumonia.
Evaluate for etiology of cough.

COMPARISON:  ___ PET CT ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Multiple MDCT axial images were obtained from the base of the neck
through the upper abdomen after the uneventful intravenous administration of
75 cc of Optiray.  1.5- and 5-mm collimation was used for actual slices.
Multiplanar reformats were derived.

FINDINGS:

CTA OF THE CHEST WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST:

The patient is status post thyroidectomy.  Paramediastinal lung apices
demonstrate scarring and traction bronchiectasis consistent with post-
radiation changes.  There is no axillary adenopathy.  A subcarinal node is
more prominent on today's study (series 2, image 24) measuring to 11 mm. The
pulmonary artery is normal in caliber 2.6 cm.  The aorta is normal in caliber
and course.  The heart is top normal in size.  There is no pericardial
effusion.

The trachea and bilateral main bronchi are patent.  Previously demonstrated
secretions are absent today.  There is mucoid impaction of subsegmental
bronchi to the right lower lobe (see for example series 2, image 37).
Peripheral to this, a nodular consolidative opacification is seen (series 2,
image 46).  In the right upper lobe (2, image 20) consolidative opacification
with proximal centrolobular nodularity suggests an infectious etiology.
Nodular opacification in the right middle lobe is also consistent with an
infectious etiology.  A consolidative opacity is seen in the right perihilar
area (series 2, image 35).  In the left lung, there is mild basilar
atelectasis. Small pulmonary nodules seen at the apex (series 4, image 23,
30).  There is no pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

This study is not optimized for visualizing the abdomen.  The liver,
gallbladder, pancreas, and adrenals are grossly normal.  The kidneys
symmetrically take up and excrete contrast.  There are multiple hypodensities
seen within the left kidney, likely represent cysts.  Calcification in the
splenic hilum likely represents calcified splenic artery aneurysm.

MUSCULOSKELETAL:  Well-marginated structures in the vertebral bodies of polka-
dot appearance are likely hemangioma.  Small 3-mm sclerotic focus in the mid
thoracic vertebra is indeterminate.

IMPRESSION:  Multifocal consolidation in the right lung with mucoid impaction
and associated nodularity suggests an infectious process.  Coexistent
metastatic disease cannot be assessed.  Recommend repeat chest CT after
treatment has been completed.

Left apical pulmonary nodules which can be evaluated on the followup chest CT.

Indeterminate sclerotic apparently new focus in the mid thoracic vertebrae. 
Uncharacteristic for a papillary thyroid metastasis.  Recommend attention to
this area on followup.

radiology_facts.report_text:
CLINICAL INDICATION:  ___ female with left pleuritic chest pain and
tachycardia, also with history of malignancy.  Evaluate for pulmonary
embolism.

COMPARISON:  Multiple prior CTs, most recently ___ and ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Axial CT images through the chest were acquired before and after
administration of intravenous contrast.  Coronal, sagittal, and oblique
reconstructions were reviewed.

FINDINGS:  The pulmonary arteries are patent to the subsegmental levels. 
There is no evidence for pulmonary embolism.  The aorta is normal in caliber
and contour.  The remainder of the visualized portions of the great vessels
and heart are unremarkable.  There are no pleural or pericardial effusions.

There are numerous ill-defined nodular opacities distributed throughout the
right upper, middle, and lower lobes, as well as the lingula.  There is
bronchial wall thickening and likely small airways mucoid impaction, all of
which are suggestive of small airways infection.  Small right hilar lymph
nodes are likely reactive. Evaluation for metastatic disease is limited in
this setting.  Paramediastinal post-radiation changes and bronchiectasis are
again visualized.  Calcified hilar nodes are unchanged.  

Visualized bony structures demonstrate degenerative changes of the spine.

IMPRESSION:
1.  No evidence for pulmonary embolism or acute aortic pathology.
2.  Ill-defined nodular opacities, mucoid impaction, and bronchial wall
thickening throughout the right lung and lingula, suggestive of small airways
infection.  Evaluation for metastatic disease is limited in this setting, and
therefore repeat chest CT is recommended after resolution of acute process.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CTA CHEST WANDW/O CANDRECONS, NON-CORONARY

INDICATION:  History: ___ with left upper extremity deep venous thrombosis,
new hypoxia

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.
Reformatted coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal
intensity projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Total DLP (Body) = 420 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  PA and lateral chest dated ___

FINDINGS: 

The aorta and its major branch vessels are patent, with no evidence of
stenosis, occlusion, dissection, or aneurysmal formation.  There is no
evidence of penetrating atherosclerotic ulcer.  Moderate atherosclerosis of
the aorta is noted.  Evaluation of the heart demonstrates extensive coronary
artery calcifications as well as mild aortic valve calcifications to the is
moderate enlargement of the heart.

The pulmonary arteries are well opacified to the subsegmental level, with no
evidence of filling defect within the main, right, left, lobar, segmental or
subsegmental pulmonary arteries.  The main pulmonary artery is enlarged,
measuring up to 3.6 cm in greatest diameter.

There is no supraclavicular, axillary, mediastinal, or hilar lymphadenopathy. 
The thyroid gland is markedly enlarged with multiple nodules and extends into
the anterior mediastinum, causing mass effect on and marked narrowing of the
trachea to a transverse diameter of 6 mm.  These findings predominantly
involve the left lobe of the thyroid, which measures roughly 6.8 x 7.7 x 6.4
cm.

There is no evidence of pericardial effusion.  There is no pleural effusion.

Mild centrilobular emphysematous changes are seen at the apices.  Multiple
solid pulmonary nodules are noted bilaterally including left upper lobe
(3:81), left lower lobe (3:  130 and 168), and right lower lobe (3:160 and
161).  The largest of these measures up to 7 mm in the right lower lobe. The
airways are patent to the subsegmental level.  Atelectasis is noted in the
lingula and left upper lobe.

Limited images of the upper abdomen demonstrates a small hiatal hernia. 
Scattered sub cm hypodensities in the liver may reflect cysts or biliary
hamartomas, but not fully characterized.  Multiple renal hypodensities are
noted, likely cysts.  A splenic cyst is noted as well.  Marked atherosclerotic
calcifications are seen at the takeoff of the celiac artery with at least
moderate narrowing.

Multilevel degenerative changes are seen throughout the thoracic spine,
without significant vertebral body height loss.

IMPRESSION: 

1.   No evidence of pulmonary embolism or acute aortic abnormality.
2.  Multiple pulmonary nodules, the largest of which measures 7 mm.  If
clinically indicated, follow-up chest CT is suggested at ___ months.  If there
is no change, a second follow-up in ___ months and then at 24 months is
recommended if there is no change.
3.  Dilated main pulmonary artery likely reflective of pulmonary arterial
hypertension.
4.  Markedly enlarged thyroid gland with mediastinal extension and severe
narrowing of the trachea.
5.  Mild centrilobular emphysema.

RECOMMENDATION(S):  Multiple pulmonary nodules, the largest of which measures
7 mm.  If clinically indicated, follow-up chest CT is suggested at ___ months.
If there is no change, a second follow-up in ___ months and then at 24 months
is recommended if there is no change.

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  Lung cancer.  Sternal mass.

COMPARISON:  CT of the chest performed at an outside institution ___.

PROCEDURE:  CT-guided biopsies of soft tissue mass centered at the anterior
aspect of the manubrium/upper sternum.

After reviewing the patient's previous imaging studies and obtaining informed
consent, the patient was brought to the CT suite.  Initial axial non-contrast
images through the manubrium and upper sternum were obtained.  A timeout was
performed.  

Conscious sedation was provided by a radiology nurse trained in conscious
sedation including a total dosage of 50 mcg of fentanyl and 1.0 mg of
intravenous Versed.  The total intraservice time was 15 minutes during which
the patient's hemodynamic parameters were continuously monitored.

The skin overlying the manubrium and upper sternum was prepped with Betadine
solution.  1% lidocaine was injected into the subcutaneous tissues for local
anesthetic.  A 16-gauge Achieve soft tissue biopsy device was advanced into
the lesion and four samples were taken from slightly different positions
within the lesion.  Specimens were taken to surgical pathology.

FINDINGS:

Similar to the previous CT are multiple pulmonary nodules and masses, the
largest in the superior segment of the left upper lobe measures approximately
3 cm (AP) x 2.3 cm (TV).  There is a small amount of right pleural fluid.  

There is atherosclerotic calcification of the thoracic aorta and there are
heavy coronary artery calcifications.

Again seen centered at the anterior aspect of the manubrium and the upper
sternum is a 5.9 cm (TV) x 3.5 cm (AP) destructive soft tissue mass.  There
are large areas of erosion and soft tissue infiltration of the medial left
clavicle at the clavicular manubrial junction.  Sternal wires are again seen
in the more inferior aspect of the sternum.

The patient tolerated the procedure well and was observed in the department
for two hours following the procedure.

The procedure was performed with and supervised by Dr. ___.

IMPRESSION:

Successful CT-guided biopsies of the soft tissue mass at the anterior aspect
of the manubrium/upper sternum.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with severe AS  // Assessment of aortic
calcifications no IV contrast

TECHNIQUE:  Volumetric CT acquisitions over the entire thorax in inspiration,
no administration of intravenous contrast material, multiplanar
reconstructions.

DOSE:  DLP:  239 mGy-cm

COMPARISON:  No comparison.

FINDINGS: 

No incidental thyroid findings.  No supraclavicular, infraclavicular or
axillary lymphadenopathy.  The lymph nodes in the mediastinum (3, 14) are
increased in number but not in size.

Severe calcification of the aortic arch, moderate calcification of the
ascending and of the descending aorta.  At the level of the main pulmonary
artery (3, 29) the ascending aorta has a diameter of 32 x 33 mm, at the same
anatomical level, the descending aorta has a diameter of 25 x 28 mm.  The
diameter of the pulmonary artery is normal.  Severe aortic valve
calcifications.  Severe coronary calcifications, global enlargement of the
heart, no pericardial effusions.

No osteolytic lesions at the level of the ribs, the sternum, or the vertebral
bodies.  Mild degenerative vertebral disease.  No vertebral compression
fractures.  Moderate to severe upper lobe predominant centrilobular pulmonary
emphysema.  Signs of moderate chronic airways disease.  Moderate bilateral
pleural effusions with adjacent compressive atelectasis.  Focal atelectasis at
the bases of the lingular.  Several non suspicious small pulmonary nodules are
visualized (5, 196).  No evidence of suspicious lung lesions.  Moderate
respiratory motion at the bases of the lungs.

IMPRESSION: 

Aortic ___ are reported above.  Moderate bilateral pleural effusions
with moderate chronic airways disease.  Basal areas of atelectasis.  No
evidence of acute infectious or neoplastic changes.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  Status post stabbing to the left flank.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical CT scan of the chest, abdomen and pelvis was
obtained after the administration of oral contrast and 130 cc IV Optiray
contrast.  Axial, coronal, and sagittal reformatted images were prepared.

FINDINGS:

CT CHEST WITH ORAL AND IV CONTRAST:  Along the right minor fissure, there is a
7-mm nodular density (2A:25), which is most compatible with a small lymph
node.  In a patient of this age, no follow up is needed.  No pleural effusions
or pneumothoraces are seen.  The airways are patent to the segmental level. 
The heart and great vessels are unremarkable.  No lymphadenopathy is seen.

CT ABDOMEN WITH ORAL AND IV CONTRAST:  The liver, spleen, bilateral kidneys,
bilateral adrenal glands, pancreas and gallbladder appear unremarkable.  The
small and large bowel are of normal size and caliber.  No abdominal free air,
free fluid, or lymphadenopathy is seen.  

Within the left erector spinae muscle group, emphysema and areas of hematoma
are seen (301b:44).  Air tracks superiorly along the muscle groups; however,
does not appear to enter the abdominal cavity.  This can be best appreciated
on the sagittal series 301b.  A skin defect is seen in the left flank (2a:67).

CT PELVIS WITH ORAL AND IV CONTRAST:  The sigmoid colon, rectum, prostate
gland, bladder, and distal ureters appear unremarkable.  No pelvic free air,
free fluid, or lymphadenopathy is seen.

BONE WINDOWS:  No fracture or concerning lytic or sclerotic lesion is
identified.

IMPRESSION:
1.  No acute intra-abdominal or intrathoracic process.
2.  Left flank skin defect with underlying subcutaneous emphysema and hematoma
within the left erector spinae muscle group, consistent with history of
stabbing.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  Followup of the patient with colon cancer, status post resection.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical CT scan of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis
was obtained after the administration of oral contrast and 130 cc IV Optiray
contrast.  Three-minute delayed imaging of the abdomen was also obtained. 
Axial, coronal, and sagittal reformats were constructed.

COMPARISON:  CT scan dated ___.

FINDINGS:

CT CHEST WITH ORAL AND IV CONTRAST:  No pleural effusions or concerning
nodules or lesions are seen.  A central venous line is in place with tip at
the superior cavoatrial junction.  The heart size is top normal.

A liver hypodensity (2:55) is unchanged and most likely represents a simple
cyst, though continued attention at follow up is warranted.  The spleen,
bilateral kidneys, bilateral adrenal glands, pancreas, and gallbladder appear
normal.  The patient is status post right hemicolectomy.  The suture line is
visualized and no lesions are seen in the surgical bed.  Numerous scattered
mesenteric and para-aortic lymph nodes do not meet CT criteria for pathologic
enlargement.  A para-aortic node measuring 9 mm (2:77) is stable as well as a
9-mm node (2:79).  No abdominal free air or free fluid is seen.

CT PELVIS WITH ORAL AND IV CONTRAST:  Again, the patient is status post right
hemicolectomy.  The remaining colon and rectum appear normal.  A hypodensity
in the right psoas muscle (2:84) has a linear lateral border and may be
partial volume averaging.  The bladder is unremarkable.  The prostate is
enlarged.  No pelvic free air, free fluid, or pathologically enlarged lymph
nodes are seen.

BONE  WINDOWS:  There are degenerative changes as well as scattered bone
islands.  No concerning lytic or sclerotic lesions are seen.

IMPRESSION: 
1)  No definite recurrence or metastatic disease identified.
2)  Hypodensity in right psoas muscle has linear lateral border and may
represent partial volume averaging, however continued attention to this area
at follow up is recommended.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CTA CHEST WITH CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with new O2 requirement, low O2 sat and
shortness of breath with possible pericardial effusion. Requesting CTA chest
down to the diaphragm to rule out an aortic dissection.  // Requesting CTA
chest down to the diaphragm to rule out an aortic dissection.

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.  Reformatted
coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal intensity
projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:
Acquisition sequence:
   1) Stationary Acquisition 0.5 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 0.8 mGy (Body) DLP = 0.4
mGy-cm.
   2) Stationary Acquisition 0.5 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 0.8 mGy (Body) DLP = 0.4
mGy-cm.
   3) Stationary Acquisition 2.0 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 3.2 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.6
mGy-cm.
   4) Spiral Acquisition 3.8 s, 29.6 cm; CTDIvol = 7.7 mGy (Body) DLP = 227.6
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 230 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CT ANGIOGRAM CHEST ___.  Correlation is made to portable
chest x-ray from ___.

FINDINGS:


HEART AND VASCULATURE: Pulmonary vasculature is well opacified to the
subsegmental level without filling defect to indicate a pulmonary embolus. 
There is moderate motion artifact in the ascending aorta.  The thoracic aorta
is normal in caliber without evidence of dissection or intramural hematoma.  A
small pericardial effusion is seen, likely representing hemopericardium. 
There is cardiac enlargement, with enlargement of the right atrium, right
ventricle.

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM:  High density is seen surrounding the great
vessels and aorta, extending into the mediastinum, surrounding the upper
portion of the heart, likely representing hematoma.  This it is mildly less
prominent compared with CTA neck ___ at 22:00.  No axillary
adenopathy.  1 cm precarinal node is unchanged.  No hilar adenopathy.

PLEURAL SPACES:  There are bilateral moderate pleural effusions with increased
density, they have mildly increased since ___ CTA, and are
worrisome for hemorrhagic effusions.  There is bilateral lower lobe
atelectasis and volume loss.


LUNGS/AIRWAYS: Lungs are clear without masses or areas of parenchymal
opacification.  The airways are patent to the level of the segmental bronchi
bilaterally.  Bilateral lower lobe atelectasis is present.

BASE OF NECK:  Subcentimeter hypoattenuating nodules in the thyroid are better
appreciated on the prior examination from ___.

ABDOMEN:  Small hiatal hernia noted.  Sub cm hypoattenuating lesions in the
liver (image 82, image 87 of series 2) are too small to completely
characterize, and most likely represent cysts or biliary hamartomas. 
Accessory spleen noted.  There is tiny probable transient arterial attenuation
defect in the liver.  Small benign simple cyst right kidney.  There is stable
0.5 cm partially thrombosed splenic artery aneurysm at the hilum.

BONES: No suspicious osseous abnormality is seen.? multiple healing right rib
fractures again noted.  There are acute fractures of the left lateral fifth,
sixth, seventh, eighth ribs.  There is chronic severe T5 compression fracture.
Interval progression of T3 compression fracture, with interval height loss of
sclerosis.  Degenerative changes spine.  Kyphosis.

IMPRESSION: 

1.  Mediastinal hematoma has mildly decreased since ___. 
Hemorrhagic pleural effusions have mildly increased.  There is small
hemorrhagic pericardial effusion.  Images of the ascending aorta are
moderately compromised by motion.  No definite evidence for dissection or
pulmonary embolism.  If there is high index of suspicion for aortic injury,
catheter angiogram would be helpful.
2.  Acute left rib fractures again noted.  Worsened T3 compression fracture.

NOTIFICATION:  Findings discussed with NP ___ by ___, M.D.
___ at 10:11 pm, approximately two hours after discovery of the
findings.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT chest abdomen and pelvis with contrast

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman s/p cerebral angiogram with known mediastinal
hematoma.  // Please include chest/abdomen and pelvis, evaluate for
hemothorax, retroperitoneal hematoma

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast in the arterial
phase.  Then, imaging was obtained through the abdomen and pelvis in the
portal venous phase. Reformatted coronal and sagittal images through the
chest, abdomen, and pelvis, and oblique maximal intensity projection images of
the chest were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Total DLP (Body) = 2,955 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CT angiogram of the chest ___ at 15 50, CT torso on
___

FINDINGS: 

CHEST:

HEART AND VASCULATURE: Pulmonary vasculature is well opacified to the
subsegmental level without filling defect to indicate a pulmonary embolus.  
Hematoma is again seen in the superior mediastinum, as on examination is
performed ___, and ___.  This finding is unchanged from the
prior examination.  There is no interval evidence for active extravasation The
thoracic aorta is again, normal in caliber without evidence of dissection or
intramural hematoma.  There is trace pericardial effusion, previously
characterized as hemopericardium.

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM:  Mediastinal lymph nodes are again noted to
measure up to 1 cm (image 39, series 3A).  No axillary or hilar
lymphadenopathy.

PLEURAL SPACES: Bilateral effusions, with elevated density, are unchanged, and
most likely represent hemothorax.


LUNGS/AIRWAYS:  Atelectasis is again seen overlying the bilateral effusions. 
No new parenchymal consolidation.  The airways are patent to the level of the
segmental bronchi bilaterally.

BASE OF NECK:  Hematoma in the superior mediastinum/base of the neck,
surrounding the great vessels is unchanged.  Subcentemeter thyroid nodules
again noted.

ABDOMEN:  No evidence for retroperitoneal hematoma.

HEPATOBILIARY: Sub cm hypoattenuating lesions are seen in the liver, too small
to completely characterize, statistically most likely to represent simple
cysts or biliary hamartomas.  The largest of these is in the dome of the liver
(image 85, series 3), and measures 8 mm.  No suspicious liver lesions.  There
is no evidence of intrahepatic or extrahepatic biliary dilatation.  The
gallbladder contains gallstones without wall thickening or surrounding
inflammation.

PANCREAS: There is fatty atrophy of the pancreas.  No concerning pancreatic
lesions. There is no peripancreatic stranding.

SPLEEN: The spleen shows normal size and attenuation throughout, without
evidence of focal lesions.  Accessory spleen noted.

ADRENALS:  The right and left adrenal glands are normal in size and shape.

URINARY: The kidneys are atrophic, with symmetric nephrogram.  5 mm
nonobstructing stone noted in the midpole of the right kidney.  There is a
subcentimeter hypoattenuating lesion in the midpole of the left kidney, too
small to completely characterize, potentially representing a cyst, or focus of
cortical scarring (image 109, series 3). There is no perinephric abnormality.

GASTROINTESTINAL: Small hiatal hernia noted.  Small bowel loops demonstrate
normal caliber, wall thickness, and enhancement throughout.  Diverticulosis
noted.  The appendix is normal.  There is no free intraperitoneal fluid or
free air.

PELVIS: Air in the urinary bladder is likely related to recent
instrumentation.  Diverticulum noted at the left anterior aspect the bladder. 
There is no free fluid in the pelvis.

REPRODUCTIVE ORGANS: Again seen is a large, predominantly simple cystic lesion
in the pelvis, which appears to be arising from the left adnexa.  This
measures approximately 10.8 cm AP by 11.0 cm transverse.  Peripheral
calcifications are seen anteriorly, as on the prior examination.  Free fluid
is noted in the uterus, abnormal for the patient's age.  .

LYMPH NODES: There is no retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy.  There
is no pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

VASCULAR: There is no abdominal aortic aneurysm.  Mild atherosclerotic disease
is noted.

BONES AND SOFT TISSUES: Compression deformities of T3 and T5 again noted. 
Bilateral rib fractures again noted.  Stranding at the right groin is likely
related to recent vascular access.  No hematoma.

IMPRESSION: 

1.  Superior mediastinal hematoma, without evidence for active extravasation,
not significantly changed from the prior examination.
2.  High-density, moderate size pleural effusions, not significantly changed,
likely representing hemothorax.
3.  Bilateral rib fractures again noted.
4.  Compression deformities of T3 and T5.
5.  No retroperitoneal hematoma.
6.  Large cystic lesion, likely arising from the left ovary, predominately
simple, with peripheral calcification.
7.  Fluid in the endometrial canal, abnormal, given the patient's age.
8.  Diverticulosis.


RECOMMENDATION(S):  Nonurgent pelvic ultrasound is recommended for further
evaluation of the cystic left adnexal lesion, and intrauterine fluid versus
endometrial thickening.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ male with massive hematemesis.  Please evaluate
aortoenteric fistula.

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of 100 cc of Omnipaque intravenous
contrast in the arterial phase.  Then, imaging was obtained through the
abdomen and pelvis in the arterial phase phase. Reformatted coronal and
sagittal images through the chest, abdomen and pelvis, and oblique maximal
intensity projection images of the chest were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:   This study involved 4 CT acquisition phases with dose indices as
follows:
   1) CT Localizer Radiograph
   2) CT Localizer Radiograph
   3) Stationary Acquisition 2.0 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 6.1 mGy (Body) DLP = 3.0
mGy-cm.
   4) Spiral Acquisition 8.7 s, 68.4 cm; CTDIvol = 15.0 mGy (Body) DLP =
1,022.9 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 1,026 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  Chest radiograph from ___.

FINDINGS: 

VASCULAR:
The thoracic aorta is unremarkable without dissection or aneurysm.  The
thoracic great vessels are unremarkable. The pulmonary arteries are well
opacified to the subsegmental level without filling defect to suggest
pulmonary embolism. Pulmonary arteries are normal in caliber.

There is no aortic aneurysm or dissection. There is moderate calcium burden in
the abdominal aorta and great abdominal arteries.  The abdominal aorta and its
major branches are patent.  The celiac artery and superior mesenteric artery
arise from a common trunk (series 602b:image 46).  There is no evidence for
aortoenteric fistula.

CHEST:
The esophagus contains an enteric tube and appears unremarkable without
evidence of perforation, wall thickening, or presence of esophageal varices. 
No esophageal fistula is present.  There is no supraclavicular or axillary
lymphadenopathy.  There is no mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy.  An
endotracheal tube terminates in the distal trachea, and the airways are patent
to the segmental level.  The esophagus is normal with an enteric tube
terminating in the stomach.  The heart is normal in size without pericardial
effusion.  Left ventricular hypertrophy is noted (series 2:image 58).

There are centrilobular nodular opacities in the right upper lobe likely
reflective of aspiration (series 2:  Image 46, 43).  Dependent atelectasis is
noted in the bilateral lower lobes.  No pleural effusion or pneumothorax is
seen.

ABDOMEN:
HEPATOBILIARY: There are multiple peripherally enhancing lesions scattered
throughout the liver with the largest measuring 2.4 x 2.2 cm in the right
hepatic lobe (series 2:80, 83, 100,105,117). These are concerning for
metastatic disease. There is no evidence of intrahepatic or extrahepatic
biliary dilatation. The gallbladder is within normal limits.

PANCREAS: There is an ill-defined mass arising from the pancreatic body which
encases the celiac artery, splenic artery, common hepatic artery and left
gastric artery (series 2: Image 104, 100).  There is no pancreatic ductal
dilation.  There is a mildly enlarged periportal lymph node measuring 1.1 x
1.8 cm (series 2:image 112).

SPLEEN: The spleen shows normal size.  Heterogeneous enhancement of the spleen
is due to timing of imaging.

ADRENALS: The right and left adrenal glands are normal in size and shape.

URINARY: The kidneys are of normal and symmetric size with normal nephrogram. 
There is no evidence of focal renal lesion or hydronephrosis. There is no
perinephric abnormality.

GASTROINTESTINAL:  An enteric tube terminates in the stomach.  A small hiatal
hernia is noted.  There is no evidence of active contrast extravasation within
the stomach, small bowel or large bowel.  Small bowel loops demonstrate normal
caliber, wall thickness and enhancement throughout.  High-density material
within the lumen of the distal ileum, cecum, ascending colon and transverse
colon likely reflect blood given clinical history.  There is no definite
colonic wall thickening or signs of obstruction.

RETROPERITONEUM: There is no evidence of retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy.
There is no free intraperitoneal fluid.  There is a mild amount of mesenteric
free fluid (series 2:image 130).

PELVIS: The urinary bladder is decompressed by a Foley catheter. There is no
evidence of pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy. There is a small amount of
free fluid in the pelvis.

REPRODUCTIVE ORGANS: Prostate and seminal vesicles are unremarkable.

BONES AND SOFT TISSUES: There is no evidence of worrisome osseous lesions. 
There is no fracture.  Bilateral fat-containing inguinal hernias are seen as
well as a small fat containing umbilical hernia.  .

IMPRESSION: 

1.  High density material in the lumen of the distal ileum, cecum, ascending
colon and transverse colon compatible with blood.  Source of bleeding is
unclear with no evidence of active contrast extravasation within the
gastrointestinal tract or aortoenteric fistula.  The esophagus appears
unremarkable without evidence of varices or perforation.
2.  Poorly evaluated malignant pancreatic mass encasing the celiac artery and
its branches with multiple hepatic lesions compatible with metastases.
3.  Nodular opacities in the right upper lobe compatible with aspiration.

GASTROINTESTINAL:  An enteric tube terminates in the stomach.  A small hiatal
hernia is noted.  There is no evidence of active contrast extravasation within
the stomach, small bowel or large bowel.  Small bowel loops demonstrate normal
caliber, wall thickness and enhancement throughout.  High-density material
within the lumen of the distal ileum, cecum, ascending colon and transverse
colon likely reflect blood given clinical history.  There is no definite
colonic wall thickening or signs of obstruction.

RETROPERITONEUM: There is no evidence of retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy.
There is no free intraperitoneal fluid.  There is a mild amount of mesenteric
free fluid (series 2:image 130).

PELVIS: The urinary bladder is decompressed by a Foley catheter. There is no
evidence of pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy. There is a small amount of
free fluid in the pelvis.

REPRODUCTIVE ORGANS: Prostate and seminal vesicles are unremarkable.

BONES AND SOFT TISSUES: There is no evidence of worrisome osseous lesions. 
There is no fracture.  Bilateral fat-containing inguinal hernias are seen as
well as a small fat containing umbilical hernia.  .
sections.impression:
1.  High density material in the lumen of the distal ileum, cecum, ascending
colon and transverse colon compatible with blood.  Source of bleeding is
unclear with no evidence of active contrast extravasation within the
gastrointestinal tract or aortoenteric fistula.  The esophagus appears
unremarkable without evidence of varices or perforation.
2.  Poorly evaluated malignant pancreatic mass encasing the celiac artery and
its branches with multiple hepatic lesions compatible with metastases.
3.  Nodular opacities in the right upper lobe compatible with aspiration.

radiology_facts.report_text:
CHEST ___

HISTORY:  Evaluate ascending thoracic aorta for size and calcification prior
to surgery.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was performed without
intravenous contrast agent reconstructed as contiguous 1.25- and 5-mm thick
axial and 5-mm thick coronal and paramedian sagittal images.  There are no
prior chest CTs for comparison.

FINDINGS:  The aortic valve is heavily calcified.  The ascending thoracic
aorta is top normal size, 39 mm across, and contains minimal calcified plaque.
at the level of the main pulmonary artery.  Atherosclerotic plaque is
considerably heavier in the arch, extending into the origin of the innominate
artery and at its carotid/subclavian bifurcation, occluding the lumens by at
least half, possibly considerably more. Atherosclerotic calcification is
heaviest in the coronary arteries involving all branches, and in the abdominal
aorta and major branches.  The most inferior axial image in this examination
raises the possibility of abdominal aortic aneurysm with an increase in
caliber from 21 to at least 25 mm. Imaging assessment is recommended.

There is no pathologic enlargement of central lymph nodes by size criteria.
Pericardial effusion is small.  Main and right pulmonary artery are
mildly enlarged.  There is no pleural effusion. Two tiny nodules in the
inferior segment of the lingula, 4:125 and 127 are
probably tiny mucoid impactions.  Lungs are otherwise clear and
tracheobronchial tree otherwise normal to the subsegmental level.


IMPRESSION:
1.  Ascending thoracic aorta is not appreciably calcified, normal caliber.
Heavy atherosclerotic calcification present in the innominate artery at its
origin and bifurcation, in all coronary branches, in the visceral arteries of
the abdomen and abdominal aorta which may be aneurysmal, and warrants imaging
evaluation, if not already known.

2.  Calcific aortic stenosis.  Possible pulmonary hypertension.  Small
pericardial effusion.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  Evaluate for interval change in a patient with worsening
shortness of breath, status post bronchoscopy yesterday and recent Y stent
placed in the carina.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic iodinated contrast agent reconstructed as
contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick axial, 2.5-mm thick coronal and parasagittal,
and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 6.0 s, 38.9 cm; CTDIvol = 6.6 mGy (Body) DLP = 250.7
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 251 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CT chest from ___.

FINDINGS: 

Again seen is consolidation in the left lower lobe, likely a combination of
atelectasis and aspiration pneumonia/pneumonitis.  This is improved compared
to prior CT chest.  Peribronchiolar nodularity at the right lung base is
increased.  There is new ground-glass opacity involving the right upper lobe,
consistent with an infectious or inflammatory process.  There has been
interval placement of a tracheal stent, which is patent.  Bronchial wall
thickening compatible with bronchitis is increased compared to CT from 5 days
prior.  There is no concerning pulmonary nodule, pleural effusion, or
pneumothorax.

The thyroid gland is unremarkable.  There is no axillary or lymph node
enlargement by CT size criteria.  A partially calcified 5.6 x 9.1 cm posterior
mediastinal mass is again seen, unchanged compared to most recent CT chest
allowing for differences in planes of scanning.  An esophageal stent has been
placed in the interim, with aerosolized material filling the lumen.

There is no focal lytic or sclerotic osseous lesion to suggest neoplasm or
infection.

This exam is not optimized for evaluation of infra diaphragmatic structures. 
Within these limitations, the visualized upper abdomen is remarkable only for
cholelithiasis.

IMPRESSION:


1. New ground-glass opacity of the right upper lobe and increased
peribronchiolar nodularity at the right lung base, compatible with sequela of
aspiration and/or hemorrhage after bronchoscopy.  Improvement of left lower
lobe consolidation.
2. Unchanged posterior mediastinal mass, now with patent airways after
tracheal stenting.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CTA CHEST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with hypoxia, +DOE, leukocytosis POD ___ s/p L
TKA// r/o PE

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector CT of the pulmonary arteries

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Sequenced Acquisition 0.5 s, 0.2 cm; CTDIvol = 7.2 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.4
mGy-cm.
   2) Stationary Acquisition 4.3 s, 0.2 cm; CTDIvol = 71.7 mGy (Body) DLP =
14.3 mGy-cm.
   3) Spiral Acquisition 4.2 s, 27.3 cm; CTDIvol = 9.5 mGy (Body) DLP = 252.1
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 268 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  Prior chest radiograph done ___ at 12:46

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: No supraclavicular or axillary
adenopathy.  No gross breast lesions.

UPPER ABDOMEN: No subdiaphragmatic pathology.

MEDIASTINUM: Subcentimeter mediastinal lymph nodes.

HILA: Subcentimeter hilar lymph nodes.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: Normal cardiac configuration.  No pericardial effusion.
PLEURA: Trace left pleural effusion.
LUNG:

1. PARENCHYMA:  No suspicious pulmonary nodules or masses.  Linear
atelectasis/scarring in the left upper lobe (series 5, image 24).  No
confluent airspace consolidation
2. AIRWAYS:  The airways are patent to the subsegmental level.
3. VESSELS:  The pulmonary artery is mildly dilated measuring 34 mm diameter
suggesting pulmonary hypertension.  No pulmonary arterial filling defect to
suggest pulmonary emboli.
CHEST CAGE: No lytic/destructive bony lesion.  Old lateral right rib
fractures.

IMPRESSION: 

No pulmonary emboli.  No secondary features of pulmonary emboli.

Dilated pulmonary artery suggesting pulmonary hypertension.

No suspicious pulmonary nodules or masses.  No confluent airspace
consolidation.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ male with esophageal cancer status post neoadjuvant
chemo rads and MIE on ___

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic iodinated contrast agent reconstructed as
contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick axial, 2.5-mm thick coronal and parasagittal,
and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Sequenced Acquisition 0.5 s, 0.2 cm; CTDIvol = 9.0 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.8
mGy-cm.
   2) Stationary Acquisition 14.8 s, 0.2 cm; CTDIvol = 240.9 mGy (Body) DLP =
48.2 mGy-cm.
   3) Spiral Acquisition 11.0 s, 71.4 cm; CTDIvol = 10.6 mGy (Body) DLP =
749.6 mGy-cm.
   4) Spiral Acquisition 4.7 s, 30.4 cm; CTDIvol = 11.5 mGy (Body) DLP = 343.5
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 1,143 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND
PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

COMPARISON:  Outside CT torso on ___

FINDINGS: 

Compared with ___, patient has undergone interval esophagectomy and
gastric pull-through with expected postoperative appearance of the right
hemithorax.  The thyroid is normal. There are no pathologically enlarged
supraclavicular, axillary, mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes.

The aorta and pulmonary arteries are normal in size.  The heart is normal in
size and demonstrates no appreciable coronary artery calcifications. There is
no pericardial effusion.

No pneumothorax or pleural effusion is identified. The airways are patent to
the subsegmental level.  There is minimal bibasilar atelectasis.  No focal
consolidation.  No suspicious pulmonary nodules.

There are postsurgical changes in the right posterior ribs.  A punctate
sclerotic focus in the T9 vertebral body is stable, likely representing a bone
island.  No osseous lesions suspicious for infection or malignancy are
identified.

 Please see separate dictation for CT abdomen and pelvis performed on same day
for description of subdiaphragmatic findings including cholelithiasis.

IMPRESSION:


1. Expected postoperative appearance of the chest status post esophagectomy
and gastric pull-through.
2. No evidence of intrathoracic metastatic disease.
3. Please see separate dictation for CT abdomen and pelvis performed on same
day for description of subdiaphragmatic findings including cholelithiasis.

IV contrast// ___ year old man with esophageal cancer s/p neoadjuvant chemorads

administration of intravenous contrast material, multiplanar reconstructions.

upper the anastomosis of the gastric pull-through is unremarkable.  The port 3

scarring.  No unexpected parenchymal changes along side the gastric
pull-through.  Minimal bilateral basal areas of atelectasis.  No suspicious
pulmonary nodules or masses.  No pleural effusions.
