radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ year old man with history of renal cell carcinoma, heart
failure with incidental lung findings on OSH chest CT in ___

TECHNIQUE:  Axial helical MDCT images were obtained from the suprasternal
notch to the upper abdomen without intravenous contrast material. Multiplanar
reformatted images in coronal and sagittal axes were generated.

COMPARISON:  Chest radiograph from ___ and CT from ___.

FINDINGS: 

The thyroid is unremarkable, and there is no supraclavicular lymph node
enlargement. The airways are patent to the subsegmental level.  A right
paratracheal lymph node measures 1.4 x 1.0 cm and is stable from ___
(2:15). The heart is severely enlarged, and there is no pericardial effusion. 
There is a left-sided cardiac pacer with its leads in stable position. No
hiatal hernia or any other esophageal abnormality is present.

Lung windows do not demonstrate any focal opacity.  Non-characteristic
scarring noted within the left upper lobe and right middle and lower lobes are
stable from ___ . No pleural effusion or pneumothorax is present. 
There are no suspicious pulmonary nodules.

BONES: No focal osseous lesion concerning for malignancy.  There is no acute
fracture.

Although this study is not designed for assessment of intra-abdominal
structures, the visualized organs are unremarkable.

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  ___ man with MND disease versus ALS versus paraneoplastic
syndrome.  Evaluate for malignancy.

TECHNIQUE:

Axial MDCT images were acquired from the thoracic inlet to the symphysis pubis
following oral and intravenous contrast.  Coronal and sagittal reformats were
produced and reviewed.

CT CHEST WITH IV CONTRAST:

No enlarged axillary or mediastinal lymph nodes.  The visualized portions of
the thyroid gland are unremarkable.  There is moderate coronary artery
calcification.  Otherwise, the heart and great vessels are unremarkable.  No
pericardial or pleural effusion is seen.  The airways are patent to a
subsegmental level.  There is slight irregularity of the right posterolateral
wall of the trachea above the thoracic inlet with a small amount of mucus
seen.  This appearance could represent a shallow tracheal diverticulum.  There
is bibasilar atelectasis, more marked on the right.  No suspicious pulmonary
nodules.

CT OF THE ABDOMEN WITH IV CONTRAST:

No focal liver lesions.  No biliary duct dilatation.  The gallbladder is
unremarkable in appearance.  The spleen, both adrenal glands and both kidneys
are unremarkable.  The pancreas is unremarkable in appearance.  No mesenteric
or retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy.  No free fluid in the abdomen.

CT OF THE PELVIS WITH IV CONTRAST:

Scattered colonic diverticulosis without diverticulitis.  The urinary bladder
is unremarkable in appearance; however, the distal ureters have an appearance
consistent with bilateral ureteroceles (2:113).  The rectum is unremarkable. 
The prostate gland is enlarged measuring 5.6 x 4 cm; however, this is
unchanged compared to the prior study ___.  No pelvic
lymphadenopathy.  No free fluid.

BONY STRUCTURES:

A spinal fixation device is present at L4 and L5.  There is an unchanged
well-circumscribed lucency in the right iliac bone (2:97).  The appearances
suggest a prior bone graft; however, correlation with patient's clinical
history is advised.  There is a mixed lytic and sclerotic lesion seen in the
left femoral head.  This is also unchanged in appearance since ___.

IMPRESSION:
1.  No primary malignancy identified.
2.  Diverticulosis without diverticulitis.
3.  Possible bilateral ureteroceles.
4.  Stable well-circumscribed lucency in the right iliac bone.  Stable mixed
sclerotic and lucent left femoral head lesion.  Both of these areas are
unchanged since ___.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:
___ woman with seizures and left frontal lobe lesion, infection versus
malignancy.  Evaluate for malignancy.

TECHNIQUE:  Helical axial MDCT images were acquired through the chest as part
of a CT torso exam after the administration of IV contrast.  Reformatted
images in coronal and sagittal axes were generated.  Maximum intensity
projection images were generated on a separate workstation reviewed on PACs.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Stationary Acquisition 6.0 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 28.9 mGy (Body) DLP =
14.4 mGy-cm.
   2) Spiral Acquisition 6.5 s, 71.9 cm; CTDIvol = 12.3 mGy (Body) DLP = 882.8
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 897 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND
PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

COMPARISON:  CTA chest from ___.

FINDINGS: 

The lungs are fairly well-expanded, with dependent atelectasis.  A sub-4 mm
triangular nodular opacity is noted in the right middle lobe (04:104).  No
concerning nodule, mass, or focal consolidation is identified.  No pleural
effusion or pneumothorax is present.

Subcentimeter hypodensities are seen in the left lobe of the thyroid gland. 
There is no supraclavicular, mediastinal, or hilar lymphadenopathy.  Left
axillary lymph nodes are enlarged, but demonstrate normal morphology with
fatty hila.  The heart is mildly enlarged with a small pericardial effusion. 
There is no significant coronary artery calcification.  The aorta is normal in
caliber.  The pulmonary artery is mildly enlarged, measuring 3.1 cm in
diameter.  This is suggestive but not diagnostic of pulmonary artery
hypertension.  There is a 7 mm aneurysm of a left upper lobe artery, and
possible aneurysmal dilation at the origin of the left lower lobe pulmonary
artery, both of which are unchanged compared to ___.  The lower
esophagus is mildly thickened but not hyperemic.

No focal lytic or sclerotic osseous lesion is identified.

Please see the dedicated CT abdomen/pelvis report from the same day for
detailed evaluation of infra diaphragmatic structures.

IMPRESSION:


1. No evidence of intrathoracic malignancy.
2. Small pericardial effusion.
3. Possible pulmonary hypertension.  Two subcentimeter pulmonary artery
aneurysms, stable for at least a year, origin and significance uncertain,
probably not due to an active condition.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  History of renal cell carcinoma with mass in chest.

COMPARISON:  ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Continuous axial images through the chest were obtained without
intravenous contrast at 5 and 1.25 mm collimation.  A set of coronal and
sagittal images were presented for review.

CT CHEST WITHOUT INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST:  The heart and great vessels are
unremarkable.  The bronchi are patent to the subsegmental level bilaterally.
There is evidence of prior right upper lobe wedge resection.  There has been
interval progression of diffuse metastatic disease.  Multiple pulmonary
nodules have increased in size and number.  There are new pleural-based
metastases within the left upper lobe (2:14) and right upper lobe medially
(2:26).  There are no pleural or pericardial effusions.

A 47 x 22 mm expansile destructive soft tissue lesion in the right second rib
is new.  There is extensive sclerosis of the right hemivertebrae at the site
of the involuted T11 soft tissue lesion.

While not tailored for infradiaphragmatic evaluation, a mass in the right
hepatic lobe measures 16 x 13 mm and is similar in size.  There is a small
hiatal hernia.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Interval progression of diffuse pulmonary metastases.
2.  New expansile right second rib lesion.
3.  Stable right hepatic lobe lesion.

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  ___ female with history of endometrial cancer, concerning
for metastasis to the right femur and lungs.  Patient now presenting acutely
with left-sided weakness, mental status changes, and concern for pulmonary
embolism.

COMPARISON:  CT of the chest from ___.

TECHNIQUE:  ___ MDCT-acquired axial images from the thoracic inlet to the upper
abdomen were displayed with 2.5-mm slice thickness.  Intravenous contrast was
administered and axial phase imaging was obtained.  Coronal and sagittal
reformations were prepared.

CT CHEST WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST:

The imaged portion of thyroid gland is homogeneous, without focal nodule.  A
punctate nodule seen on prior examination is not included in the field of view
on the current study.  No supraclavicular or axillary lymphadenopathy is
identified.  Previously seen mediastinal and hilar adenopathy has slightly
increased in size compared to the most recent prior examination.  A large left
hilar node now measures 1.8 x 2.6 cm and previously measured 1.8 x 2.1 cm
(4:45).  A subcarinal lymph node measures 2.0 x 2.7 cm and previously measured
1.9 x 2.2 cm (4:50).  No new mediastinal adenopathy is identified.  The heart
size is normal and there is no pericardial effusion.  The thoracic aorta is
non-aneurysmal and demonstrates no signs of acute aortic syndrome.  The timing
of the contrast bolus is suboptimal, however, no large central pulmonary
embolism is identified.

The tracheobronchial tree is patent to subsegmental levels.  A known solid
nodule within the medial right lower lobe measures 9 x 11 mm and previously
measured 9 x 9 mm.  A left lower lobe pulmonary nodule previously measured 4
mm and now measures 5 mm (4:71).  No new pulmonary nodule or mass is
identified.  There is no pleural effusion.

The imaged portion of the upper abdominal viscera appears within normal
limits.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  No bone destructive lesion or acute fracture is
identified.

IMPRESSION:

1.  Slightly increased mediastinal lymphadenopathy, findings concerning for
worsening metastatic disease from patient's known endometrial cancer.

2.  Slightly increased size of both right and left lower lobe pulmonary
nodules.  No new nodule or mass.

3.  No pleural effusions.

4.  No pulmonary embolism or evidence of acute aortic syndrome.

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  Pleuritic chest pain which radiates to the back, evaluate for the
presence of an aortic dissection or pulmonary embolism.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT axial images were acquired through the chest after the
uneventful administration of 100 mL of Omnipaque during the pulmonary arterial
phase of enhancement.  Coronal and sagittal reformations were provided
reviewed.  Maximum intensity projection images were created and reviewed as
well.

DLP: 432.14 mGy/cm 

COMPARISON:  None.

FINDINGS:

Contrast is seen opacifying the segmental and subsegmental arteries of the
pulmonary vascular tree, without filling defects to suggest a pulmonary
embolus.  The aorta is normal in caliber, without evidence of dissection.  The
left common carotid is incidentally noted to originate from the right
brachiocephalic artery.

The thyroid is normal.  There is no supraclavicular, hilar or mediastinal
lymphadenopathy.  The heart is normal in size.  There is no pericardial
effusion.  The trachea is normal in caliber and airways are patent the
subsegmental level.  There is no pleural effusion or pneumothorax.  There is
no focal consolidation to suggest pneumonia.

The study was not tailored to evaluate subdiaphragmatic contents.  A 7 mm
right adrenal nodule is seen on a background of bilateral adrenal nodularity. 
The imaged kidneys, liver and gallbladder are normal.

There are no suspicious osseous lesions.  Bilateral gynecomastia is noted.

IMPRESSION:

1.  No pulmonary embolus.  

2.  7 mm right adrenal nodule on a background of bilateral adrenal nodularity.
This most likely represents an adenoma but is not fully characterized on this
study.

radiology_facts.report_text:
CTA CHEST

COMPARISON:  ___.

HISTORY:  Chest pain.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT axially acquired images of the chest were obtained. Non-
contrast and post-contrast imaging was performed.  Coronal and sagittal
reformats were obtained.

FINDINGS:  There are prominent bilateral axillary lymph nodes, none of which
meet CT criteria for pathologic enlargement.  Incidental note is made of a
soft tissue density in the anterior mediastinum without discrete mass.  There
are no filling defects within the pulmonary arteries to suggest pulmonary
emboli.  The heart and great vessels are unremarkable.  There is no evidence
of dissection.  There is no focal consolidation or effusion.  There is no
pneumothorax.  Within the right middle lung (3,37), there is a 4-mm pulmonary
nodule.  No other nodules are identified.

Limited views of the upper abdomen demonstrate multiple gallstones, stable in
appearance when compared to prior exam.  A subcentimeter hypodensity in the
right lobe of the liver is also identified which is too small to characterize,
although slightly increased when compared to prior exam.

BONE WINDOWS:  There are no suspicious lytic or sclerotic lesions identified.

IMPRESSION:
1.  No evidence of PE or dissection.

2.  Prominent anterior soft tissue density within the anterior
pneumomediastinum.  This may represent thymic hyperplasia.  No discrete masses
identified.  Clinical correlation is recommended, and further workup may be
warranted.

3.  Cholelithiasis, stable.

4.  Subcentimeter right lobe liver hypodensity, too small to characterize,
although slightly increased when compared to prior exam.

5.  4 mm pulmonary nodule in right middle lobe. If high risk patient, needs
follow up in one year.

There are prominent bilateral axillary lymph nodes, none of which
meet CT criteria for pathologic enlargement.  Incidental note is made of a
soft tissue density in the anterior mediastinum without discrete mass.  There
are no filling defects within the pulmonary arteries to suggest pulmonary
emboli.  The heart and great vessels are unremarkable.  There is no evidence
of dissection.  There is no focal consolidation or effusion.  There is no
pneumothorax.  Within the right middle lung (3,37), there is a 4-mm pulmonary
nodule.  No other nodules are identified.

Limited views of the upper abdomen demonstrate multiple gallstones, stable in
appearance when compared to prior exam.  A subcentimeter hypodensity in the
right lobe of the liver is also identified which is too small to characterize,
although slightly increased when compared to prior exam.

BONE WINDOWS:  There are no suspicious lytic or sclerotic lesions identified.

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  ___ old female for evaluation of potential lesion.

COMPARISONS:  Recent CT ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Non-contrast biometric MDCT imaging was performed from the
thoracic inlet to the upper abdomen.  Axial reconstructed images were
displayed in 5 mm and 1.25-mm collimation.  Coronal and sagittal reformatted
images were also provided.

FINDINGS:  Study is mildly limited secondary to respiratory motion.  Bilateral
small pleural effusions, right greater than left are both smaller compared to
the CT ___.  There is minimal associated atelectasis.  No focal areas
of consolidation are evident.  There are no pulmonary nodules or masses.
Minimal biapical pleural parenchymal scarring is stable.  The airways are
patent to the subsegmental level.

Moderate cardiomegaly is unchanged.  Severe calcifications involving the left
anterior descending, left circumflex, and coronary arteries are similarly
unchanged.  Mild to moderate calcification of the aortic valve is stable.
There is no pericardial effusion.  Moderate calcifications involving the
ascending aorta are additionally evident.  Caliber of the main pulmonary
artery is within normal limits.  A precarinal 9 mm lymph node, allowing for
slight differences in technique, is unchanged (2:20).  There are no
pathologically enlarged mediastinal, hilar, or axillary lymph nodes.

Please note this study was not optimized for subdiaphragmatic assessment.  A
large hiatal hernia with associated organoaxial gastric volvulus is unchanged.
A small amount of dense material is seen within the dependent stomach portion
consistent with retained barium from previous evaluations.  Imaged portions of
the upper abdomen are otherwise grossly unremarkable.  There are no suspicious
osseous lesions for malignancy.  Moderate degenerative changes and
dextroscoliosis of the lower thoracic spine are likewise stable.

IMPRESSION:

1.  Interval decrease in size of bilateral pleural effusions with associated
atelectasis compared to ___ CT.  No consolidation or pulmonary nodule.

2.  Unchanged appearance of large hiatal hernia with organoaxial gastric
volvulus, partially assessed.

3.  Severe coronary artery calcifications and mild to moderate aortic valvular
calcification.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with recent admission to hospital for chest pain,
dyspnea, syncope. Has left sided pleural effusion and RML pneumonia that has
been slowly resolving  // Please evaluate for infiltrates, effusions, masses.

TECHNIQUE:  Multi detector helical scanning of the chest was reconstructed as
5 and 1.25 mm thick axial, 2.5 mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 mm MIP
axial images. Contrast agent was not administered.

DOSAGE:  TOTAL DLP 277.7mGy-cm

COMPARISON:  Read in conjunction with conventional chest radiographs ___.  No prior chest CTs are available.

FINDINGS: 

Supraclavicular nodes are not pathologically enlarged ; axillary lymph nodes
are difficult to separate from mildly dilated axillary veins on this
noncontrast study but may reach 10 mm bilaterally. Subcutaneous 10 mm wide
nodule in the left paramedian anterior chest, at the level of the intra
pericardial right pulmonary artery, 02:31, is a nonspecific finding, but
usually a sebaceous cyst. There are no other soft tissue lesions in the chest
wall suspicious for malignancy. Gynecomastia is moderate.

This study is not designed for subdiaphragmatic diagnosis, but shows
normal-size adrenal glands and a cyst in the upper pole left kidney and 2
cysts in the liver. Heterogeneity in the left lobe of the liver it is
nondiagnostic, 2: 53 through 62 and should be evaluated with hepatic
ultrasound.

Right thyroid lobe has been resected.  Left is homogeneous. Mediastinal lymph
nodes are not pathologically enlarged ranging in diameter up to 9 mm in the
right lower paratracheal station, 2: 26. Fusiform dilatation of the non
calcified ascending thoracic aorta reaches maximum diameter 46 mm. Aortic
caliber in the arch is 36 mm, in the descending aorta to the level of the
diaphragm, 29 mm. Aortic valvular calcification is moderate. Atherosclerotic
coronary calcification is also moderate, predominantly in the left anterior
descending branch. Intracardiac calcification in the left ventricle is
probably an indication of previous papillary muscle infarction, but there may
be mitral leaflet calcification as well. Heart is top-normal size. 
Hypoattenuation of cardiac contents indicates some anemia.

Small nonhemorrhagic left pleural effusion layers posteriorly. There is no
clear pleural nodulation.

An elliptical peripheral lung lesion in the right upper lobe, containing
punctate calcification, 10 x 20 mm at the level of its greatest axial
cross-sectional area, 04:59 the tracks the pleura, as demonstrated by widening
of the subcostal fat and is presumably a scar. Adjacent small bronchi are
mildly thickened. A similar subpleural lesion against the right major fissure,
in the right middle lobe also has punctate calcifications and thickened
bronchi in the vicinity. A larger, calcified granuloma is found in the right
lower lobe.

Pleural parenchymal scarring at the left apex is identifiable by expansion of
the extra pleural fat.

There are no bone findings in the chest wall suspicious for malignancy.


IMPRESSION: 

No radiographic findings in the chest suspicious for malignancy or active
infection. Postinflammatory scarring is present in the right upper lobe, right
middle lobe, and left lung apex.

Moderate aortic valvular calcification could be stenosing, and would explain
mild fusiform dilatation ascending thoracic aorta.

Probable post infarction papillary muscle calcification, possible mitral
leaflet calcification.

Small layering nonhemorrhagic left pleural effusion, without obvious
explanation, present since at least ___.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with left effusion s/p ___ 900mL removedh/o PNA
in ___ small effusion at that time, p/w chest pain in ___ (negative cards
workup)  // ? underlying etiology of effusion

TECHNIQUE:   Multi detector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic iodinated contrast agent and reconstructed as
5 and 1.25 mm thick axial, 2.5 mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 mm MIP
axial images. All images were reviewed.
DOSAGE:  TOTAL DLP 365.0mGy-cm

COMPARISON:  CHEST CT SCANNING SINCE ___ MOST RECENTLY ___.

FINDINGS: 

7 mm wide low attenuation lymph node in the left axilla, 02:13, is unchanged
since ___.  There is no pathologic enlargement of lymph nodes elsewhere
in the supraclavicular or axillary stations and no soft tissue abnormality in
the chest wall suspicious for malignancy.  This study is not designed for
subdiaphragmatic diagnosis but shows well- circumscribed low-attenuation
lesions in the imaged portion of the liver are unchanged since ___,
most likely cysts. The adrenals are normal size

Right thyroid lobe is been resected. Left is unremarkable.  Atherosclerotic
calcification is mild in the coronaries, present in at least the left anterior
descending branch. Borderline enlargement ascending thoracic aorta, 41 mm,
unchanged since ___, was 42 mm in ___. Pulmonary arteries are
normal size.  Previous cardiomegaly may have improved.  Pericardium is
physiologic.

There is only minimal residual left pleural effusion decreased substantially
since ___ and there is no pleural thickening suspicious for malignancy
or active inflammation.  Central lymph nodes are not pathologically enlarged.


Geographic soft tissue lesion in the right upper lobe containing eccentric
calcification and extending to thickened pleura, 04:45- 59 is smaller than it
was in ___, and is associated with retraction of extrapleural fat,
both indications of a maturing scar. Adjacent to it is another calcified
nodule. The combination suggests prior tuberculosis. There is no evidence of
active infection.  A cluster of bronchiolar nodules in the posterior segment
of the right upper lobe, 4:93- 101 is stable since ___. Additional
calcifications in chronic atelectasis in the right middle lobe, has improved
and isolated granulomatous calcifications are present in multiple other
pulmonary site as well.

A large serpentine left apical scar is stable since ___. Atelectasis
in the inferior subsegment of the lingula is chronic, and the small region of
atelectasis in the posterior basal segment of the left lower lobe is to be
expected given the long history of the since resolved left pleural effusion.
All in all there are no lung lesions suspicious for malignancy or active
infection.

There are no bone lesions in the chest cage suspicious for malignancy. Vacuum
discs at multiple levels in the thoracic spine, including intervertebral disc
intrusions, are due to disk degeneration.

IMPRESSION: 

Left pleural effusion essentially resolved, with no sequelae, or indication of
associated pathology.  No evidence of intrathoracic malignancy or active
infection or inflammation.

Multi focal scarring and granulomatous calcifications, probably in indication
of prior tuberculosis, and/or histoplasmosis. No evidence of reactivation.

Borderline enlarged ascending thoracic aorta stable than a year.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  Evaluation of patient with history of gallbladder adenocarcinoma.

COMPARISON:  CT abdomen and pelvis from outside hospital from ___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT-acquired axial images were obtained through the abdomen prior
to administration of IV contrast.  Following the administration of 150 cc of
IV contrast, MDCT-acquired axial images of the abdomen in multiple phases
including arterial, portal venous, and delayed phases as per liver CTA
protocol.  Delayed images were also obtained through the chest and pelvis. 
Multiplanar reformatted images were prepared.

FINDINGS:

CT OF THE CHEST WITH IV CONTRAST:  Post-surgical changes are visualized in the
left axilla and left breast along with a soft tissue nodule in the left
breast, consistent with prior surgery.  Furthermore, post-radiation changes
are visualized in the left upper lobe.  There is a prominent 7-mm precarinal
lymph node (6:19) which does not meet pathologic criteria for enlargement and
there is no pathologic hilar, mediastinal, or axillary lymphadenopathy by CT
size criteria.  Aside from the previously mentioned left upper lobe opacities
suggestive of radiation fibrosis, the remainder of the lungs are clear with no
evidence of nodules or consolidates.  The visualized portions of heart and
great vessels are within normal limits.

CT OF THE ABDOMEN WITH AND WITHOUT IV CONTRAST:  Post-cholecystectomy changes
are visualized in the gallbladder fossa.  Otherwise, the liver is within
normal limits with no evidence of focal distinct lesions.  The portal vein,
splenic vein, superior mesenteric vein, and hepatic veins are widely patent.

The stomach, visualized loops of small and large bowel, pancreas, bilateral
adrenal glands, and spleen are within normal limits.  Two subcentimeter
hypodense lesions which are too small to characterize are visualized
throughout the interpolar region of the right kidney; otherwise, the kidneys
enhance symmetrically and excrete promptly and are within normal limits.

Atherosclerotic changes are visualized throughout the abdominal aorta, but the
aorta is normal in caliber and contour.  No mesenteric or retroperitoneal
lymphadenopathy.  No free fluid or free air throughout the abdomen.

CT OF THE PELVIS WITH IV CONTRAST:  The visualized portions of the bladder,
rectum, and sigmoid colon are within normal limits.  No pelvic or inguinal
lymphadenopathy by CT size criteria.  No free fluid or free air throughout the
pelvis.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  No suspicious lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions.

IMPRESSION:
1.  No evidence of metastatic disease in the chest, abdomen, or pelvis.
2.  No evidence of gross invasion of liver with any focal masses.
3.  Post-surgical changes are visualized at the left breast and axilla with
radiation fibrosis at the left upper lobe.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ woman with six-month cognitive decline, anorexia and
weight loss.  Has atypical blood smear an elevated LDH.  Rule out lymphoma.

TECHNIQUE:   Multi-detector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic, iodinated contrast agent, reconstructed as
contiguous 5 mm and 1.0 or 1.25 mm thick axial, 2.5 or 5 mm thick coronal and
parasagittal, and 8 mm MIP axial images. Sequential scanning of the abdomen
and pelvis  will be reported separately. Images of the chest were reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Sequenced Acquisition 0.5 s, 0.2 cm; CTDIvol = 9.3 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.9
mGy-cm.
   2) Stationary Acquisition 0.8 s, 0.2 cm; CTDIvol = 13.7 mGy (Body) DLP =
2.7 mGy-cm.
   3) Spiral Acquisition 9.8 s, 63.4 cm; CTDIvol = 5.7 mGy (Body) DLP = 358.2
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 363 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND
PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

COMPARISON:  No prior chest CT available for comparison.

FINDINGS: 

FINDINGS:

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: No thyroid lesions.  No
supraclavicular or axillary adenopathy.  No gross breast lesions.

UPPER ABDOMEN: Will be reported separately.  Small hiatal hernia.  Note is
made of increased retroperitoneal soft tissue at the level of the celiac axis
and SMA.

MEDIASTINUM: No mediastinal adenopathy.

HILA: No hilar adenopathy.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: Normal cardiac configuration.  No pericardial effusion.
Mild aortic annular calcification.  Moderate coronary artery calcification. 
No aneurysmal dilatation of the ascending aorta.  Mild atherosclerotic changes
of the aortic arch and supra-aortic vessels.
PLEURA: No pleural effusion.
LUNG:

-PARENCHYMA:  Motion artifact obscures the lower lung zones for small
pulmonary nodules and fine interstitial changes.  Mild to moderate
centrilobular emphysematous changes.  No suspicious pulmonary nodules or
masses.  No confluent airspace consolidation.  Mild subsegmental atelectasis
in the lingula.
-AIRWAYS:  The airways are patent to the subsegmental level.  Mild, but
diffuse bronchial wall thickening.  No bronchiectasis.
-VESSELS:  The pulmonary artery measures at the upper limits of normal.  No
filling defects on this nondedicated study.
CHEST CAGE: Spondylotic changes of the thoracic spine.  No lytic/ destructive
bony lesions.  Scoliotic changes of the thoracolumbar spine.

IMPRESSION: 

No supraclavicular, axillary or mediastinal adenopathy.  No suspicious
pulmonary nodules or masses.

Mild to moderate centrilobular emphysematous changes with associated diffuse
bronchial wall thickening suggests smoking related changes.

Note is made of increased retroperitoneal soft tissue at the level of the
celiac axis and SMA.
For abdominal findings please refer to CT abdomen report.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  Staging for newly diagnosed right breast cancer with positive
lymph nodes status post bilateral mastectomies with ___ flap.

COMPARISON:  No prior studies available for comparison.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT contrast-enhanced axial images were obtained from the
thoracic inlet to the pelvic outlet.  Coronal and sagittal reformations were
performed.  Additional non-contrast axial images were obtained from the lung
bases to the pelvic brim.

FINDINGS:

CT CHEST:  Post-surgical changes are noted in the bilateral breasts secondary
to bilateral ___ flaps.  A 2.3 x 2.4 cm high-density collection is noted in
the medial aspect of the left breast as well as a second fusiform collection
is identified in the left posterior lateral breast measuring approximately 10
x 1.7 cm, both likely reflecting a postoperative seromas.  Locules of gas and
inflammation are noted throughout the subcutaneous tissues of the abdomen
consistent with surgical history.  The lungs are clear without focal
opacification or suspicious pulmonary nodules.  There is no hilar,
mediastinal, or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy identified.  Multiple
prominent lymph nodes are noted in the bilateral axilla, left greater than
right; however, none meet CT criteria for pathologic enlargement.  Heart size
is normal without pericardial effusion.  The great vessels appear
unremarkable.  The airways are patent to the subsegmental level.  No pleural
effusions are evident.

CT ABDOMEN:  Liver is homogenous in parenchymal attenuation without distinct
masses or lesions.  There is no intrahepatic biliary ductal dilatation.
Gallbladder is unremarkable.  There is no common bile duct dilatation.  The
pancreas and spleen are unremarkable.  The bilateral adrenal glands have
normal limb thickness without convex margin to suggest mass.  The bilateral
kidneys are normal in size.  The stomach, small and large bowel are
unremarkable.  There is no retroperitoneal, mesenteric or portacaval
lymphadenopathy identified.  There is no free fluid within the abdomen.
Minimal atherosclerotic changes identified within the aorta, without
associated aneurysmal change.  The celiac axis and superior mesenteric artery
are patent.  The portal venous system is patent.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  There no suspicious lytic or blastic lesions identified.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Expected post-surgical changes secondary to bilateral mastectomy and ___
flaps including subcutaneous emphysema and two seromas in the left breast.  No
evidence of metastatic disease.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  Chronic cough, hemoptysis, clear x-ray.

COMPARISON:  None.

TECHNIQUE:  Helical CT acquisitions from top of lungs through upper abdomen
without intravenous contrast.  Multiplanar reformations were reviewed.

CT OF THE CHEST WITHOUT INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST:  Small amount of thymic
hyperplasia is present.  There is no mediastinal or hilar adenopathy.  Heart
and the great vessels are unremarkable.  Minimal aortic arch calcification is
present.

The tracheobronchial tree is patent to subsegmental level.  Lungs are clear. 
Pleural surfaces normal.

Imaged upper abdomen demonstrates nodular densities in the left upper quadrant
compatible with splenules.  Otherwise grossly unremarkable.

There are no suspicious intraosseous lesions.

IMPRESSION:  
1.  Normal chest CT.  No abnormalities to explain patient's symptoms.
2.  Minimal atherosclerotic aortic calcification is present.
3.  Prominent thymic tissues compatible with thymic hyperplasia. Given the
age, MR can be performed to confirm this diagnosis.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ male with MGUS and new abnormal flow showing presence
of clonal T cell population. Evaluate for malignancy.

TECHNIQUE:  Contrast-enhanced chest CT was performed acquiring sequential
axial images from the thoracic inlet through the adrenal glands. Thin section
axial, coronal, sagittal and axial MIP's were also obtained. 130 cc of
Omnipaque 350 were administered intravenously without reported complication.

DOSE:  As per CT abdomen/pelvis.

COMPARISON:  ___.

FINDINGS: 

The thyroid gland is unremarkable. There are no pathologically enlarged
supraclavicular, mediastinal, hilar or axillary lymph nodes.  There is a
stable small amount of residual thymic tissue in the anterior mediastinum.

Heart size is top-normal with no pericardial effusion. The main pulmonary
artery and thoracic aorta are normal caliber.  No incidental central pulmonary
embolus is identified.

There is no pulmonary nodule, mass or consolidation. No endobronchial lesion
or pleural abnormality is identified. Previous bilateral areas of dependent
nodular atelectasis have resolved.

For a detailed discussion of the upper abdomen, please refer to the separate
report from the CT abdomen/pelvis performed concurrently.

There are no bony lesions in the thorax worrisome for infection or malignancy.

IMPRESSION: 

No evidence of intrathoracic malignancy.

radiology_facts.report_text:
CT CHEST, ___

COMPARISON:  Chest radiograph, ___ and comparison CT abdomen, ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Volumetric, multidetector CT was performed without intravenous or
oral contrast.  Images are presented at 5 mm and 1.25 mm collimation.

FINDINGS:  Moderate, predominantly dependent bilateral simple pleural
effusions are present.  The right effusion is similar to ___ and the
left effusion has slightly increased since that time.  Additionally, extensive
bilateral calcified pleural plaques are present, consistent with prior
asbestos exposure.  In addition to a dominant dependent distribution, the
right effusion has a small loculated component laterally in the upper right
hemithorax (for example, image 20, series 2).  Heart is mildly enlarged, and
note is made of diffuse coronary artery calcifications as well as the presence
of aortic valvular calcifications.

Both effusions are associated with adjacent atelectasis in the lower lobes. 
Assessment of the remainder of the lungs is limited due to extensive
respiratory motion and inadvertent expiratory phase of respiration.  This
limits assessment for small pulmonary nodules and subtle diffuse lung
abnormalities.  Within the right upper lobe centrally is an approximately 2.2
x 1.2 cm diameter peribronchovascular area of consolidation with surrounding
ground-glass opacity.  This is contiguous with a linear opacity more
inferiorly.  Multifocal band-like opacities are present in both lungs,
predominantly adjacent to pleural plaques.

Skeletal structures demonstrate no suspicious lytic or blastic lesions within
the thorax.  Widespread degenerative changes are present throughout the spine.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Moderate bilateral pleural effusions, with interval increase in left
effusion since recent abdominal CT ___.

2.  2 cm nodular focus of consolidation and ground-glass centrally in right
upper lobe, which could potentially represent a round focus of pneumonia, but
primary lung malignancy is an important consideration in this patient with
evidence of previous asbestos exposure.  If warranted clinically, followup CT
could be performed in eight weeks after completion of antibiotic therapy.

3.  Please see recent abdominal CT ___ for complete description of
abdominal findings.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ man with p-ANCA positive vasculitis and acute kidney
injury.

TECHNIQUE:  High-resolution CT of chest was performed using a standard
protocol.  Images were acquired both end inspiration, expiration, in supine
and prone position.

No prior chest CT was available for comparison.

LUNGS AND AIRWAYS:  Central airways are patent till subsegmental level.  Due
to motion artifacts in the lower lungs, the sensitivity for detection of fine
details is limited.  Band-like atelectasis is seen in the right lower lobe. 
There is no evidence of interstitial thickening within the lungs free of
motion artifacts.  There are no lung findings related to or as a sequela to
vasculitis. No evidence of lung hemorrhage/consolidation/pleural effusion.  On
expiration HRCT, there is no airtrapping.  There are no lung nodules of
concern.

MEDIASTINUM:  Thyroid gland is normal.  Heart size is normal and there is no
pericardial effusion.  Pulmonary artery is 24 mm in caliber and normal.

ABDOMEN:  The study is not tailored for evaluation of abdomen; however,
limited views were remarkable for a tiny 8-mm left adrenal nodule, which has
attenuation values characteristics for adenoma.

BONES:  There is no bone lesion suspicious for malignancy/infection. 

IMPRESSION:  

1.Due to motion artifacts, the evaluation of the lower lungs is limited;
within the lungs free of motion artifact, there is no evidence to suggest
interstitial abnormalities or any findings as a sequela to the clinical
history of vasculitis.  

2.Atelectasis in the dependent parts of right lung base.  

3.Left adrenal nodule with attenuation characteristics of adenoma.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CTA chest

INDICATION:  ___ man status post sigmoid colectomy who was reintubated
for respiratory failure and has spiking fevers.  Evaluate for pulmonary
embolus.

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of 130 cc of Omnipaque intravenous
contrast.
Reformatted coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal
intensity projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  DLP: 134 2.32 mGy-cm

COMPARISON:  No prior cross-sectional imaging of the chest is available on
PACs at the time of this dictation.  Limited reference is made with the CT
abdomen and pelvis dated ___.

FINDINGS: 

The aorta and its major branch vessels are patent, with no evidence of
stenosis, occlusion, dissection, or aneurysmal formation.  The heart is
enlarged.  Diffuse coronary artery calcifications or moderate on this
nondedicated exam.  Aortic valve calcifications are mild-to-moderate.  Mitral
non was calcifications are mild.  No evidence of a pericardial effusion.  A
right subclavian vein approach PICC tip ends in the distal SVC.

Central filling defects in right upper lobar, segmental, and subsegmental
pulmonary arteries are consistent with acute pulmonary emboli (e.g., series 3,
image 99, 98, 92, 111, 70, 80).  The main pulmonary artery is normal in
caliber.  The right pulmonary artery is top-normal in caliber measuring up to
27 mm.  The left pulmonary artery is normal in caliber.  No evidence of right
heart strain.

Axillary lymph nodes are prominent but not pathologically enlarged.  No
supraclavicular lymphadenopathy.  Multiple mediastinal lymph nodes are
measurable up to 8 mm in short axis in the upper paratracheal station and 11
mm in short axis in the subcarinal station, possibly reactive (series 3, image
57).  No hilar lymphadenopathy.

A trace right pleural effusion is nonhemorrhagic and has markedly decreased in
size since ___.  Associated dependent right lung relaxation atelectasis
is minimal in also markedly improved.  A small left nonhemorrhagic pleural
effusion persists and is minimally changed.  Left lower lobe segmental and
subsegmental bronchi are opacified and obstructed with mucous secretions with
associated heterogeneously enhancing parenchymal opacity that likely reflects
a combination of atelectasis and postobstructive pneumonia given the provided
clinical history.

The patient is intubated with the ETT in standard position.  No evidence of
pneumothorax.

Detailed evaluation of the lung parenchyma for nodules is markedly limited
secondary to respiratory and cardiac motion artifact. A left apex pulmonary
micronodule measures 2 mm (Series 3, image 40; series 601b, image 37).  Linear
perifissural opacity in the right upper lobe is likely atelectasis (series
601B, image 41).

The thyroid gland is normal in size and enhances normally other than a
millimetric a hypodense nodule in the right lobe of doubtful clinical
significance (series 3, image 32).

An enteric tube is incompletely imaged in the esophagus coursing into the
stomach in the left upper quadrant of the abdomen.  This exam is not dedicated
for imaging of the abdomen.  Limited views of the upper abdomen are grossly
unremarkable.

No osseous lesions concerning for malignancy or infection in the chest cage. 
Multi-level degenerative changes of the thoracic spine are moderate with
prominent anterior osteophytes.  No evidence of acute fracture.

IMPRESSION:


1. Acute pulmonary embolus in right upper pulmonary lobar, segmental, and
subsegmental arteries.
2. Mucous secretions obstructing left lower lobe segmental bronchi with
associated atelectasis and post-obstructive pneumonia.
3. Small left and trace right non-hemorrhagic pleural effusions, unchanged on
the left and markedly improved on the right from ___.
4. Interval improvement in right lower lobe atelectasis since ___.
5. At least moderate calcified coronary artery disease on this nondedicated
exam.
6. Cardiomegaly.
7. Aortic valve calcifications.
8. 2-mm left apex pulmonary micronodule.  If the patient is low risk, no
follow-up is needed.  If the patient is high risk, dedicated chest CT in ___
year is recommended.

RECOMMENDATION(S):   If the patient is low risk, no follow-up is needed for a
sub- 4 mm pulmonary micronodule.  If the patient is high risk, dedicated chest
CT in ___ year is recommended.

NOTIFICATION:  The findings were discussed with ___, M.D. by
___, M.D. on the telephone on ___ at 2:15 ___, 1 minutes after
discovery of the findings.  Findings were also discussed with Dr. ___
___ on the telephone on ___ at 218 pm.

radiology_facts.report_text:
CT CHEST, ___

COMPARISON:  ___

INDICATION:  Diffuse interstitial lung disease and pulmonary nodules.

COMPARISON:  CT CHEST, ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector CT of the chest was performed without intravenous or
oral contrast.  Images were obtained in contiguous helical fashion at both
end-inspiration and end-expiration with images were presented for display in
the axial plane at 5-mm and 1.25-mm collimation.  Coronal reformation images
were also submitted for review from the series.  Additionally, a series of
prone axial high-resolution CT images are also presented for review.

FINDINGS:  Two previously reported new right upper lobe ground-glass nodules
have resolved.  Diffuse interstitial lung disease is again demonstrated with a
subpleural pattern of reticulation accompanied by traction bronchiectasis and
bronchiolectasis.  Although diffusely distributed, lower lungs are involved to
a greater degree than the upper and mid lung.  Tiny foci of peripheral
calcification or ossification are identified in association with the
interstitial lung abnormalities, involving the right lung to a greater degree
than the left.

As compared to the previous CT scan, the interstitial lung disease appears
similar in distribution and severity to the prior scan.  Borderline
mediastinal lymph nodes are without change and are likely related to diffuse
lung disease.  The heart size is normal.  There has been previous aortic valve
replacement and coronary bypass surgery with diffuse calcifications of the
native coronary arteries noted.  Heart is upper limits of normal in size
without change.  No pericardial or pleural effusion is identified.  Small
hiatal hernia is incidentally noted.

Examination was not specifically tailored to evaluate the subdiaphragmatic
region, but note is made of dependent gallstones within the gallbladder and
diffuse vascular calcifications in the abdominal aorta and its branches,
including the left renal artery.  Please note, absence of intravenous and oral
contrast limits sensitivity for detecting abdominal abnormalities.

No suspicious lytic or blastic skeletal lesions are identified.

A moderate degree of air-trapping is demonstrated on expiratory CT images.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Interval resolution of right upper lobe ground-glass nodules, which were
likely inflammatory or infectious.

2.  No change in subpleural and basilar predominant diffuse interstitial lung
disease, likely due to UIP or a fibrotic subtype of NSIP.

3.  Tiny peripheral calcific or ossific densities are unchanged, and likely
represent idiopathic ossification associated with diffuse interstitial lung
disease.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with low grade fevers, tacycardia, increased
hypoxia..here as COVID r/o  // Eval for PE, and to see if anything suspicious
of potential COVID

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images of the chest obtained without the
administration of IV contrast given acute renal injury.  Coronal and sagittal
reformats were provided. Maximum intensity projections were also acquired.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 1.8 s, 27.9 cm; CTDIvol = 17.9 mGy (Body) DLP = 500.1
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 500 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  ___ F FDG PET-CT ___, chest radiographs from ___.

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: Thyroid gland is incompletely
evaluated.  Numerous nonenlarged lymph nodes are seen in the thoracic inlet. 
Collateral veins are noted in the subcutaneous tissues of the anterior chest
wall.

UPPER ABDOMEN: Limited sections through the upper abdomen demonstrate nodular
contours of the liver and multiple hypodensities measuring up to 3.9 x 2.6
predominantly in the right hepatic lobe are nonspecific however unchanged from
___ PET-CT and non- FDG avid.  Small amount of perihepatic ascites
and splenomegaly.

MEDIASTINUM: A epicardial lymph node has increased in size currently measuring
1.1 cm, previously 0.8 cm in ___.

HILA: No hilar adenopathy.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: Heart is mildly enlarged.  There is small amount of
pericardial effusion.
PLEURA: Trace left pleural effusion.  No right pleural effusion.  No pleural
thickening.
LUNG:

1. PARENCHYMA:  Bilateral subsegmental lower lobe and lingular atelectasis. 
There is diffuse bronchial wall thickening.  No parenchymal opacities or
pulmonary nodules.
2. AIRWAYS:  The airways are patent to subsegmental level.  No bronchiectasis
mucous plugging or bronchial wall thickening
3. VESSELS:  Thoracic aorta is normal in size.  The main pulmonary artery is
measures up to 3.6 cm.

CHEST CAGE: No suspicious bony lesions or acute fractures.

IMPRESSION:


1. Bilateral subsegmental lower lobe and lingular atelectasis and trace left
pleural effusion.
2. Pulmonary embolism cannot be evaluated given the lack of contrast.
3. Small amount of ascites and pericardial effusion.
4. Stigmata of chronic hepatic disease includes nodular contours of the liver
and collateral circulation.  Stable nonspecific hypodensities throughout the
liver.

RECOMMENDATION(S):  CTA chest for evaluation of pulmonary embolus

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST WITH CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year-old man with PMH of Caroli disease with cirrhosis c/b HE,
ascites, and esophageal varices on the transplant list; recurrent cholangitis
and multiple MDR infections; prostate cancer ___ 3+4) s/p Cyberknife
___, Afib and PE not on anticoagulation, presenting with GNR bacteremia
with no clear source.  // Please evaluate for abscess/infection

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.  Reformatted
coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and axial maximal intensity
projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 5.4 s, 71.9 cm; CTDIvol = 22.7 mGy (Body) DLP =
1,631.3 mGy-cm.
   2) Stationary Acquisition 0.6 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 3.4 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.7
mGy-cm.
   3) Stationary Acquisition 6.0 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 33.7 mGy (Body) DLP =
16.8 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 1,650 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND
PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

COMPARISON:  CT chest ___.

FINDINGS: 

HEART AND VASCULATURE: Thoracic aorta is normal caliber. Main pulmonary artery
is enlarged measuring up to 3.3 cm. There is suggestion of a possible filling
defect within the left anterior segmental pulmonary artery (series 302, image
83). Heart size is mildly enlarged. No evidence of right heart strain. No
pericardial effusion. No pathologic aortic valvular calcifications. There are
mild coronary artery calcifications.

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM: No axillary, supraclavicular, mediastinal or
hilar lymphadenopathy. Esophagus appears normal by CT. No mediastinal mass.

PLEURAL SPACES: There are trace pleural effusions. No pneumothorax.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS: The lung volumes are low with persistent elevation of the right
hemidiaphragm with resultant right lower lobe volume loss. There is associated
bibasilar and lingular atelectasis. No evidence of pneumonia. Central airways
are patent. There are no suspicious pulmonary nodules.

BASE OF NECK: Visualized portions of the base of the neck show no abnormality.

ABDOMEN: Please refer to separately dictated CT abdomen/pelvis.

BONES: No worrisome osseous lesion.

IMPRESSION:


1. No evidence of active infection within the chest.
2. Suggestion of a filling defect within the left anterior segmental pulmonary
artery suspicious for a pulmonary emboli versus mixing artifact. Dedicated
CTPA is recommended for further assessment.
3. Low lung volumes with persistent elevation of the right hemidiaphragm and
bibasilar atelectasis.

NOTIFICATION:  The findings were discussed with ___, M.D. by
___, M.D. on the telephone on ___ at 11:27 am, 5 minutes
after discovery of the findings.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CTA CHEST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with ___'s syndrome c/b recurrent cholangitis
and cirrhosis presenting with acute shortness of breath and hypoxemia. CXR
same as baseline.// Eval for PE vs PNA

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.
Reformatted coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal
intensity projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 2.2 s, 28.7 cm; CTDIvol = 14.5 mGy (Body) DLP = 416.2
mGy-cm.
   2) Stationary Acquisition 0.6 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 3.3 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.7
mGy-cm.
   3) Stationary Acquisition 3.6 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 19.9 mGy (Body) DLP =
9.9 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 428 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  Prior chest CTs, most recently ___.

FINDINGS: 

HEART AND VASCULATURE:
The heart is normal in size and shape.  No pericardial effusion.  No
atherosclerotic calcifications in the coronary arteries, aorta or cardiac
valves.  The pulmonary arteries and aorta are normal in caliber throughout. 
Large segmental pulmonary emboli in the right main pulmonary artery (301:73)
and in the left inferior pulmonary artery extending to its segmental branches
(301:70).  There is mild reticulation of the interventricular septum..

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE AND CHEST WALL:
The thyroid is not visualized.  No enlarged lymph nodes in either axilla or
thoracic inlet.  No abnormalities on the chest wall.  No atherosclerotic
calcifications in the head and neck arteries.

MEDIASTINUM AND HILA:
The esophagus is unremarkable.  Small mediastinal lymph nodes, none
pathologically enlarged by CT size criteria.  No hilar lymphadenopathy.

PLEURA:
No pleural effusions.  No apical scarring bilaterally.

LUNGS:
Respiratory motion artifacts impair optimal parenchymal evaluation. The
airways are patent to the subsegmental levels.  No bronchial wall thickening,
bronchiectasis or mucus plugging.  No grossly enlarged suspicious lung nodules
or masses.  Bibasilar atelectasis.

CHEST CAGE:
Mild dorsal spondylosis.  No acute fractures.  No suspicious lytic or
sclerotic lesions.

UPPER ABDOMEN:
The limited sections of the upper abdomen show redemonstration of a cirrhotic
liver with biliary dilation is noted in the right hepatic lobe, as previously
described by the MRCP from ___.

IMPRESSION: 

Large bilateral pulmonary emboli with evidence of right heart strain.  No
signs of associated pulmonary infarct.

radiology_facts.report_text:
CT CHEST WITHOUT CONTRAST

INDICATION:  Patient with severe aortic stenosis, pre-op.  Vascular disease,
aortic calcification.

COMPARISON:  None.

TECHNIQUE:  Axial helical MDCT images were obtained from the suprasternal
notch to the upper abdomen without administration of IV contrast with 1.25-mm
slice collimation.  Multiplanar reformatted images in coronal and sagittal
axis were generated.

FINDINGS:

MEDIASTINUM:

The thyroid is unremarkable.  There is no pathologic supraclavicular,
axillary, or mediastinal lymph node enlargement by CT size criteria.  There is
no pleural or pericardial effusion.

Thoracic aorta is not dilated.  Aortic valve is heavily calcified.  Ascending
aorta has a long segment without significant calcification except for focal
calcification measuring 12 x 4 mm in aortic medial wall adjacent to pulmonary
trunk.  Aortic cross and descending aorta is severely calcified with multiple
atherosclerotic plaques.

The abdominal aorta is included up to the infrarenal portion.  This patient
was known for an infrarenal aortic aneurysm measuring 3.1 cm in ___. Only the
top of the aneurysm is included in this exam.  

Atherosclerotic calcification of coronary arteries is moderate.The esophagus
is unremarkable. The patient has a history of left mastectomy for breast
cancer.

This study is not tailored for assessment of intra-abdominal organs.  There
has been a cholecystectomy.  Non-obstructive kidney stones are seen on the
left.  For example, the first one measuring 5 mm is in the lower calix, the
second one is in the renal pelvis measuring 4 mm.  There is also cortical
atrophy of left kidney.

AIRWAYS AND LUNGS:

Very mild paraseptal emphysema is found in right apex.

Very small calcified nodules are seen bilaterally, some of them are hard to
localize and may be on pleural surface; does the patient has a history of
prior exposure to asbestos?

Airways are unremarkable up to subsegmental level.

Very mild interstitial lung disease characterized mostly by small
reticulation, little ground-glass opacities and minimal bronchiolectasis.  It
has subpleural and inferior predominance.  It is also heterogeneous in space
with a more focal zone in right middle lobe(4;211) with honeycombing.

Very small nodules are scattered throughout the lung, measuring less than 4
mm, they are on series 4, at image 69, 87, 106, 212.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  There are no bone lesions concerning for malignancy.

IMPRESSION

1.  Thoracic aorta is not dilated.  Aortic valve is heavily calcified in this
patient known with severe aortic stenosis.  Ascending aorta is mostly not
calcified; afterwards the aorta has multiple calcified and non-calcified
atherosclerotic plaques.  Infrarenal aortic aneurysm is not completely
assessed on this exam.

2.  Very mild interstitial lung disease.

3.  Bilateral calcified nodules, some of them could be pleural plaques.  Has
the patient had prior history of exposure to asbestos?

4.  Few less than 4-mm nodules are found throughout the lungs.  Because of the
risk factors (smoking and possible asbestos exposure), a CT scan can be done
in a year, except if prior chest CT was done at an outside hospital.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CTA CHEST AND CT ABDOMEN AND PELVIS

INDICATION:  History: ___ with fever, tachycardia, clear chest x-ray, hx
ulcerative colitis  // PE, pneumonia, intraabdominal abscess?

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT axial images were acquired through the chest, abdomen and
pelvis following intravenous contrast administration with split bolus
technique.
Oral contrast was not administered.
Coronal and sagittal reformations were performed and reviewed on PACS.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Stationary Acquisition 1.0 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 3.0 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.5
mGy-cm.
   2) Spiral Acquisition 2.9 s, 22.6 cm; CTDIvol = 14.0 mGy (Body) DLP = 315.8
mGy-cm.
   3) Spiral Acquisition 7.3 s, 57.1 cm; CTDIvol = 16.1 mGy (Body) DLP = 920.1
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 1,237 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  MRI abdomen dated ___.  CT abdomen dated ___.

FINDINGS: 

CHEST:

HEART AND VASCULATURE: The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber without
evidence of acute injury.  No pericardial effusion is seen.

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM: No axillary, mediastinal, or hilar
lymphadenopathy is present.  No mediastinal mass or hematoma.

PLEURAL SPACES: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS: The airways are patent to the level of the segmental bronchi
bilaterally.  There are bibasilar atelectasis.  There is a 1.4 cm focal
opacification in the left upper lobe (series 4, image 71) which may represent
early pneumonia.


ABDOMEN:

HEPATOBILIARY: The liver demonstrates homogenous attenuation throughout. 
There is no evidence of focal lesion or laceration.  There is no evidence of
intrahepatic or extrahepatic biliary dilatation.  The gallbladder is not
visualized.

PANCREAS: The pancreas has normal attenuation throughout, without evidence of
focal lesions or pancreatic ductal dilatation.  There is no peripancreatic
stranding.

SPLEEN: The spleen shows normal size and attenuation throughout, without
evidence of focal lesion or laceration.

ADRENALS: The right adrenal gland is surgically removed.  The left adrenal
gland is normal.

URINARY: The kidneys are of normal and symmetric size with normal nephrogram. 
There is no evidence of focal renal lesions or hydronephrosis.  There is no
perinephric abnormality.

GASTROINTESTINAL: The stomach is unremarkable.  Small bowel loops demonstrate
normal caliber, wall thickness, and enhancement throughout.  There is moderate
amount of stool throughout the colon and rectum.  There is equivocal mild
thickening of the distal sigmoid colon/rectosigmoid.

There is no free fluid or free air in the abdomen.

PELVIS:

There is air visualized in the urinary bladder likely secondary to recent
instrumentation.  There is a Foley catheter visualized with the balloon at the
base of the penis.  There is no free fluid in the pelvis.

REPRODUCTIVE ORGANS: The reproductive organs are unremarkable.

LYMPH NODES: There is no retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy.  There
is no pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

VASCULAR: There is no abdominal aortic aneurysm or retroperitoneal hematoma. 
No atherosclerotic disease is noted.

BONES: There is mild degenerative changes of the thoracolumbar spine.  There
is left hip posterior dislocation which is likely chronic with adjacent soft
tissue thickening, adjacent joint effusion not excluded..  There is chronic
deformity of the left femoral head and left femoral head/hip degenerative
changes..  There is no evidence of bone erosion.

SOFT TISSUES: The abdominal and pelvic wall is within normal limits.  There is
a pacer versus stimulator hardware visualized in the right lower quadrant
subcutaneous soft tissue.

IMPRESSION:


1. Nonspecific 1.4 cm nodular left upper lobe opacity which may represent
pneumonia.  Recommend follow-up CT chest in 3 months to assess for resolution.
Pulmonary nodule not excluded.
2. Malpositioned Foley catheter with balloon in the base of the penis.
3. Moderate amount stool in the distal sigmoid colon/rectosigmoid.  Equivocal
associated mild wall thickening, possible early stercoral colitis.
4. Chronic appearing left hip dislocation with adjacent soft tissue
thickening, adjacent joint effusion not excluded..

NOTIFICATION:  The findings of the study had been discussed a by Dr. ___
with Dr. ___ on ___ at 11:54 p.m.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with 5mm nodule for f/u  // Any change in size or
character?

TECHNIQUE:  Volumetric CT acquisitions over the entire thorax in inspiration,
no administration of intravenous contrast material, multiplanar
reconstructions.

DOSE:  DLP:  794 mGy-cm

COMPARISON:  ___.

FINDINGS: 

No incidental thyroid findings.  No supraclavicular, infraclavicular or
axillary lymphadenopathy.  Subcutaneous cyst in the anterior chest wall (2,
16).  Status post sternotomy and CABG.  Expected postoperative appearance of
the heart.  No lymphadenopathy in the mediastinum. No lymphadenopathy in the
chest wall.  No abnormalities in the posterior mediastinum.  The upper abdomen
shows a 1 cm right kidney cyst (2, 60 but no evidence of acute abnormalities. 
No osteolytic lesions at the level of the ribs, the sternum, or the vertebral
bodies.  Mild degenerative vertebral disease.  No vertebral compression
fractures.
Azygos lobe as anatomical variant.  New scar in the left upper lobe (4, 41). 
Several pre-existing subpleural micronodules are stable in size and
morphology.  There is no evidence of new or growing nodules.  Despite the
history of asbestos exposure, no pleural abnormalities are noted.  The airways
are patent.

IMPRESSION: 

Stability of non suspicious.  Pre-existing pulmonary micronodules.  No new or
growing nodules.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CTA CHEST WITH CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ with hypoxia, possible metastatic CA// please eval for PE

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.  Reformatted
coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal intensity
projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Total DLP (Body) = 462 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  None

FINDINGS: 

HEART AND VASCULATURE: Pulmonary vasculature is well opacified to the
subsegmental and there is a filling defect within the segmental branch of the
left upper lobe (4:84).  Apparent filling defect in a subsegmental branch of
the left lower lobe is noted though there is significant respiratory motion
which limits detailed evaluation.  Given slight hypoenhancement of the
atelectatic lung at the left lung base medially this could indeed represent
additional filling defect with possible infarct.  No additional filling
defects are identified.

Coronary artery and mitral annular calcifications are noted.  Atherosclerotic
calcifications also noted at the aortic arch.  The heart is mildly enlarged.  
No pericardial effusion is seen.

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM: No axillary, mediastinal, or hilar
lymphadenopathy is present.  No mediastinal mass.

PLEURAL SPACES: Small left and trace right pleural effusions are noted.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS: Dependent areas of atelectasis are again noted.  As detailed
above, there is slight hypoenhancement at the left lung base medially which
could represent an infarct.  Respiratory motion does limit detail evaluation
for tiny pulmonary nodules.  The airways are patent to the level of the
segmental bronchi bilaterally.

BASE OF NECK: Visualized portions of the base of the neck show no abnormality.

ABDOMEN:  Included portion of the upper abdomen is notable for partially
visualized adenopathy and numerous hypoenhancing liver lesions.  These
findings are concerning for metastatic disease.

BONES: No suspicious osseous abnormality is seen.? old bilateral anterior rib
fractures are noted.  In addition there are fractures of the left posterior
eighth, ninth ribs near the costovertebral junction to be correlated
clinically regarding acuity.

IMPRESSION: 

1.  Left upper lobe segmental pulmonary embolism.  Questionable left lower
lobe subsegmental pulmonary embolism which is likely real given slight
hypoenhancement of the lung supplied by this branch which could represent

ribs posteriorly to be correlated clinically regarding acuity as these may be

after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.  Reformatted

subsegmental and there is a filling defect within the segmental branch of the

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ man status post fall.  Findings concerning for
cerebellar tumor.

COMPARISON:  Chest radiograph from ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector CT scanning of the abdomen and pelvis was performed
after oral and intravenous contrast.  Non-contrast images of the abdomen and
delayed images of the kidneys were also obtained.

CT OF THE CHEST:  An endotracheal tube is seen terminating in the high
trachea.  Within the lung in the right upper lobe, there is a 1.7 x 3.0 cm
mass which extends along the bronchial tree towards the hilum.  Within the
paratracheal region in the AP window, subcarinal region, and in both hila,
there are necrotic-appearing lymph nodes in conglomeration.  In the
paratracheal region they measure up to 14 mm, in the subcarinal region they
measure up to 18 mm.  There are also small prevascular lymph nodes which have
the same appearance.  Elsewhere in the lungs, there are several other
pulmonary nodules, including on images 3:10, 30, 31, 39, 52, and 32. A
nasogastric tube extends into the stomach.  The heart size is not enlarged.
There is a small amount of pericardial fluid. There is extensive aortic valve
calcification as well as coronary artery and mitral annular calcification. The
thoracic aorta is heavily calcified throughout.

There is a right-sided chest tube in place, which terminates in the posterior
region of the hemithorax.  A small hydropneumothorax remains on the right.
Several minimally displaced rib fractures on the right are also noted with
subcutaneous emphysema.

CT OF THE ABDOMEN:  The liver, gallbladder, right adrenal gland, spleen, and
pancreas appear unremarkable.  There is a hypoattenuating 15 mm left adrenal
lesion.

Both kidneys contain cysts.  The one on the left is too small to characterize.
Loops of small and large bowel demonstrate no evidence of obstruction.  There
is a large amount of stool, particularly in the right colon.  There is no
extraluminal air.  There is no ascites.  There is heavy calcification of the
abdominal aorta without aneurysmal dilation.

CT OF THE PELVIS:  There is a Foley catheter within the bladder lumen.  The
rectum appears unremarkable.  There is no free fluid.  There is no
lymphadenopathy.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  There is a compression deformity of the L2 vertebral
body, age indeterminate.  Confluent anterior osteophytes are noted.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Lung mass in the right upper lobe with several other pulmonary nodules
bilaterally as above.  Extensive medial and hilar lymphadenopathy.  Left
adrenal lesion.  All these findings are highly suspicious for metastatic lung
cancer.

2.  Multiple right-sided rib fractures with small right hydropneumothorax.
Chest tube in place.

3.  Compression deformity of the L2 vertebral body.

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  Unexplained weight loss, rule out malignancy.

COMPARISON:  None.

TECHNIQUE:  CT through the chest, abdomen and pelvis was obtained after
administration of oral contrast and 130 cc of IV contrast.
Multiplanar reformats performed.

FINDINGS:

CHEST:  There is scarring and bronchiectasis seen in the left lower lobe.
Minimal scarring is seen in the right lower lobe.  No suspicious pulmonary
nodules are seen.  There is no mediastinal or hilar adenopathy.  The heart
size is normal.  There is no pleural effusion.  There are dense coronary
artery calcifications.

ABDOMEN:  Status post cholecystectomy.  Liver, pancreas, spleen, adrenal
glands appear normal.  There are multiple hypodensities in the kidneys
bilaterally too small to characterize, likely cysts.  The bowel loops appear
normal.  There is no adenopathy.

PELVIS:  The pelvic bowel loops appear normal.  There is no pelvic adenopathy
or free fluid.  There is borderline thickening of the bladder wall diffusely.
The bladder is not entirely distended, however.  Correlation for urinary tract
infection could be made.  There is degenerative disc disease of the spine.

IMPRESSION:

1.  No definite evidence of malignancy in the chest, abdomen or pelvis.

2.  Left lower lobe scarring and bronchiectasis.  This can be seen in chronic
infection or recurrent aspiration.

3.  Coronary artery atherosclerotic disease.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with CHF with dobutamine requirement.  //
Transplant Evaluation.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was performed without
intravenous contrast.

DLP:  ___

COMPARISON:  Prior CT scan from ___ (OSH).

FINDINGS: 

A right PICC line is identified with the distal tip in the SVC. A ___-___
catheter is identified with the distal tip in the right lower lobe pulmonary
artery. An AICD device is identified with the distal tip in the right
ventricle. There is severe cardiomegaly, with likely four-chamber enlargement.
No pericardial or pleural effusions are identified. Dense calcification of the
coronary arteries is noted. All no axillary, all supraclavicular, mediastinal
or hilar lymphadenopathy. Mild atheromatous calcification of the thoracic
aorta is noted.

Moderate centrilobular emphysema is identified, predominately within the upper
lobes. No evidence of interstitial pulmonary edema. No suspicious lung nodules
are identified. A calcified granuloma is identified in the right lower lobe
(603b:29).

Subdiaphragmatic structures are suboptimally assessed in the absence of
intravenous contrast, however the liver demonstrates slightly nodular contour.
Thickening of the adrenal glands is noted bilaterally, likely in keeping with
adrenal hyperplasia. Small amount of fluid/stranding is identified surrounding
the pancreatic head near the porta hepatis (2:70), not completely assessed.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:

Mild mole multilevel degenerative changes of the thoracic spine are evident.
No focal osteolytic or osteoblastic lesions are seen.

IMPRESSION:


1. Right ___ catheter identified with the distal tip in the right lower
lobe pulmonary artery. This needs to be retracted back to the level of the
right ventricular outflow tract.
2. Severe cardiomegaly with four-chamber enlargement. AICD device identified.
3. Dense coronary artery calcifications.
4. Moderate centrilobular emphysema predominate within the upper lobes.
5. Slightly nodular contour of the liver, suggestive of cirrhosis.
6. Thickening of adrenal glands bilaterally, likely in keeping with adrenal
hyperplasia.
7. Small amount of fluid/ stranding identified surrounding the pancreatic head
near the porta hepatis, not completely assess, likely related to trace ascites
rather than pancreatitis in the absence of clinical history of abdominal pain.

NOTIFICATION:  This consultation was reviewed with Dr. ___ by Dr. ___
telephone at 20:30 hr on ___, 25 min following discovery.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST WITHOUT CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with aspergillis in sputum, unable to wean
vent// eval for infiltrate, cavitating lesion.  Status post emergent type A
dissection surgery ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images of the chest obtained without
administration of IV contrast.  Coronal and sagittal reformats were provided.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 2.2 s, 34.2 cm; CTDIvol = 17.7 mGy (Body) DLP = 604.4
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 604 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CTA chest ___

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: Numerous small lymph nodes in the
supraclavicular regions are not enlarged by size criteria.  No axillary
lymphadenopathy.  There is mild subcutaneous edema noted in the chest.

UPPER ABDOMEN: An NG tube is noted and however the tip is not included in the
field of view.  A 3 mm nonobstructing stone in the superior pole of the left
kidney.

MEDIASTINUM: Widening of the mediastinum secondary to type A dissection, which
is better evaluated on prior CTA.  Hyperdense material at the root of the
aorta are new since last CT and could be related to dissection repair.  The
calcified walls outline the true lumen which is not significantly changed in
caliber from the level of the aortic arch to the distal thoracic aorta.  Right
upper paratracheal lymphadenopathy measure up to 1.6 cm appears unchanged
since prior and reactive.  There is diffuse fat stranding of the mediastinal
fat.

HILA: No hilar adenopathy with limitations of the study.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: Marked cardiomegaly appear similar to prior. 
Resolution of the previous pericardial effusion.  Mitral annulus
calcification, valvular aortic calcification and coronary artery
calcifications are again seen.  Right-sided internal jugular catheter
terminates in the lower SVC.

PLEURA: New small bilateral pleural effusions.

LUNG:

1. PARENCHYMA: Bilateral multifocal ground-glass and nodular opacities with
consolidation in both lower lobes, worse on the left.
2. AIRWAYS:  Patient is intubated and the ETT terminates in appropriate
position.  Decreased AP diameter of the trachea could be secondary to
tracheomalacia.  Hypodense material is noted in the bronchus to the left lower
lobe.  Focal area of narrowing is noted in the bronchus to the lingula
(302:73).
3. VESSELS:  Please refer to above description of the thoracic aorta.  Deep
main pulmonary artery is top normal.
CHEST CAGE: New since ___ is median sternotomy with cerclage wires in
place.  No suspicious osseous structures of the chest

IMPRESSION:


1. Multifocal bilateral ground-glass, nodular opacities and consolidation in
both lower lobes, worse on the left are likely secondary to multifocal
pneumonia.
2. New small bilateral pleural effusions.
3. Type A aortic dissection incompletely characterized in this study, with new
hyperdense material at the ascending aorta, likely related to the repair. 
Atherosclerotic plaque CT outline the true lumen in the remainder thoracic
aorta which appears not significantly changed in caliber from prior.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST WITHOUT CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with aspergillus PNA, repeat CT chest to
evaluate resolution of PNA// ___ year old woman with aspergillus PNA, repeat CT
chest to evaluate resolution of PNA

TECHNIQUE:  Contiguous axial images were obtained through the chest without
intravenous contrast.  Coronal, sagittal and MIP reformats were performed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 8.7 s, 33.3 cm; CTDIvol = 16.0 mGy (Body) DLP = 508.5
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 522 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CTA torso done ___ and CT chest ___.

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: Tracheostomy tube in situ with the
tip at the level of the aortic arch.  Right central line terminates at the
cavoatrial junction.  No supraclavicular adenopathy.  Subcentimeter axillary
lymph nodes are most likely reactive.

UPPER ABDOMEN: Percutaneous gastrostomy tube in situ. A 0.5 cm right renal
calculi is incompletely imaged.

MEDIASTINUM: Mediastinal lymph nodes appear fairly similar compared to prior
imaging ranging up to 13 mm in the right lower paratracheal station.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: The patient is status post aortic root repair.  Post
dissection changes are difficult to assess without IV contrast and reference
is made to prior contrast CTA torso done ___.

PLEURA: Small sized simple left pleural effusion.
LUNG:

1. PARENCHYMA:  Interstitial thickening in keeping with pulmonary edema. 
Nodular peribronchovascular airspace disease in the dependent aspect of the
right upper lobe and basal aspects of the right middle and lower lobes most
likely represents bronchopneumonia.  These areas of airspace opacification are
slightly less confluent, but there is a slight increase in number of the
peribronchovascular nodules.  The overall disease burden is slightly decreased
compared to prior (especially in the dependent aspect of the right upper
lobe).  Confluent airspace opacification in the dependent aspect of the left
upper lobe and superior and basal aspects of the left lower lobe most likely
represent atelectasis, however please note that underlying pneumonia cannot be
excluded.  Ground-glass airspace opacification the a left lower lobe (series
5, image 24) is nonspecific.
2. AIRWAYS:  Partial collapse of the central airways may represent
tracheobronchomalacia in the correct clinical setting.
3. VESSELS:  The pulmonary artery is dilated measuring 4 cm diameter
suggesting pulmonary hypertension.
CHEST CAGE: The patient is status post midline sternotomy.  Old, healed left
lower posterior rib fractures.  Marked degenerative changes of the thoracic
spine.  No lytic/destructive bony lesions.

IMPRESSION: 

1.  Nodular peribronchovascular airspace disease in the dependent aspect of
the right upper lobe and basal aspects of the right middle and lower lobes
most likely represents bronchopneumonia.  The overall disease burden is
decreased (especially in the dependent aspect of the right upper lobe)
compared to prior  CT studies.
2.  Please note that it is difficult to differentiate atelectasis from
consolidation on a non contrasted study.  However, airspace opacification in
the dependent aspect of the left upper lobe and superior segment of the left
lower lobe most likely represents atelectasis.  Ground-glass airspace
opacification in the left lower lobe is nonspecific.
3.  Small left-sided pleural effusion.
4.  Patient is status post aortic root repair. Residual post dissection
changes are difficult to assess on a noncontrast study.

sections.findings:
NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: Tracheostomy tube in situ with the
tip at the level of the aortic arch.  Right central line terminates at the
cavoatrial junction.  No supraclavicular adenopathy.  Subcentimeter axillary
lymph nodes are most likely reactive.

UPPER ABDOMEN: Percutaneous gastrostomy tube in situ. A 0.5 cm right renal
calculi is incompletely imaged.

MEDIASTINUM: Mediastinal lymph nodes appear fairly similar compared to prior
imaging ranging up to 13 mm in the right lower paratracheal station.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: The patient is status post aortic root repair.  Post
dissection changes are difficult to assess without IV contrast and reference
is made to prior contrast CTA torso done ___.

PLEURA: Small sized simple left pleural effusion.
LUNG:

1. PARENCHYMA:  Interstitial thickening in keeping with pulmonary edema. 
Nodular peribronchovascular airspace disease in the dependent aspect of the
right upper lobe and basal aspects of the right middle and lower lobes most
likely represents bronchopneumonia.  These areas of airspace opacification are
slightly less confluent, but there is a slight increase in number of the
peribronchovascular nodules.  The overall disease burden is slightly decreased
compared to prior (especially in the dependent aspect of the right upper
lobe).  Confluent airspace opacification in the dependent aspect of the left
upper lobe and superior and basal aspects of the left lower lobe most likely
represent atelectasis, however please note that underlying pneumonia cannot be
excluded.  Ground-glass airspace opacification the a left lower lobe (series
5, image 24) is nonspecific.
