tracheobronchomalacia in the correct clinical setting.

suggesting pulmonary hypertension.
CHEST CAGE: The patient is status post midline sternotomy.  Old, healed left
lower posterior rib fractures.  Marked degenerative changes of the thoracic
spine.  No lytic/destructive bony lesions.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ male with intracranial hemorrhage and left pleural
effusion.  Evaluate for primary malignancy.

COMPARISON:  None.

TECHNIQUE:  Non-contrast axial images of the abdomen were obtained followed by
contrast-enhanced axial images of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis.  Multiplanar
reformatted images were also submitted for review.

CT CHEST WITH CONTRAST:  Minimal atherosclerotic coronary artery
calcifications are noted.  The heart and great vessels are otherwise
unremarkable and there is no evidence for pericardial or pleural effusion. The
left costophrenic angle blunting noted on recent chest radiograph is secondary
to prominent left subpleural fat.  Scattered small mediastinal nodes, some of
which are partially calcified, are associated with small calcified right lower
lobe peribronchial nodes. A 5- mm calcified granuloma is noted in the right
lower lobe (3:30). A 6 mm nodule at the right lung base (3:25) is
noncalcified. Hypoventilatory changes are noted at the lung bases bilaterally.
The airways are patent to the subsegmental level.

CT ABDOMEN WITH AND WITHOUT CONTRAST:  Calcified granulomas are noted in the
spleen and at the hepatic dome.  No other focal hepatic lesions are
identified.  The gallbladder, pancreas, and adrenal glands appear normal.  The
kidneys enhance symmetrically and excrete contrast normally without
hydronephrosis or hydroureter.  Multiple hypoattenuating renal lesions are
identified, several of which are too small to characterize.  A 4.9 x 4.7 cm
exophytic cyst arises from the lower pole of the left kidney.  A focus of
cortical thinning involving the interpolar region of the right kidney likely
represents prior infectious or inflammatory insult.  The stomach is moderately
distended with fluid and debris.  Intra-abdominal loops of large and small
bowel are of normal caliber and there is no pneumoperitoneum or free fluid. No
pathologically enlarged mesenteric or retroperitoneal lymph nodes are
identified.  The abdominal aorta is of normal caliber.

CT PELVIS WITH CONTRAST:  The rectum and sigmoid colon appear unremarkable.
The bladder contains a Foley catheter.  The prostate is mildly enlarged. There
is no free pelvic fluid, or pathologically enlarged lymph nodes.

Bone windows reveal no worrisome lytic or sclerotic lesions.  Multilevel
thoracolumbar degenerative changes are observed.  A 10-mm sclerotic lesion in
the left acetabulum likely represents a bone island.

IMPRESSION:
1.  No thoracic or abdominopelvic malignant process identified.
2.  In the setting of other sequela of prior granulomatous exposure, the non-
calcified right lung base nodule may represent a non-calcified granuloma.
However, repeat chest CT in one year is recommended if there are risk factors
for malignancy (i.e. smoking).

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old female with stage IV lung cancer who received prior
palliative chemotherapy.// Evaluate disease burden. Compare to prior studies.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
intravenous infusion of nonionic iodinated contrast agent reconstructed as
contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick axial, 2.5-mm thick coronal and parasagittal,
and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images.

DOSE:  DLP: Please refer to the separate dose report in the abdomen and pelvis
CT report.

COMPARISON:  ___

FINDINGS: 

Aorta and pulmonary arteries are overall unremarkable.  Left upper lobe
continues to be collapsed due to obstructed left upper lobe bronchus,  series
4, image 124.  Overall the volume of the collapsed lobe is stable since the
most recent prior examination.  Subpleural nodule in the superior segment of
the left lower lobe (03:21) is stable.  Besides left upper lobe bronchus the
rest of the airways are patent to the subsegmental level bilaterally.  New
mild atelectasis and linear opacity in the right lower lobe.  Otherwise no new
pulmonary nodules masses or consolidations demonstrated.

There is no pleural effusion accumulation.  No mediastinal lymph nodes
currently seen with resolution of lymphadenopathy demonstrated on ___.  Heart size is normal.  No pericardial effusion is seen.  The left
thyroid has been surgically removed.

Lytic and sclerotic lesions previously mentioned of the thoracic spine are
overall unchanged.  Multiple compression fractures are stable.  No substantial
retropulsion.  Mid sternal deformity with associated sclerosis is more
apparent since the last examination likely reflecting subacute fracture.

Image portion of the upper abdomen will be reviewed and reported separately.

IMPRESSION: 

Stable appearance of the left upper lobe collapse secondary to obstructed left
upper lobe bronchus.
No new lung nodules, lymphadenopathy or pleural disease in the thorax.
Subacute sternal fracture is more apparent since ___ and new since
___.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT Chest without contrast

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with nocardia lung infection// eval for interval
improvement

TECHNIQUE:  Contiguous axial images were obtained through the chest without
intravenous contrast.  Coronal and sagittal reformats were obtained.

COMPARISON:  CT chest without contrast from ___

FINDINGS: 

HEART AND VASCULATURE: Normal caliber aorta.  Atherosclerotic calcifications
are seen along the aortic arch.  There is a normal 3 vessel aortic arch.  The
heart, pericardium, and great vessels are within normal limits based on an
unenhanced scan. No pericardial effusion is seen.  Left PICC line tip is in
mid to low SVC.

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM: There is interval decrease in size previously
enlarged mediastinal lymph nodes, now measuring up to 7 mm (series 2: Image
25).  Few small mediastinal lymph nodes are partially calcified.  No axillary
lymphadenopathy is present.
PLEURAL SPACES: There is a small dependent, nonhemorrhagic, layering left
pleural effusion, more prominent compared to prior..  There is no right
pleural effusion.  No evidence of pneumothorax.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS: There has been interval improvement in previously seen diffuse
bilateral multifocal consolidations and centrilobular nodules since the prior
exam in ___, compatible with improving infection.  There is
multifocal bronchiectasis, mildly worsened since prior, most apparent in the
right upper lobe, at the lung bases.  If areas of bronchial wall thickening
are similar, and are most prominent in the left lower lobe.  The emphysematous
changes at the right lung base similar.

BASE OF NECK: Visualized portions of the base of the neck show no abnormality.

ABDOMEN:  Included portion of the unenhanced upper abdomen is remarkable for
stable hepatic cysts, measuring up to 4.8 x 3.9 cm in the left hepatic lobe. 
(Series 2: Image 56).  Enteric tube is seen terminating in the stomach.  There
is a 1.4 cm simple cyst in the left upper renal pole.

BONES: No suspicious osseous abnormality is seen.? There is no acute fracture.


IMPRESSION:


1. Interval improvement in previously seen diffuse bilateral multifocal
consolidations, centrilobular nodules, and mediastinal lymphadenopathy since
the prior exam in ___, compatible with improving infection.
2. Multifocal bronchiectasis is more prominent..
3. Small layering left pleural effusion.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ woman with pancreatitis.  Prior imaging suggestive of
metastatic disease.

COMPARISON:  Right upper quadrant ultrasound and chest radiograph from the
same day.

TECHNIQUE:  Non-contrast CT of the chest, abdomen and pelvis.

CT OF THE CHEST:  Study is limited by lack of intravenous contrast.  The heart
and pericardium demonstrate coronary artery calcifications, calcifications of
the mitral annulus, and calcifications of the aortic valve.  The thoracic
aorta is also heavily calcified but not aneurysmally dilated.  There is
mediastinal lymphadenopathy, particularly in the subcarinal region where there
is a node measuring up to 1.8 cm.  There is a left hilar lymphadenopathy up to
2.5 cm.  The lungs demonstrate extensive emphysematous changes at the apices.
A 15 mm nodule is seen in the left lower lobe with patchy surrounding opacity,
and linear extension to the hilum.  In the left upper lobe, there is a 4-mm
pulmonary nodule (2:18).  In the right upper lobe, there is a 4-mm pulmonary
nodule, (2:25).  Several other small pulmonary nodules are identified
including on series 2, image 39, 35, 28.  The airways are narrowed in the
region of the left hilum.  No pleural effusions are seen.

CT OF THE ABDOMEN:  There are innumerable hypodense lesions replacing the
majority of the liver.  There is a small amount of ascites tracking adjacent
to the liver.  The adrenal glands appear thickened bilaterally.  The spleen
also demonstrates some fluid adjacent to it.  The area of the pancreatic head
is not well evaluated without oral or intravenous contrast; however, there is
some surrounding stranding.  There is high density within the gallbladder
which raises the possibility of sludge.  There is stranding extending along
the anterior pararenal space on the right, and down the right lateral conal
fascia.  The kidneys demonstrate no hydronephrosis.  Loops of small bowel are
opacified with contrast.  Contrast has reached the cecum.  No dilated loops of
bowel are evident.  There is sigmoid diverticulosis without diverticulitis. No
definite free intraperitoneal air is identified.  There appears to be a focal
saccular aneurysm of the infrarenal abdominal aorta up to 3.6 cm.  There are
large retroperitoneal lymph nodes, particularly in the left periaortic region
measuring up to 15 mm.

CT OF THE PELVIS:  A Foley catheter is within the bladder lumen.  The uterus
and rectum appear unremarkable.  There is the appearance of a focal area of
rectal prolapse on the left.  There is no free fluid within the pelvis.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  There are healed multiple right lateral rib fractures as
well as a posterior second rib fracture on the right.  There is a more acute
appearing left-sided rib fracture in the eleventh rib laterally.  Degenerative
change is seen within the spine; grade 1 anterolisthesis of L4 on L5.

There are innumerable nodules in the subcutaneous tissues of various sizes,
some of the largest are seen in the right chest wall anteriorly measuring 1.4
cm, in the inferior right breast measuring 10 mm in the left lateral chest
wall measuring 10 mm, in the right flank measuring 12 mm and 13 mm.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Innumerable hepatic lesions, hilar and mediastinal adenopathy, several
pulmonary nodules, as well as retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy are consistent
with metastatic disease. Left lower lobe lung lesions may also be a metastatic
foci, or related to post- obstructive atelectasis or pneumonia given the
narrowing of the bronchi in the left hilum.
2.  Innumerable subcutaneous metastatic nodules, raising the possiblity of
melanoma.
3.  Stranding surrounding the pancreatic head and extending more laterally in
the anterior pararenal space is consistent with pancreatitis as suggested by
lab values.  
4.  Saccular abdominal aortic aneurysm.

CT OF THE PELVIS:  A Foley catheter is within the bladder lumen.  The uterus
and rectum appear unremarkable.  There is the appearance of a focal area of
rectal prolapse on the left.  There is no free fluid within the pelvis.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  There are healed multiple right lateral rib fractures as
well as a posterior second rib fracture on the right.  There is a more acute
appearing left-sided rib fracture in the eleventh rib laterally.  Degenerative
change is seen within the spine; grade 1 anterolisthesis of L4 on L5.

There are innumerable nodules in the subcutaneous tissues of various sizes,
some of the largest are seen in the right chest wall anteriorly measuring 1.4
cm, in the inferior right breast measuring 10 mm in the left lateral chest
wall measuring 10 mm, in the right flank measuring 12 mm and 13 mm.
sections.comparison:
Right upper quadrant ultrasound and chest radiograph from the
same day.
sections.impression:
1.  Innumerable hepatic lesions, hilar and mediastinal adenopathy, several
pulmonary nodules, as well as retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy are consistent
with metastatic disease. Left lower lobe lung lesions may also be a metastatic
foci, or related to post- obstructive atelectasis or pneumonia given the
narrowing of the bronchi in the left hilum.
2.  Innumerable subcutaneous metastatic nodules, raising the possiblity of
melanoma.
3.  Stranding surrounding the pancreatic head and extending more laterally in
the anterior pararenal space is consistent with pancreatitis as suggested by
lab values.  
4.  Saccular abdominal aortic aneurysm.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  History: ___ with fall, head/neck/chest/ABD pain  // acute trauma

TECHNIQUE:  CT of the Chest, Abdomen and Pelvis with IV contrast and with oral
contrast

DOSE:  DLP:  1142 mGy-cm

COMPARISON:  None

FINDINGS:


CHEST:
Mediastinum and Heart: Minimal atherosclerotic calcifications of the left
anterior descending as well as left circumflex artery are noted.  No
pericardial effusion.  No mediastinal hematoma
Lungs: Bibasilar atelectasis is present. No evidence of pneumothorax. A 8 mm
left lower lobe nodule (02:46) is noted which may be related to atelectasis,
but should be followed. No pleural effusion.
Vasculature: Aortic calcifications are noted throughout the aortic arch as
well as descending aorta.

ABDOMEN:

Liver:  Within normal limits
Gallbladder: Within normal limits
Spleen: Within normal limits
Kidneys and Ureters: Numerous hypodensities are noted within both kidneys, the
largest of which are compatible with simple renal cysts, but many of which are
too small to accurately characterize. Statistically these most likely
represent cysts as well.
Adrenals: Thickening of the left adrenal gland is noted without discrete
nodularity. The right adrenal within normal limits.
Pancreas: Within normal limits
Lymph Nodes: Within normal limits
Aorta and Branches: Extensive atherosclerotic disease of the aorta is noted
including a thrombosis of the initial portions of the left internal iliac
artery which is aneurysmal dilatated up to 1.9 cm.  The left internal iliac
reconstitutes more distally.
Stomach:  Within normal limits
Small Bowel: Within normal limits
Large Bowel: Sigmoid diverticulosis is noted without active diverticulitis.  A
large amount of stool is noted within the rectum.
Appendix: Within normal limits
Free Fluid: None

PELVIS:
Reproductive Organs: Within normal limits
Rectum:  Large stool ball is within the rectum.
Bladder: Within normal limits
Inguinal Region: Within normal limits
Lymph Nodes: Within normal limits
Free Fluid: None

BONES AND SOFT TISSUES:  No suspicious blastic or lytic lesions.  Numerous,
old rib fractures are noted in the right posterior ribs including the right
posterior sixth, seventh, eighth, ninth, probable tenth ribs as well as the
right lateral eleventh rib.

There are also several age indeterminate compression fractures of the
vertebral body including the T11, T9, T8 and T7 vertebral bodies. The greatest
compression is of the T9 vertebral body which demonstrates over 50% height
loss in its mid portions.  No acute appearing fracture lines are present.

There is grade 1 anterolisthesis of L5 on S1 with bilateral pars defects.

IMPRESSION:



1. No evidence of solid organ injury.
2. Several age indeterminate thoracic vertebral body compression fractures,
but likely  chronic.
3. Several chronic right rib fractures.
4. Severe atherosclerotic disease of the aorta including a short portion of
the proximal left internal iliac which is thrombosed and aneurysmal dilated.
5. 8 mm left lower lobe nodule.  This should be followed in 3 months initially
taking into account the patient's clinical status

radiology_facts.report_text:
CHEST CT WITHOUT CONTRAST

INDICATION:  Patient with diabetes, chronic heart failure, probably
ANCA-positive vasculitis versus mixed connective tissue disorder, growing
ground-glass solid nodule in the right middle lobe, close followup and
evaluation of treatment response to immunosuppressives.

COMPARISON:  Chest CT of ___ and ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Axial helical MDCT images were obtained from the suprasternal
notch to the upper abdomen without administration of IV contrast and 1.25 mm
slice collimation.  Multiplanar reformatted images in coronal and sagittal
axis were generated.

FINDINGS:

LUNGS AND AIRWAYS:  

Bilateral ground-glass opacities including a more nodular area in anterior
right upper lobe have significantly improved after treatment.  There is no new
lung lesion or enlarging nodule concerning for malignancy.

Fibrosis in lower lungs with bronchiectasis, volume loss and honeycombing is
unchanged since ___.

The airways are patent until subsegmental level.

MEDIASTINUM:  There is no pathologic supraclavicular, mediastinal or axillary
lymph node enlargement by CT size criteria.  Mild dilatation of ascending
aorta to 4.2 cm is unchanged.  Left-sided pectoral pacemaker is in adequate
position with right atrium and ventricle leads.  Coronary arteries are heavily
calcified with multiple stents in place.

There is no pleural or pericardial effusion.  The esophagus is not dilated.

UPPER ABDOMEN:  This study is not tailored for assessment of intra-abdominal
organs.  The upper abdomen appears unremarkable.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  There is no bony lesion concerning for malignancy.

CONCLUSION:

1.  Patient is known with ANCA-positive vasculitis versus mixed connective
tissue disorder.

2.  Bilateral ground-glass opacities including a nodular area in anterior
right upper lobe has significantly improved since prior exam.  Differential
diagnosis includes alveolitis related to the connective tissue
disease/vasculitis, microangitis or medication reaction.

3.  Fibrotic interstitial lung disease in lower lobes is unchanged since ___.

4.  There is no lung lesion concerning for malignancy.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with acute desat and SOB ___ s/p Left total
shoulder replacement  // Eval for PE or other acute process

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.
Reformatted coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal
intensity projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Sequenced Acquisition 0.5 s, 0.2 cm; CTDIvol = 7.2 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.4
mGy-cm.
   2) Stationary Acquisition 6.2 s, 0.2 cm; CTDIvol = 82.2 mGy (Body) DLP =
16.4 mGy-cm.
   3) Spiral Acquisition 5.2 s, 33.9 cm; CTDIvol = 4.5 mGy (Body) DLP = 149.6
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 167 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  Comparison is made to chest radiograph from ___.

FINDINGS: 

The aorta and its major branch vessels are patent, with no evidence of
stenosis, occlusion, dissection, or aneurysmal formation.  There is no
evidence of penetrating atherosclerotic ulcer or aortic arch atheroma present.
There is a background of moderate atherosclerosis.

The pulmonary arteries are well opacified to the subsegmental level, with no
evidence of filling defect within the main, right, left, lobar, segmental or
subsegmental pulmonary arteries.  The main and right pulmonary arteries are
normal in caliber, and there is no evidence of right heart strain.

There is no supraclavicular, axillary, mediastinal, or hilar lymphadenopathy. 
The thyroid gland appears unremarkable.

There is no evidence of pericardial effusion.  There is no pleural effusion.

There is bilateral basal subsegmental atelectasis.  There is a 3 mm nodular
opacity in the right middle lobe (5:80), which may represent than
intraparenchymal lymph node. The airways are patent to the subsegmental level.

Limited images of the upper abdomen are unremarkable.  Small volume fluid
within the esophagus may reflect reflux.

No lytic or blastic osseous lesion suspicious for malignancy is identified. 
There is a right total shoulder arthroplasty.  Metal rod seen in the imaged
portion of the left humerus from left total shoulder arthroplasty.  Multilevel
degenerative changes within the spine.

IMPRESSION: 

1. No evidence of pulmonary embolism or aortic abnormality.  Mild bibasal
subsegmental atelectasis.
2. Small 3 mm nodular opacity within the right middle lobe.

RECOMMENDATION(S):
The ___ society pulmonary nodule recommendations
are intended as guidelines for follow-up and management of newly incidentally
detected pulmonary nodules smaller than 8 mm, in patients ___ years of age or
older. Low risk patients have minimal or absent history of smoking or other
known risk factors for primary lung neoplasm. High risk patients have a
history of smoking or other known risk factors for primary lung neoplasm.

In the case of nodule size <= 4 mm: No follow-up needed in low-risk patients.
For high risk patients, recommend follow-up at 12 months and if no change, no
further imaging needed.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ woman with right-sided pleuritic chest pain, after
recent long travel, to rule out pulmonary embolism.

COMPARISON:  Chest radiograph ___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT helical images were acquired through the chest before and
after administration of 100 cc of Optiray intravenous contrast using the chest
CTA protocol.  Sagittal, coronal and oblique reformats were generated and
reviewed.

FINDINGS:  The pulmonary arteries are well opacified to the subsegmental
levels, without evidence of acute pulmonary embolism.  The thoracic aorta is
normal, without evidence of acute aortic pathology.  The lungs are clear,
without focal consolidation or pleural effusions.  Mild dependent atelectasis
is seen in both lower lobes.  There is a 3 mm right middle lobe subpleural
nodule (___).  No significant mediastinal, hilar or axillary
lymphadenopathy is seen.  There is minimal calcification of the aortic arch
and descending thoracic aorta, without aneurysmal dilation.  Minimal
calcification is seen within the mitral annulus.  There is no pericardial
effusion.  No abnormality is seen in the imaged upper abdomen. 

BONES AND SOFT TISSUES:  Again seen is a nondisplaced right posterolateral
sixth rib fracture, with surrounding soft tissue swelling, suggesting an acute
fracture.  Mild degenerative changes are seen in the thoracic spine.  No bone
lesions suspicious for infection or malignancy are detected.

IMPRESSION:
1.  No acute pulmonary embolism or thoracic aortic pathology.
2.  Acute right posterolateral sixth rib fracture.  No pneumothorax.
3.  3 mm right middle lobe subpleural nodule.  If no risk factors such as
history of smoking, no further follow-up needed.  Otherwise, 12 month
follow-up chest CT would be recommended.

Additional finding and recommendation under #3 was submitted to the ED QA
nurses on ___ at 12:45 AM.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ female with history of cough and smoking.  Recent
chest x-ray with right upper lobe opacity.

COMPARISON:  Chest radiograph ___ and ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Volumetric multidetector CT acquisition of the chest was performed
without intravenous or oral contrast.  Images are presented for display in the
axial plane as 1.25-mm and 5-mm collimation.  A series of multiplanar
reformations images were also submitted for review.

NON-CONTRAST CHEST CT:  There is no parenchymal opacity or other lesion to
correspond with the right upper lobe chest x-ray finding, which was likely due
to overlapping structures.  Small ground-glass nodules in a centrilobular
distribution involving the right upper lobe are consistent with respiratory
bronchiolitis.  There is no pleural or pericardial effusion.  The heart is
normal in size.

The thoracic aorta is normal in course and caliber.  The heart size is normal.
Mediastinal lymph nodes do not meet size criteria for pathologic enlargement. 
There is no hilar or axillary lymphadenopathy.  The airways are patent
bilaterally to the subsegmental level.

There are no bone lesions concerning for malignancy.  

This study is not tailored for subdiaphragmatic evaluation except to note
fatty infiltration of the liver.

IMPRESSION:
1.  No mass or lesion to correspond with the right upper lobe findings on
chest x-ray, which was likely due to overlapping structures.
2.  Centrilobular ground-glass nodules in the right upper lobe, consistent
with respiratory bronchiolitis in this patient with history of smoking.
3.  Fatty infiltration of the liver.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with severe RA, diffuse pulmonary infiltrates
with parainfluenza and concern for PCP as well as possible tracheoesophageal
fistula seen on prior CT chest, evaluate for tracheoesophageal fistula,
evaluate for change in parenchymal disease

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was performed without
intravenous contrast agent and reconstructed as contiguous 5 mm and 1.25 mm
thick axial, 2.5 mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 mm MIP axial images.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 5.4 s, 34.9 cm; CTDIvol = 5.9 mGy (Body) DLP = 201.4
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 201 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  Prior chest CT dated ___

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE:  The visualized thyroid is extensively
calcified, likely due to underlying calcified thyroid nodules. 
Supraclavicular and axillary lymph nodes are not enlarged.

MEDIASTINUM:  Mediastinal lymph nodes are not enlarged.

HILA:  Hilar lymph nodes are not enlarged.

HEART:  The heart is not enlarged and there is extensive coronary arterial
calcification.  There is no pericardial effusion.  Extensive aortic valvular
mitral valvular calcification is noted as well.

VESSELS:  Vascular configuration is conventional.  Aortic caliber is normal. 
The main, right, and left pulmonary arteries are enlarged suggesting
underlying pulmonary hypertension.  A left approach PICC terminates in the low
SVC.

PULMONARY PARENCHYMA:  Extensive pulmonary parenchymal abnormality is
persistent but slightly improved from the prior study.  Extensive interstitial
thickening with underlying ground-glass opacity is worst in the right upper
lobe.  The apex and medial aspect of the right upper lobe have cleared
somewhat compared with the prior study.  Otherwise extensive chronic appearing
changes are similar.

AIRWAYS:  The airways are patent to the subsegmental level bilaterally. The
previously seen area concerning for tracheoesophageal fistula is less apparent
on the present study, possibly due to differences in esophageal distension or
technical artifact related to motion on the prior study (02:15, previously). 
Material along the posterior aspect of the origin of the right mainstem
bronchus may represent mucous secretions or aspirated oral content (02:23).

PLEURA:  There is are moderate bilateral right greater than left pleural
effusions.

CHEST WALL AND BONES:  There is no worrisome lytic or sclerotic lesion. 
Multilevel degenerative changes are moderate to severe.

UPPER ABDOMEN:  This study is not tailored for evaluation of the abdomen.
Allowing for this, the partially visualized upper abdomen is notable for
partially imaged bilateral upper pole simple appearing renal cysts..

IMPRESSION: 

1.  Slight interval improvement in persistent extensive pulmonary parenchymal
abnormalities, worst in the right upper lobe.  Differential diagnosis
includes, as before, severe viral infection and acute interstitial pneumonia. 
Chronic aspiration is possible as well.
2.  Posterior defect in the trachea is not currently seen and likely
represented artifact on prior CT.  Confirmation of tracheoesophageal fistula
would require barium swallow study under fluoroscopy.

NOTIFICATION:  The findings were discussed with ___, M.D. by ___
___, M.D. on the telephone on ___ at 12:05 ___, 1 minutes after
discovery of the findings.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ with lingular and left lower lobe nodules.

COMPARISON:  Chest CT ___ and abdominal CT ___.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT-acquired axial images of the chest were obtained without
intravenous contrast.  5-mm, 1.25 mm, and coronal reformatted images were
reviewed.

NON-CONTRAST CT OF THE CHEST:  No interval change in appearance of a
noncalcified 4-mm lingular nodule (4:40).  The 2-mm left lower lobe nodule is
also stable in size and increasingly dense which may reflect developing
calcification (4:38).  No additional lung nodules are identified.  The central
airways are patent to the subsegmental level.  There is no pleural or
pericardial effusion.  The heart and great vessels are normal.  No
pathologically enlarged mediastinal, hilar, or axillary lymph nodes.

This exam is not tailored for subdiaphragmatic assessment.  An incompletely
imaged 9 mm right upper pole renal calculus appears unchanged.

There are no bone findings of malignancy.

IMPRESSION:
1.  No significant interval change in lingular and left lower lobe nodules
since ___, reflecting ___ years of stability.  In this low-risk
patient with no history of malignancy, no further followup is necessary.

2.  Persistent 8 mm right upper pole renal calculus, incompletely imaged
however grossly unchanged.

radiology_facts.report_text:
COMPUTED TOMOGRAPHY OF THE THORAX

INDICATION:  Status post allogenic bone marrow transplant, evaluation for
infection.

COMPARISON:  CT part of the PET-CT examination from ___.

FINDINGS:  No incidental thyroid findings.  No enlarged supraclavicular,
infraclavicular or axillary lymph nodes.  All visible axillary lymph nodes are
normal in size.  The anterior mediastinum is unremarkable.  In the middle
mediastinum several normal-sized lymph nodes are seen but no lymph nodes are
enlarged.  The ascending aorta is unremarkable, the descending aorta is also
normal.  Normal appearance of the pulmonary artery.  There are moderate
coronary calcifications but no substantial alveolar calcifications. 
Visibility of the intraventricular septum suggests the presence of anemia. 
Normal appearance of the heart.  Normal pericardial fat.  In the upper
abdomen, there is ascites in perihepatic and splenic location.  The left
adrenal shows a large adenoma, unchanged since the previous examination.

There is no evidence of osteolytic lesions.  A solitary lesion of reduced
spongiosa substance, surrounded by sclerotic rim is unchanged since the
previous examination.  No evidence of sternal abnormalities.  No rib lesions.

Mild bilateral pleural effusions, right more than left.  Subsequent areas of
atelectasis at the lung bases, also right more than left.  The opacities
appear atelectatic rather than infectious given their band-like morphology.  

In the right upper lobe, there is an approximately 2 cm big
not-perfectly-rounded solid structure (6, 82) that has direct contact to
multiple airways, a predominantly solid core and a minimal ground-glass halo. 
The lesion also shows areas of decreased surrounding lung attenuation,
potentially reflecting local fibrosis.  Although lesion could represent a
scar, the possibility of a potentially fungal infection must also be
considered.

A second smaller but morphologically similar lesion is located in the middle
lobe (6, 190).  

Irregularities of the airways suggest mild chronic airways disease.  No
evidence of endobronchial lesions.  The airways are patent.  No other
abnormalities in the lung parenchyma.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Right upper lobe and right middle lobe nodule.  The possibility of a
fungal infection should be considered.
2.  Bilateral areas of atelectasis and pleural effusions.
3.  Mild chronic airways disease.  No evidence of lymphadenopathy.

At the time of dictation and observation at 4:58 p.m., the referring
physician, ___, was notified on ___.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old male with DLBCL on R-CHOP and neutropenic fever//
evaluation of perihilar opacities

TECHNIQUE:  Multi detector helical scanning of the chest was reconstructed as
5 and 1.25 mm thick axial, 2.5 mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 mm MIP
axial images.  Contrast agent was not administered. All images were reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 5.3 s, 34.4 cm; CTDIvol = 6.0 mGy (Body) DLP = 203.2
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 203 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  No prior chest imaging is available.

FINDINGS: 

A large collection of abnormal soft tissue infiltrates the right upper lateral
chest wall starting in the sub muscular plane at the level of the first rib
and extends into the lower axilla and right lower lateral chest wall
inferiorly to the level of the sixth rib laterally.  At the thoracic inlet it
extends into the right supraclavicular station.  Numerous left supraclavicular
and left upper axillary nodes are mildly enlarged.

The main to the chest wall is unremarkable.  This study is not designed for
subdiaphragmatic evaluation but shows ascites.  Small bilateral pleural
effusions layering dependently are not hemorrhagic.  Minimal pericardial
effusion is physiologic.

A right transjugular central venous infusion port catheter ends in the right
atrium.

Lungs and airways:

A large densely consolidative mass like soft tissue abnormality in the
anterior segment of the right upper lobe marginating the prevascular
mediastinum, and anterior costal pleura to the level of the sternal angle
contains central low-attenuation material, 28 ___, suggesting necrosis. 
Bronchial architecture is entirely subsumes medially but there are preserved
bronchi at the lateral margin of lesion which is quite irregular and has a
ground-glass halo and severely thickened septal architecture.

It is difficult to say where this lesion arose without looking at pre
treatment imaging, in attempt to determine whether it is a lung or pleural
lesion.  Less likely it started in mediastinum.

Aside from to subcentimeter left upper lobe nodules, left upper lung is clear.

In both lungs there is thick subpleural atelectasis in the dependent lower
lobes.

Thoracic lymph nodes:

Right upper lobe bronchus is distorted and angulated as it deploys around
adenopathy in the right hilus and right lower paratracheal station toward the
large mass.

Cm size lymph nodes in the lower paratracheal, subcarinal, and paraesophageal
mediastinal stations are not necessarily enlarged.

Chest cage:

 Although there are no bone lesions in the imaged chest cage suspicious for
malignancy or infection, it should be noted that radionuclide bone and FDG PET
scanning are more sensitive in detecting early osseous pathology than chest CT
scanning.

IMPRESSION: 

Large, partially necrotic mass like lesion, anterior segment right upper lobe
has features which suggest treated primary tumorand needs to be compared with
pretreatment imaging to assess the real change.  If this is not the primary
lymphoma, or the lymphoma involuted substantially, then the lung lesion is a
necrotizing pneumonia.

Right hilar and right lower paratracheal lymph nodes are enlarged.  Several
other mediastinal lymph nodes are top-normal size.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  Trauma torso

INDICATION:  ___ with MVC, polytrauma // trauma

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT axial images were acquired through the chest, abdomen and
pelvis following intravenous contrast administration with split bolus
technique.
Oral contrast was not administered.
Coronal and sagittal reformations were performed and reviewed on PACS.

DOSE:  Total DLP (Body) = 1,497 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  None.

FINDINGS: 

CHEST:

HEART AND VASCULATURE: The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber without
evidence of acute injury.  The heart, pericardium, and great vessels are
within normal limits. No pericardial effusion is seen.

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM: No axillary, mediastinal, or hilar
lymphadenopathy is present.  No mediastinal mass.

PLEURAL SPACES: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS: There is right upper, and right lower lobe atelectasis.  Lungs
are clear without masses or areas of parenchymal opacification.  The airways
are patent to the level of the segmental bronchi bilaterally.

BASE OF NECK: The thyroid is multinodular with a dominant 1.1 cm right thyroid
nodule visualized.

ABDOMEN:

HEPATOBILIARY: The liver demonstrates homogenous attenuation throughout. 
There is no evidence of focal lesion or laceration.  There is no evidence of
intrahepatic or extrahepatic biliary dilatation.  The gallbladder is within
normal limits.

PANCREAS: The pancreas has normal attenuation throughout, without evidence of
focal lesions or pancreatic ductal dilatation.  There is no peripancreatic
stranding.

SPLEEN: The spleen shows normal size and attenuation throughout, without
evidence of focal lesion or laceration.

ADRENALS: The right and left adrenal glands are normal in size and shape.

URINARY: The kidneys are of normal and symmetric size with normal nephrogram. 
Multiple bilateral cortical renal hypodensities are visualized most of which
are too small to characterize though there is a 1.5 cm right upper pole renal
cyst and a 1.3 cm left lower pole renal cysts.  There is no evidence of
hydronephrosis.  There is no perinephric abnormality.

GASTROINTESTINAL: The stomach is unremarkable.  Small bowel loops demonstrate
normal caliber, wall thickness, and enhancement throughout.  The colon and
rectum are within normal limits.  The appendix is normal.  There is no
evidence of mesenteric injury.

There is no free fluid or free air in the abdomen.

PELVIS:

The urinary bladder and distal ureters are unremarkable.  There is no free
fluid in the pelvis.

REPRODUCTIVE ORGANS: The uterus is of normal size and enhancement.  There is a
2.9 x 2.0 cm right adnexal cyst.

LYMPH NODES: There is no retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy.  There
is no pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

VASCULAR: There is no abdominal aortic aneurysm or retroperitoneal hematoma. 
Mild atherosclerotic disease is noted.

BONES/SOFT TISSUE: There is a burst fracture of T4 with anterior displacement
of the fracture fragments and surrounding prevertebral hematoma extending
superiorly to T1 inferiorly to T6.  There is no bony canal narrowing
identified from this injury.  Multiple spinous process fractures are
visualized from the level of T1 through T4.  There is extension of the spinous
process fractures to the bilateral inferior articular facets at T3 and T4. 
There is also small fracture at the base of an osteophyte at the right
superolateral endplate (103:81).  Suspected right first rib fracture
posteriorly (103:71).  No suspicious osseous abnormality.

The abdominal and pelvic wall is within normal limits.

IMPRESSION:


1. Burst fracture of T4 with anterior displacement of the fracture fragments
and surrounding prevertebral hematoma, as described above, with no significant
bony spinal canal narrowing identified.
2. T1 through T4 spinous process fractures with the T3 and T4 fractures
extending to the inferior articular facets bilaterally.  Suspected
nondisplaced right first posterior rib fracture.  Fracture at the base of an
endplate osteophyte at the right superolateral aspect of the T5 vertebral
body.
3. No acute solid organ injury identified within the chest, abdomen, or
pelvis.
4. A 2.9 cm right adnexal cyst can be further assessed by nonurgent pelvic
ultrasound when clinically feasible.

radiology_facts.report_text:
HISTORY:  Shortness of breath and DVT in the right side.

TECHNIQUE:  CTA chest without and with IV contrast with 100 cc of Optiray
administrated.  Coronal and sagittal reformatted images are provided from a
post-contrast series.

COMPARISON:  CT abdomen ___ and PET CT ___.

FINDINGS:  The airways are patent up to subsegmental level.  There is a small
left pleural effusion.  There are bibasilar atelectasis at the lung bases. 
There is a focal area of opacity at the right mid lung, 2:42, could be focal
atelectasis.  There is no pneumothorax.

There are calcifications in the coronary arteries.  There are no
pathologically enlarged lymph nodes in the mediastinum, hilum, or axilla
according to CT size criteria.

There are bilateral filling defects in the pulmonary artery starting from
branching of the bilateral right and left main branch into segmental and
subsegmental levels, involving right middle and lower lobes and all left
pulmonary lobes.  There is increased size of the right ventricle, concerning
for right heart strain. There is no pericardial effusion.

The pateint is status post partial esophagectomy (patient has history of
esophageal cancer).  There is retained material in the lower neoesophagus
which appears mildly distended. 

There is a hypodensity in the liver, 4:78, presumably a cyst seen on prior. 
However, the lesion seen on prior CTA abdomen in segment VI is not
demonstrated on current study, probably due to bolus phase. There are multiple
soft tissue nodules in the left upper abdominal quadrant.  The patient is
status post splenectomy.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES:  No suspicious lytic or sclerotic lesion is seen.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Bilateral pulmonary emboli as above.  Increased size of right heart cavity
concerning for right heart strain.  Recommend correlation with echocardiogram
2.  Small right pleural effusion and adjacent atelectasis.  Focal opacity at
the right lower lobe likely focal atelectasis vs early infarct.  Additional
areas of atelectasis.
3.  Status post partial esophagectomy (patient has history of esophageal
cancer).  Retained material in the lower neoesophagus which is mildly
distended.
4.  Hypodensity in the liver, similar to prior, likely cysts.  Please note
lesion seen on prior in segment VI of the liver is not seen on current study,
probably due to phase of post-contrast imaging.  As recommended on prior CT,
further evaluation with MRI with Eovist is recommended.
5.  Status post splenectomy with multiple soft tissue nodules in the left
upper abdominal quadrant, which could potentially be splenosis as suggested on
prior imaging.

Findings regarding PE and heart strain were discussed with Dr. ___
___ at 7:40 p.m. ___ by phone.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  History: ___ with chest pain  // eval for dissection, PE

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of 100 cc of Omnipaque intravenous
contrast.
Reformatted coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal
intensity projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:   Acquisition sequence:
   1) CT Localizer Radiograph
   2) CT Localizer Radiograph
   3) Stationary Acquisition 4.5 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 13.7 mGy (Body) DLP =
6.8 mGy-cm.
   4) Spiral Acquisition 3.8 s, 29.4 cm; CTDIvol = 9.4 mGy (Body) DLP = 274.8
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 282 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CT chest from ___

FINDINGS: 

The aorta and its major branch vessels are patent.  There is moderate
atherosclerotic calcification of the origin of the left subclavian artery at
the aortic arch (03:27).  The remainder of the arch vessels are widely patent.
There is no evidence of dissection or aneurysm.  No penetrating
atherosclerotic ulcer.

The pulmonary arteries are well opacified to the subsegmental level, with no
evidence of filling defect within the main, right, left, lobar, segmental or
subsegmental pulmonary arteries.  The main and right pulmonary arteries are
normal in caliber, and there is no evidence of right heart strain.

There is no supraclavicular, axillary, mediastinal, or hilar lymphadenopathy. 
The thyroid gland appears unremarkable.

There is no evidence of pericardial effusion.  There is no pleural effusion.

There is a chronic bronchovascular consolidation in the right upper and lower
lobe, with areas of central cavitation (03:36, 86).  There is also subpleural
scarring at the right apex and along the major fissure, with associated volume
loss of right upper lobe.  There are stable nodules in the left upper lobe
(3:101, 112, 116, 124).  There is no new focal consolidation to suggest
pneumonia.

There is persistence of the capacious esophagus throughout its course in the
thorax, with no identifiable mass at the gastroesophageal junction.  Limited
images of the upper abdomen are unremarkable.

No lytic or blastic osseous lesion suspicious for malignancy is identified.

IMPRESSION:


1. No evidence of pulmonary embolism or acute aortic abnormality.
2. Chronic consolidation in the right upper and lower lobes, with central
cavitation, and associated volume loss.  Multiple scattered pulmonary nodules,
unchanged since ___.
3. No evidence of new pneumonia or pleural effusion.
4. Persistent esophageal distention, unchanged since ___.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST WITHOUT CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ with  fall from standing, rib pain, headstrike// ? ICH, ? Fx

TECHNIQUE:  Contiguous axial images were obtained through the chest without
intravenous contrast.  Coronal and sagittal reformats were obtained.

COMPARISON:  Radiograph from ___

FINDINGS: 

HEART AND VASCULATURE:  The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber.  Aortic,
mitral annulus, and coronary calcifications are seen.  The heart, pericardium,
and great vessels are within normal limits based on an unenhanced scan. No
pericardial effusion is seen.

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM: No axillary or mediastinal lymphadenopathy is
present.  No mediastinal mass or hematoma.

PLEURAL SPACES: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS: There are multiple bilateral scattered 2-3 mm ground-glass
nodules.  For reference there is a right upper lobe nodule (04:56) and a left
lower lobe nodule (04:59).  There is scattered areas of atelectasis
bilaterally.  The airways are patent to the level of the segmental bronchi
bilaterally.

BASE OF NECK: Visualized portions of the base of the neck show no abnormality.

ABDOMEN: There is a right liver lobe hypodensity, likely compatible with
hepatic cysts versus hamartoma.  Otherwise the included portion of the
unenhanced upper abdomen is unremarkable.

BONES: No acute fracture or suspicious osseous abnormality is seen. 
Degenerative changes are seen in bilateral shoulders.  There are old healed
right rib fractures.  There is a T2 superior endplate compression deformity,
unchanged compared to prior study.  There is a T3 superior endplate
compression deformity not previously imaged though no findings are present to
suggest acuity.

IMPRESSION:


1. No acute fracture or traumatic malalignment.
2. Multiple scattered bilateral 2-3 mm ground-glass pulmonary nodules as
detailed above.

RECOMMENDATION(S):  Based upon ___ ___ society guidelines multiple
pure ground-glass nodules less than 6 mm are usually benign but follow up
should be considered in high risk patients at 2 and ___ years.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with cough, SOB// eval for interstitial disease

TECHNIQUE:  Multi detector helical scanning of the chest was reconstructed as
5 and 1.25 mm thick axial, 2.5 mm thick coronal and parasagittal, and 8 mm MIP
axial images.  Contrast agent was not administered. All images were reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 4.6 s, 34.5 cm; CTDIvol = 18.4 mGy (Body) DLP = 634.6
mGy-cm.
   2) Spiral Acquisition 1.2 s, 7.8 cm; CTDIvol = 18.6 mGy (Body) DLP = 145.3
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 780 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  Multiple prior chest x-rays.

FINDINGS: 

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE AND CHEST WALL:
The thyroid is unremarkable.  No enlarged lymph nodes in either axilla or
thoracic inlet.  No abnormalities on the chest wall mild atherosclerotic
calcifications in the head and neck arteries.

HEART AND VASCULATURE:
The heart is normal size and shape.  No pericardial effusion.  Mild
atherosclerotic calcifications in the coronary arteries, aorta and aortic
valve.  The pulmonary arteries and aorta are normal in caliber throughout.

MEDIASTINUM AND HILA:
The esophagus is unremarkable.  Small mediastinal lymph nodes, none
pathologically enlarged by CT size criteria.  No hilar lymphadenopathy.

PLEURA:
Bilateral large pleural effusions.  Small calcified pleural plaques are noted,
for example in the right lung base (4:192).

LUNGS:
The airways are patent to the subsegmental levels.  No bronchial wall
thickening, bronchiectasis or mucus plugging.  Partial compressive atelectasis
of both lower lobes.  Left paramediastinal consolidations with punctate
indwelling calcifications in the left upper lobe.  No suspicious large lung
nodules or masses.

CHEST CAGE:
Moderate dorsal spondylosis.  No acute fractures.  No suspicious lytic or
sclerotic lesions.

UPPER ABDOMEN:
The limited sections of the upper abdomen show several small calcified
gallstones.  Bilateral renal simple cysts measuring up to 6.5 cm.

IMPRESSION: 

Linear atelectasis in the left upper lobe.  Large bilateral pleural effusions
with subsequent compressive atelectasis of both lower lobes.
No evidence of interstitial lung disease.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CTA chest

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with a history of CML with elevated platelets
and SOB. Please evaluate for PE.

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.
Reformatted coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal
intensity projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Stationary Acquisition 4.0 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 19.3 mGy (Body) DLP =
9.6 mGy-cm.
   2) Spiral Acquisition 4.2 s, 33.3 cm; CTDIvol = 18.6 mGy (Body) DLP = 620.3
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 630 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  None

FINDINGS: 

The aorta and its major branch vessels are patent, with no evidence of
stenosis, occlusion, dissection, or aneurysmal formation.  There is no
evidence of penetrating atherosclerotic ulcer or aortic arch atheroma present.
There is mild atherosclerosis.  Incidentally noted prominent left superior
intercostal vein.

The pulmonary arteries are well opacified to the subsegmental level, with no
evidence of filling defect within the main, right, left, lobar, segmental or
subsegmental pulmonary arteries.  The main and right pulmonary arteries are
normal in caliber, and there is no evidence of right heart strain.

There is no supraclavicular, axillary, mediastinal, or hilar lymphadenopathy. 
There are multiple prominent, though nonenlarged, bilateral axillary,
bilateral supraclavicular, paraesophageal, cardiophrenic and mediastinal lymph
nodes.  The thyroid gland appears unremarkable.

Mild cardiomegaly.  There are coronary artery stents.  There is no evidence of
pericardial effusion.  There is no pleural effusion.
Respiratory motion limits evaluation of fine pulmonary nodules.  There is
centrilobular and paraseptal emphysema. The airways are patent to the
subsegmental level.

Limited images of the upper abdomen are unremarkable.

No lytic or blastic osseous lesion suspicious for malignancy is identified.

IMPRESSION: 

1.  No evidence of pulmonary embolism or aortic abnormality.
2.  There is centrilobular and paraseptal emphysema.  No evidence of
pneumonia.
3.  The are multiple prominent, though nonenlarged, bilateral axillary,
bilateral supraclavicular, paraesophageal, cardiophrenic and mediastinal lymph
nodes.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/O CONTRAST

INDICATION:  ___ year old man with aortic valve stenosis please evaluate
ascending aorta ___  // ascending aorta ___

TECHNIQUE:   Multidetector CT performed without the administration of contrast
of the entire volume of the thorax with multi planar reformations and MIP
reconstructions.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 7.2 s, 38.2 cm; CTDIvol = 7.3 mGy (Body) DLP = 282.1
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 282 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  No priors

FINDINGS: 

FINDINGS:

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: No thyroid lesions. No
supraclavicular or axillary adenopathy.  No gross breast lesions.

UPPER ABDOMEN: This study was not tailored to evaluate the subdiaphragmatic
organs.  No adrenal lesions.

MEDIASTINUM: Subcentimeter mediastinal and paraesophageal (2, 48) lymph nodes.

HILA: No hilar adenopathy.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: No cardiomegaly.  Normal cardiac configuration.  Trace
pericardial fluid.  Severe aortic valve calcification.  The LAD arises from
its normal position with moderate calcification at its origin.  The right
coronary artery appears to arise from its normal position.  Dilatation of the
ascending aorta measuring 40 mm in diameter.  No significant calcification of
the ascending aorta.  Moderate calcification of the aortic arch.
PLEURA: No pleural effusion.
LUNG:

-PARENCHYMA:  Biapical paraseptal emphysematous changes.  Mild, but diffuse
bronchial wall thickening with mild retained secretions.  No suspicious
pulmonary nodules.  No confluent airspace consolidation.  No diffuse lung
disease.
-AIRWAYS:  Patent to the subsegmental level.  Small tracheal diverticulum
relation to the distal trachea (2, 20).
-VESSELS:  The pulmonary arteries not dilated.
CHEST CAGE: Spondylotic changes of the thoracic spine.  No lytic/ destructive
bony lesions.

IMPRESSION: 

Severe aortic valve calcification with mild dilatation of the proximal
ascending aorta.

No significant calcification of the ascending aorta.

Biapical paraseptal emphysematous changes.  No suspicious pulmonary nodules or
masses.

Mild, diffuse bronchial wall thickening with mild retained secretions suggest
bronchial inflammation.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ female, status post trauma (pedestrian struck by
car).

COMPARISON:  None available at the time of dictation.  The patient is listed
as EU critical.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT imaging of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed
following the administration of 130 mL of Optiray intravenous contrast. 
Multiplanar reformats were prepared and reviewed.

CT CHEST WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST:

The lungs are clear.  There is no consolidation, and no effusion or
pneumothorax.  Minimal dependent atelectasis is noted, though there is no
evidence of pulmonary contusion.  The mediastinal structures are unremarkable.
There is no mediastinal hematoma or pericardial effusion.  The heart is normal
in size.  The aorta is normal in caliber, contour and configuration.  There is
no evidence of acute aortic injury.  Main pulmonary artery is unremarkable. 
There is no large central embolus.  There is no mediastinal or hilar
adenopathy.  The trachea and central airways are patent to the subsegmental
level.  There are no endobronchial lesions.  The esophagus is unremarkable.  A
small hiatus hernia is noted.

A small right thyroid nodule, measuring up to 1.5 cm, is noted, as seen on
concurrent CT of the C-spine.  

CT ABDOMEN WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST:

The liver enhances homogeneously.  There is no evidence of hepatic contusion
or laceration.  Hepatic vasculature is widely patent.  There is no biliary
ductal dilation.  The gallbladder appears to be surgically absent.  The spleen
is normal in size.  The pancreas, adrenal glands, and kidneys are
unremarkable.  There is symmetric renal parenchymal enhancement and contrast
excretion.  There is no fluid identified within the retroperitoneum to suggest
occult injury.

The stomach, duodenum, and intra-abdominal loops of small and large bowel are
normal in caliber and configuration.  There is no free fluid or free air. 
There is no evidence of mesenteric injury.  There is an anastomosis in the
right upper quadrant, reflecting prior right hemicolectomy.  There is no
mesenteric or retroperitoneal adenopathy identified.

CT PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST:

The rectum and sigmoid colon are notable only for diverticulosis.  There is no
evidence of acute diverticulitis.  There is no free pelvic fluid.  Distal
ureters and bladder are normal.  The uterus and adnexal structures are not
visualized.  There are no adnexal masses.  There is no pelvic or inguinal
adenopathy.

A midline scar is noted in the low anterior abdominal wall, with a small fluid
collection compatible with a postoperative seroma.

BONE WINDOWS: 

There is a nondisplaced fracture of the right scapula.  No further fractures
identified.  There are no suspicious lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions seen.

IMPRESSION:

1.  Right scapular fracture.  No further fractures are identified.
2.  No intrathoracic or intra-abdominal traumatic injury.
3.  Right thyroid nodule, which warrants non-emergent sonographic evaluation,
as reported on concurrent CT of the cervical spine.
4.  Diverticulosis, without evidence of acute diverticulitis.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CHEST CT

INDICATION:  ___ woman with history of Alzheimer's dimension
hypertension, now presenting with possible paraneoplastic encephalitis. 
Evaluate for evidence of malignancy.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector axial CT images of the chest were obtained as part
of a full torso examination, after the administration of intravenous contrast.
Coronal, sagittal, and axial MIP reformats were reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 4.8 s, 63.7 cm; CTDIvol = 12.5 mGy (Body) DLP = 795.9
mGy-cm.
   2) Stationary Acquisition 0.6 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 3.4 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.7
mGy-cm.
   3) Stationary Acquisition 3.6 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 20.1 mGy (Body) DLP =
10.1 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 808 mGy-cm.
** Note: This radiation dose report was copied from CLIP ___ (CT ABD AND
PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

COMPARISON:  None

FINDINGS: 

THYROID: The thyroid gland enhances homogeneously.

LYMPH NODES: There is no supraclavicular or axillary lymphadenopathy.  Note is
made of a rounded soft tissue density in the right lower paratracheal station
and measures 1.8 x 1.6 cm, likely representing enlarged lymph node (302:50). 
No other mediastinal or hilar adenopathy.

HEART AND GREAT VESSELS: The heart is normal in size, without a pericardial
effusion.  Normal caliber thoracic aorta with mild atherosclerotic
calcifications throughout the aortic arch and descending thoracic aorta.  Main
pulmonary artery is normal in caliber, without evidence of pulmonary embolism
to the distal segmental levels.

AIRWAYS AND LUNGS: The airways are patent to the segmental bronchi.  No
suspicious pulmonary parenchymal nodules or opacities are identified.  There
is no pleural effusion or pneumothorax.  There is prominent extrapleural fat
in between the right lateral fourth and fifth ribs (02:19).

BONES AND SOFT TISSUES: There is no acute fracture.  No suspicious lytic or
sclerotic lesions are identified.  A subcentimeter sclerotic focus in the
superior T6 vertebral body most likely represents a bone island (602: 46). 
The soft tissues are unremarkable.

Upper abdomen: Please refer to the separate dictation under clip# ___ for
details on subdiaphragmatic findings.

IMPRESSION:


1. Isolated enlargement of a right lower paratracheal node measuring 1.8 x 1.6
cm, for which continued follow-up is recommended with at least a repeat chest
CT in 3 months.
2. No suspicious pulmonary nodular opacities are identified.

RECOMMENDATION(S):  Short-term follow-up with a chest CT in 3 months is
recommended.

   2) Stationary Acquisition 0.6 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 3.4 mGy (Body) DLP = 1.7
mGy-cm.
   3) Stationary Acquisition 3.6 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 20.1 mGy (Body) DLP =
10.1 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 808 mGy-cm.

PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)
sections.findings:
THYROID: The thyroid gland enhances homogeneously.

LYMPH NODES: There is no supraclavicular or axillary lymphadenopathy.  Note is
made of a rounded soft tissue density in the right lower paratracheal station
and measures 1.8 x 1.6 cm, likely representing enlarged lymph node (302:50). 
No other mediastinal or hilar adenopathy.

HEART AND GREAT VESSELS: The heart is normal in size, without a pericardial
effusion.  Normal caliber thoracic aorta with mild atherosclerotic
calcifications throughout the aortic arch and descending thoracic aorta.  Main
pulmonary artery is normal in caliber, without evidence of pulmonary embolism
to the distal segmental levels.

AIRWAYS AND LUNGS: The airways are patent to the segmental bronchi.  No
suspicious pulmonary parenchymal nodules or opacities are identified.  There
is no pleural effusion or pneumothorax.  There is prominent extrapleural fat
in between the right lateral fourth and fifth ribs (02:19).

BONES AND SOFT TISSUES: There is no acute fracture.  No suspicious lytic or
sclerotic lesions are identified.  A subcentimeter sclerotic focus in the
superior T6 vertebral body most likely represents a bone island (602: 46). 
The soft tissues are unremarkable.

Upper abdomen: Please refer to the separate dictation under clip# ___ for
details on subdiaphragmatic findings.

1. Isolated enlargement of a right lower paratracheal node measuring 1.8 x 1.6
cm, for which continued follow-up is recommended with at least a repeat chest
CT in 3 months.
2. No suspicious pulmonary nodular opacities are identified.

radiology_facts.report_text:
CLINICAL HISTORY:  ___ female with progressive CLL.  Evaluate degree
of adenopathy and splenomegaly.

COMPARISON:  None.

TECHNIQUE:  Non-contrast MDCT acquired axial images of the chest, abdomen, and
pelvis from the thoracic inlet to the pubic symphysis.  Multiplanar
reformatted images were obtained.

CT OF THE CHEST WITHOUT INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST:  The lungs demonstrate apical
scarring and bronchiectasis in bilateral upper lobes and lingula. A
peribronchial consolidative opacity is seen in the right middle lobe which may
represent infectious process.  Small 2-mm to 5mm pulmonary nodules are seen
scattered throughout the lungs for which no prior comparison is available.
Coronary artery calcifications are noted.  No pleural or pericardial effusion
is detected. Prominent lymph nodes are seen within the axilla, mediastinum and
right hilum. The largeset lymph node is a conglomerate of soft tissue in the
subcarinal station measuring 31 x 16 mm. Incidental note is made of bilateral
thyroid nodules, one of which in the left lobe shows calcification.

CT OF THE ABDOMEN WITHOUT INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST:  Within the limitations of a
non-contrast exam, liver, adrenal glands and left kidney are within normal
limits. The pancreas is predominantly fatty replaced. The right kidney shows
multiple parapelvic cysts.

The spleen is enlarged and measures 16 cm. Multiple rounded soft tissue
strucutures are seen adjacent to the splenic hilum in the gastrosplenic
ligament which likely represents a conglomerate of enlarged lymph nodes, the
largest measuring approximately 14 mm. Retroperitoneal lymphadenpathy at the
celiac axis is identified as well. No free fluid or free air is identified.
Bowel loops maintain a normal caliber without evidence of obstruction. 

CT OF THE PELVIS WITHOUT INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST:  The rectum and bladder are
within normal limits.  The uterus contains a calcified fibroid.  The adnexa
are unremarkable. There is sigmoid diverticulosis without evidence of
diverticulitis. No lymphadenopathy or free fluid is detected.

BONE WINDOWS:  No suspicious lytic or sclerotic lesion is identified.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Peribronquial consolidation within the right middle lobe which likely
represents an infectious process.

2.  Multiple pulmonary nodules scattered throughout the lungs for which no
prior comparison is available. Given biapical scarring an multilobar
bronchiectasis, ___ month follow up is recommended to assess for stability and
evaluate resolution of right middle lobe consolidative process

3.  Splenomegaly and lymphadenopathy, which is in keeping with history of CLL.

4.  Sigmoid diverticulosis without evidence of diverticulitis.

5.  Bilateral thyroid nodules for which ultrasound can be performed for
further evalution.

6.  Right parapelvic renal cysts.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION: Evaluate for cause of diarrhea in a ___ year old male with CLL.

___.

TECHNIQUE: MDCT-acquired images were obtained through the abdomen and pelvis
after the uneventful administration of 130 cc of IV Optiray contrast.  Delayed
images were obtained through the chest. 

CT CHEST WITH CONTRAST:  Apical scarring with associated bronchiectasis in the
upper lobes is unchanged. Multiple 3 to 6 mm pulmonary nodules scattered
throughout both lungs that are unchanged. Bilateral small pleural effusions
are new.  A subcarinal lymph node conglomerate measures 5.1 x 2.7 cm
(previously 3.8 x 2 cm) There are multiple prominent lymph nodes in the
mediastinum the largest of which measures 1.3 cm in the anterior paratracheal
region. There are bilateral thyroid nodules which are grossly stable since
prior exam.

CT ABDOMEN WITH IV CONTRAST:  There is marked splenomegaly measuring up to 22
x 12 cm. The gallbladder is absent but no cholecystectomy clips are visible.
The common bile duct measures up to 1.1 cm with low insertion and dilation of
cystic duct to the papilla. There is equivocal intrahepatic bile duct
dilation. The liver, adrenals, right kidney are grossly unremarkable. The left
kidney is pushed inferiorly by the spleen. Perihapatic and perisplenic edema
is minimal. There is a conglomerate of lymph nodes around the periportal area
extending to the spleen that becomes confluent and extends inferiorly to the
level of the ___, encircling the infgerior and superior mesenteric artery as
well as the celiac trunk proximally without evidence of vascular invasion. At
the level of the ___, this conglomnerate measures 5.7 x 5.4 cm. A 1.2 cm
retrocrural node is noted. The intra- abdominal loops of large and small bowel
demonstrate no abdominal wall thickening, free fluid, pneumatosis or free air.

CT PELVIS WITH IV CONTRAST:  The rectum and bladder are unremarkable.  The
uterus contains a calcified fibroid, unchanged.  There is a small amount of
pelvic free fluid noted. Left common iliac nodes are enlarged to 1.1 cm.

Bone windows demonstrate no suspicious lytic or blastic lesions.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Massive splenomegaly and lymphadenopathy have increased, consistent with
worsening CLL.

2.  Stable appearance of pulmonary nodules scattered throughout the lungs with
unchanged biapical scarring and multilobar bronchiectasis.

3. There is low insertion of the cystic duct with dilation of the common bile
duct to 1.1 cm with mild central intrahepatic ductal dilation. There is no
pancreatic duct dilation. Recommend correlation with liver function tests and
if cholestatic picture, MRCP.

5.  New small bilateral pleural effusions.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  Bronchiectasis and worsening productive cough in a patient with
history of CLL.  Please evaluate for pulmonary pathology as well as reassess
the extent of known lymphadenopathy.

COMPARISON:  ___

TECHNIQUE:  Axial CT images are acquired through the abdomen without
intravenous contrast.  Thereafter, following the intravenous administration of
100 cc of intravenous Optiray contrast, images are reacquired through the
entire torso.  Coronal and sagittal reformatted images were also reviewed.

CT CHEST WITH CONTRAST:  Airways are patent to segmental levels bilaterally,
though note is made of extensive distal small airway plugging with a reference
plugged airway seen in the right lower lobe (3:37).  In addition, there are
numerous areas of ___ type opacity seen bilaterally, though worse in
the lower lobes.  A large amount of apical pleural scarring is redemonstrated
bilaterally, appearing unchanged.  Note is also made of more focal nodular
opacities, such as one in the right lower lobe which measures 5 x 3 mm (3:31),
though appears unchanged since ___.  An adjacent subpleural right lower lobe
nodule is 5 x 3 mm (3:31), again unchanged in total volume from previous
measurements of 4 x 4 mm.

There are small bilateral pleural effusions, and no pericardial effusion.  The
heart is notable for coronary arterial calcifications.  The great vessels are
normal.  Note is made of extensive mediastinal lymphadenopathy, with the
largest being a subcarinal node, which measures approximately 5.4 x 3.3 cm
(3:26), slightly larger since ___ (5.2 x 2.7 cm).

CT ABDOMEN WITH AND WITHOUT CONTRAST:  The kidneys enhance and excrete
contrast in a symmetric fashion, and the right kidney contains multiple
hypodensities which are too small to characterize, though likely cysts.  The
spleen is markedly enlarged, measuring 16.8 cm in length and contains numerous
focal hypodense lesions with the largest measuring approximately 1.5 x 1.2 cm
(3:50).  The liver is notable for intrahepatic biliary ductal dilation, the
extent of which appears similar to that seen in ___.  Also similar to that
time is extensive soft tissue density nodules which encompass the upper
abdominal aorta as well as the origins of the renal arteries, celiac trunk,
and superior mesenteric artery.  Overall, the extent of this adenopathy
appears similar to that seen previously.  There is no free gas in the upper
abdomen.

CT PELVIS WITH CONTRAST:  The urinary bladder and distal ureter are
unremarkable.  A punctate density near the bladder base (3:107) may represent
a urethral stone.  Note is made of a dual-looped pessary device.  The colon is
notable for diverticulosis.  There is no free gas in the pelvis.  There is no
pelvic sidewall or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

BONY FINDINGS:  There is no suspicious sclerotic or lytic lesion.

IMPRESSION:
1.  Multiple regions of airway plugging and ___ opacities, suggesting
endobronchial mucous plugging with possible endobronchial spread of infection.
2.  Extensive lymphadenopathy with a nodal conglomerate encompassing the
origin of the superior mesenteric artery and celiac trunk as well as a large
subcarinal node.  Those in the abdomen appear similar to that seen in ___ and
that in the subcarinal chain is slightly larger.

radiology_facts.report_text:
INDICATION:  ___ woman with questionable mitral valve vegetation,
preoperative evaluation prior to cardiac surgery.

COMPARISON:  CT torso, ___.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector CT imaging of the chest was obtained without
intravenous contrast.  Sagittal and coronal reformations were performed and
reviewed.

FINDINGS:  The heart is normal in size.  A small simple pericardial effusion
is present. Dense mitral annular calcifications are seen.  The mitral valve
vegetation is not seen in this non-contrast study. Mild aortic valvular
calcifications are seen.  The ascending thoracic aorta is normal.  Moderate
atherosclerotic calcifications are seen in the aortic arch and descending
thoracic aorta, without aneurysmal dilation.  The ascending thoracic aorta
measures 3.4 cm.  Moderately severe triple vessel coronary arterial
calcifications are seen.  The pulmonary arteries are unremarkable. Small
scattered mediastinal lymph nodes are not pathologically enlarged.  The
patient is status post thyroidectomy and radical right cervical dissection for
thyroid cancer, without evidence of recurrent thyroid mass.  

The major airways are patent to subsegmental levels bilaterally.  No
suspicious pulmonary nodule or mass is seen.  Linear subsegmental atelectasis
is seen in the right middle lobe (2:39).  There are no pleural effusions.  

This study is not tailored for subdiaphragmatic assessment, except to note
moderate abdominal aortic atherosclerosis and celiac stenosis. A 11mm
hypodense right hepatic lobe lesion (2:48), is consistent with a cyst.

BONES:  No bone lesions worrisome for infection or malignancy are detected.  
There is exaggerated thoracic kyphosis with mild degenerative changes.

IMPRESSION: Moderate aortic atherosclerotic calcifications predominant in the
arch and descending thoracic aorta.  Mild aortic valvular calcifications. 
Dense mitral annular calcifications.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT trauma torso

INDICATION:  ___ with MVC.  Evaluate fracture/injury to solid organ.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT axial images were acquired through the chest, abdomen and
pelvis following intravenous contrast administration with split bolus
technique.
Oral contrast was not administered.
Coronal and sagittal reformations were performed and reviewed on PACS.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 10.0 s, 79.1 cm; CTDIvol = 15.0 mGy (Body) DLP =
1,187.1 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 1,187 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  None.

FINDINGS: 

CHEST:

HEART AND VASCULATURE: The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber without
evidence of acute injury.  The heart, pericardium, and great vessels are
within normal limits. No pericardial effusion is seen.

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM: No axillary, mediastinal, or hilar
lymphadenopathy is present.  No mediastinal mass or hematoma.

PLEURAL SPACES: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS: There is mild bibasilar atelectasis.  Peripheral nodular
ground-glass opacities in ___ configuration may represent sequela of
aspiration in the left lower lobe (2:70).  The airways are patent to the level
of the segmental bronchi bilaterally.

BASE OF NECK: Visualized portions of the base of the neck show no abnormality.

ABDOMEN:

HEPATOBILIARY: The liver demonstrates homogenous attenuation throughout. 
There is no evidence of focal lesion or laceration.  There is no evidence of
intrahepatic or extrahepatic biliary dilatation.  The gallbladder is within
normal limits.

PANCREAS: The pancreas has normal attenuation throughout, without evidence of
focal lesions or pancreatic ductal dilatation.  There is no peripancreatic
stranding.

SPLEEN: The spleen shows normal size and attenuation throughout, without
evidence of focal lesion or laceration.

ADRENALS: The right and left adrenal glands are normal in size and shape.

URINARY: The kidneys are of normal and symmetric size with normal nephrogram. 
There is no evidence of focal renal lesions or hydronephrosis.  There is no
perinephric abnormality.

GASTROINTESTINAL: The stomach is dilated with material and air.  Small bowel
loops demonstrate normal caliber, wall thickness, and enhancement throughout. 
Large amount of stool is seen in the colon and rectum.  The appendix is normal
(2:183).  There is no evidence of mesenteric injury.

There is no free fluid or free air in the abdomen.

PELVIS:

The urinary bladder and distal ureters are unremarkable.  There is no free

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT torso with contrast

INDICATION:  History: ___ with mechanical fall and scattere ecchymosese over
chest and abdomen.  Evaluate for fractures or hemorrhage.

TECHNIQUE:  MDCT axial images were acquired through the chest, abdomen and
pelvis following intravenous contrast administration with split bolus
technique.
Oral contrast was not administered.
Coronal and sagittal reformations were performed and reviewed on PACS.

DOSE:
 Total DLP (Body) = 979 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  CT abdomen pelvis from ___ and CT chest from ___

FINDINGS: 

CHEST:

HEART AND VASCULATURE: The ascending aorta appears ectatic measuring up to 3.6
cm 3.6 cm in diameter.  Mild atherosclerotic calcification is noted.  The
heart is enlarged with right chamber enlargement.  Mitral valve replacement
noted.  No pericardial effusion.
 Left upper extremity catheter tip terminates in the distal SVC.  There is
dense coronary calcification.  Patient is status post mitral valve
replacement.  The right and left atria are mildly enlarged.  The main
pulmonary and right pulmonary artery are mildly enlarged, measuring up to 3.0
cm.  Patient status post CABG.

AXILLA, HILA, AND MEDIASTINUM: No axillary, mediastinal, or hilar
lymphadenopathy is present.  No mediastinal mass or hematoma.

PLEURAL SPACES: No pleural effusion or pneumothorax.

LUNGS/AIRWAYS: There is emphysema and mild fibrosis with scattered calcified
granulomas.  No concerning pulmonary nodule is seen.  There is evidence of
small airway disease with nodular opacities in the periphery of the lungs,
most notable in the right upper and left upper lobes.  The airways are patent
to the level of the segmental bronchi bilaterally.  However, there is mild
bronchiectasis, especially in the bilateral lower lobes.

BASE OF NECK: Visualized portions of the base of the neck show no abnormality.

Bones: Patient is status post median sternotomy with multiple intact cerclage
wires around the fractured sternum.  There is no significant bridging callus
formation in the manubrium as well as the remaining sternum.  Chronic
deformity of the left first rib at the costovertebral junction is unchanged
from prior exam.

There is bilateral gynecomastia.

ABDOMEN:

HEPATOBILIARY: The liver demonstrates mottled enhancement, consistent with
congested liver secondary to right-sided heart disease.  Focal
hyperenhancement at the edge of segment VI (2:114) likely corresponds with
previously demonstrated hemangioma on ___. There is no evidence of
intrahepatic or extrahepatic biliary dilatation.  The gallbladder is within
normal limits.

PANCREAS: The pancreas has normal attenuation throughout, without evidence of
focal lesions or pancreatic ductal dilatation.  There is no peripancreatic
stranding.

SPLEEN: The spleen shows normal size and attenuation throughout, without
evidence of laceration.  Subcentimeter hypodensity in the superior aspect of
the spleen is too small to characterize by CT (3:92).  There is a 1.5 cm
accessory spleen.

ADRENALS: The right and left adrenal glands are normal in size and shape.

URINARY: The kidneys are of normal and symmetric size with normal nephrogram. 
There is no evidence of focal renal lesions or hydronephrosis.  There is no
perinephric abnormality.

GASTROINTESTINAL: The stomach is unremarkable.  Small bowel loops demonstrate
normal caliber, wall thickness, and enhancement throughout.  Previously
demonstrated mass at the ileocecal valve is not seen.  There is no evidence
obstruction.  Diverticulosis of the sigmoid colon is noted, without evidence
of wall thickening and fat stranding.  The appendix is normal.  There is no
evidence of mesenteric injury.

There is no free fluid or free air in the abdomen.

PELVIS: The urinary bladder and distal ureters are unremarkable.  There is no
free fluid in the pelvis.

REPRODUCTIVE ORGANS: The prostate is heterogeneous and enlarged.

LYMPH NODES: There is no retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy.  There
is no pelvic or inguinal lymphadenopathy.

VASCULAR: There is no abdominal aortic aneurysm or retroperitoneal hematoma. 
Extensive atherosclerotic disease with noncalcified atheroma in the abdominal
aorta is noted.  There is new ulcerated plaque in the infrarenal aorta at the
level of the origin of the ___ (3:114).

BONES: There is no acute fracture.  No focal suspicious osseous abnormality. 
There are diffuse bilateral facet arthropathy at the lumbar spine. 
Compression fracture of L1 and L2 with minimal retropulsion is unchanged from
prior exam dated ___.  Cortical deformity at the costovertebral
junction of the right eleventh rib demonstrate some callus formation, likely
reflective of chronic changes.

SOFT TISSUES: The abdominal and pelvic wall is within normal limits.

IMPRESSION:


1. No acute sequelae of trauma.
2. Subacute and chronic fractures, detailed above.
3. Atheromatous disease of the abdominal aorta with new ulcerated plaque in
the abdominal aorta at the level of the ___.
4. Cardiomegaly with right chamber enlargement and evidence of hepatic
congestion.

SOFT TISSUES: The abdominal and pelvic wall is within normal limits.

1. No acute sequelae of trauma.
2. Subacute and chronic fractures, detailed above.
3. Atheromatous disease of the abdominal aorta with new ulcerated plaque in
the abdominal aorta at the level of the ___.
4. Cardiomegaly with right chamber enlargement and evidence of hepatic
congestion.
