radiology_facts.report_text:
CHEST CT ON ___ 

HISTORY:  ___ man with Hodgkin's lymphoma, ileocecal mass.  Assess
lymphoma in the chest.

TECHNIQUE:  Multidetector helical scanning of the chest was coordinated with
imaging of the abdomen and pelvis to be reported separately and the
intravenous infusion of 70 mL non-ionic iodinated contrast agent reconstructed
as contiguous 5- and 1.25-mm thick axial and 5-mm thick coronal and
parasagittal and 8 x 8 mm MIPs axial images, compared to prior CT scanning of
the chest on PET/CT scans since ___, most recently ___.

FINDINGS:

Central lymph nodes in the chest are numerous, and although not pathologically
enlarged, some have grown since ___, for example, in the right lower
paratracheal station, 4:74, at 10 mm, previously 4 mm, in the prevascular
station at the level of the aortopulmonic window, 4:100, now 7 mm, previously
less than 4 mm, in the subcarinal station, 2:38, now 15 mm, previously 5 mm. 
Tiny layering non-hemorrhagic pleural effusions are new and there is mild
pleural thickening in the posterior sulci bilaterally.  Previous borderline
left axillary adenopathy has resolved.  Mild peripheral interstitial pulmonary
abnormality characterized by septal thickening at the periphery of the lungs,
both in the apices and at the bases, particularly the right is difficult to
compare with previous status because earlier imaging was done at much lower
levels of resolution.  There was some abnormality at the lung bases as early
as ___, but findings suggest progression since that time.

There are no lung nodules due to malignancy.  A very small cluster of
bronchiolar nodules in the anterior segment of the left upper lobe, 4:110, is
inflammatory and not necessarily acute.  There is no consolidation or
pulmonary edema.

Findings below the diaphragm will be reported separately.  Moderately severe
L1 vertebral body compression fracture, with mild posterior protrusion of the
upper endplate, 602B:35, is unchanged since ___.

IMPRESSION:
1.  The only possible manifestation of intrathoracic lymphoma is an interval
increase in size since ___ of top normal size mediastinal lymph nodes. 
These nodes could also be a manifestation of an inflammatory reaction
associated with mild and probably progressive interstitial lung disease, which
has a pattern of usual interstitial pneumonia, but uncharacteristically slow
progression.  This could be a reflection of lung damage by chemotherapy,
depending upon clinical circumstances.  It does not look like infiltration by
lymphoma.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CTA of the chest

INDICATION:  ___ year old woman with syncope, elevated D- Dimer// Evidence of
PE?

TECHNIQUE:  Axial multidetector CT images were obtained through the thorax
after the uneventful administration of intravenous contrast.
Reformatted coronal, sagittal, thin slice axial images, and oblique maximal
intensity projection images were submitted to PACS and reviewed.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 2.5 s, 33.7 cm; CTDIvol = 10.9 mGy (Body) DLP = 366.9
mGy-cm.
   2) Stationary Acquisition 7.3 s, 0.5 cm; CTDIvol = 39.8 mGy (Body) DLP =
19.9 mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 387 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  ___ chest CTA

FINDINGS: 

The pulmonary arteries are well opacified to the subsegmental level, without
filling defect to suggest pulmonary embolism.  The main pulmonary artery is
top normal in caliber, measuring 3.3 cm, stable.  The thoracic aorta is normal
in caliber and there is no evidence of dissection.  There are mild scattered
atherosclerotic calcifications along the aortic arch. Heart is moderately
enlarged.  There is no pericardial effusion.

There is no axillary, mediastinal, or hilar lymphadenopathy.

The airways are patent to subsegmental level.  There is minimal dependent
atelectasis.  There is no consolidation.  No pulmonary edema.  There is no
suspicious pulmonary nodule.  No pleural effusion.

The thyroid gland is surgically absent.

Partially imaged upper abdomen is grossly unremarkable, noting a small
calcified granuloma in the spleen.

There are no suspicious osseous lesions or evidence of acute fracture.  There
are multilevel degenerative changes of the thoracic spine.  There is a mild
compression deformity of the T12 vertebral body with kyphoplasty changes.

IMPRESSION:


1. No evidence of pulmonary embolism or acute pulmonary parenchymal process.
2. Moderate cardiomegaly.

radiology_facts.report_text:
EXAMINATION:  CT CHEST W/CONTRAST

INDICATION:  History: ___ with upper back pain and swelling s/p MVC and cspine
surgery one week ago// concern for infection

TECHNIQUE:  MD CT axial images of the chest were obtained after administration
of intravenous contrast.  Multiplanar reformats, including coronal, sagittal,
axial maximum intensity projection images were obtained and reviewed on PACs.

DOSE:  Acquisition sequence:
   1) Spiral Acquisition 2.5 s, 39.2 cm; CTDIvol = 11.3 mGy (Body) DLP = 443.7
mGy-cm.
 Total DLP (Body) = 444 mGy-cm.

COMPARISON:  MRI from ___

FINDINGS:


NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: The imaged thyroid gland is
unremarkable.  Subtle prevertebral edema and subcutaneous fluid measuring 2.8
x 1.3 cm (2:7) in the thoracic inlet likely represents postsurgical changes
and residual prevertebral edema from prior injury.  Cervical fixation at C6-7
is better seen on the CT neck from the same day.  Overlying the spinous
processes of the lower cervical spine, extending to the level of T5 vertebral
body is a intermuscular fluid collection measuring at least 9.1 x 2.1 x 13.8
cm.  The most superior portion of the fluid collection has been excluded from
the field of view.  There is no definite rim enhancement associated this fluid
collection.

UPPER ABDOMEN: The imaged portion of the upper abdomen shows a accessory
spleen measuring 1.8 cm.  Otherwise, there is no acute abnormality.

MEDIASTINUM: There is no mediastinal lymphadenopathy by CT size criteria.  The
main pulmonary artery measures 2.8 cm.  The ascending and descending aorta are
not enlarged.

HILA: There is no hilar lymphadenopathy by CT size criteria.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: The heart size is within normal limits.  There is no
pericardial effusion.
PLEURA: There is trace pleural effusions bilaterally.
LUNG:

1. PARENCHYMA:  Ground-glass opacities and linear opacities in the lower lobes
presumably represent atelectasis.  There is no concerning pulmonary nodule the
require further follow-up.
2. AIRWAYS:  The airways are patent to the subsegmental levels.  There is mild
peribronchial wall thickening of the lower lobes.
3. VESSELS:  There is no mediastinal lymphadenopathy by CT size criteria.  The
main pulmonary artery measures 2.8 cm.  The ascending and descending aorta are
not enlarged.

NECK, THORACIC INLET, AXILLAE, CHEST WALL: The imaged thyroid gland is
unremarkable.  Subtle prevertebral edema and subcutaneous fluid measuring 2.8
x 1.3 cm (2:7) in the thoracic inlet likely represents postsurgical changes
and residual prevertebral edema from prior injury.  Cervical fixation at C6-7
is better seen on the CT neck from the same day.  Overlying the spinous
processes of the lower cervical spine, extending to the level of T5 vertebral
body is a intermuscular fluid collection measuring at least 9.1 x 2.1 x 13.8
cm.  The most superior portion of the fluid collection has been excluded from
the field of view.  There is no definite rim enhancement associated this fluid
collection.

UPPER ABDOMEN: The imaged portion of the upper abdomen shows a accessory
spleen measuring 1.8 cm.  Otherwise, there is no acute abnormality.

MEDIASTINUM: There is no mediastinal lymphadenopathy by CT size criteria.  The
main pulmonary artery measures 2.8 cm.  The ascending and descending aorta are
not enlarged.

HILA: There is no hilar lymphadenopathy by CT size criteria.

HEART and PERICARDIUM: The heart size is within normal limits.  There is no
pericardial effusion.
PLEURA: There is trace pleural effusions bilaterally.

require further follow-up.

peribronchial wall thickening of the lower lobes.
